claim: RAGC parity instrument (revision 2): (i) the shared-retriever cell shares retriever ALGORITHM, weights, configuration, and build procedure; each representation necessarily builds its own index over its own objects, hash-pinned per arm -- a same-index claim across different object sets is impossible and is not made. (ii) kappa parity is a paired equivalence test: parity holds iff a TOST/non-inferiority procedure shows |kappa_S - kappa_GR| within epsilon_par with the 90 percent CI inside the band; the revision-1 observed-difference rule without uncertainty is withdrawn; failure to PROVE equivalence, including underpowered, triggers the attribution downgrade -- no silent pass. (iii) P5b: on the same blind sample, per-source-class extraction and type-assignment precision of each arm's derived objects is audited; an incorrectly derived object pointing at the right provenance span is a precision failure, not coverage. (iv) P6 is renamed the oracle-delivery consumption probe: it measures generator task accuracy under oracle delivery and is a composite of derivation correctness, representation rendering, and generator consumption -- read jointly with P5b, never as a single-factor number. (v) after a parity failure the best-generic-system-on-this-evidence licence belongs to GR-B/native; GR-A retains only measured-comparator-under-the-shared-lens status.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 sections 2.3 and 3.1; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b technical errors; docs/next/arch/round1-critique-synthesis.md B.2.2 per-source-class precision requirement
rationale: Provenance-span survival without uncertainty or precision auditing lets a wrong-but-well-aimed derivation certify parity and lets sampling noise flip the attribution licence; paired equivalence CIs, per-source-class precision audits, and honest licence assignment close those holes.
notes: Supersedes the parity-instrument clause of ASM-0921.

claim: RAGC-CAL instrument gates (revision 2): V1 pins a NAMED harness -- proposed default FlashRAG, exact release tag + commit hash recorded at implementation start, plus a compatibility checklist (BM25 support; the pinned dense embedder; 135M-class checkpoint hosting; deterministic seeding); any failing checklist item routes to the registered fallback, a minimal in-repo retrieval+assembly harness, disclosed in the manifest -- a harness CLASS is not a pin. V2's gate is the single preregistered discrimination contrast LCB95(OR - R0) > 0 on a knowledge-intensive dev slice per vertical x rung; the revision-1 ordering invariant OR >= GR-A >= R0 is withdrawn as an instrument invariant (relevant retrieval can hurt generation; oracle delivery can underperform retrieved context through representation or context effects); orderings among OR/GR-A/R0 are reported with CIs as diagnostics only, never gated. V3 requires byte-identical BM25 indexes across two same-seed independent builds, dense-side reproduction at the LOGICAL-RECORD level (embedding vectors within a pinned numeric tolerance in the canonical dump) plus a pinned retrieval-equivalence threshold (top-k overlap on a probe query set); byte-identical ANN artifacts are not required.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 sections 3.3 and 9; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b technical errors
rationale: A gate built on an invalid ordering invariant or an impossible byte-identity requirement fails on healthy instruments and passes broken ones; a single-contrast LCB gate, a named harness pin with fallback, and logical-record determinism are testable on real toolchains.
notes: Supersedes the V1/V2/V3 clauses of ASM-0927; V4 unchanged, referencing ASM-0952.

claim: GR-C executable specification (revision 2): engine = SQLite, version-pinned, single pinned build -- the revision-1 SQLite-or-Datalog fork is CLOSED; reopening requires a registered amendment + manifest version bump. The generator never writes SQL: the only query surface is the closed tool-call protocol over the derivation-matrix tool-schema row -- lookup(entity, relation), neighbors(entity), check(claim_triple) returning true/false/unknown, path(a, b, max_depth) bounded reachability -- with pinned max tool calls per query, per-call timeout, total wall clock, error surface, and deterministic result ordering on canonical keys. Expressivity envelope: conjunctive queries plus bounded transitive closure via recursive CTE with pinned max depth and row cap; no negation beyond check()'s closed-world unknown. The manifest pins a kernel-op mapping table from every kernel-engine operation S exercises at that scope to its GR-C tool + SQL template, or an explicit NOT-EXPRESSIBLE row; an S run exercising an unmapped operation voids the S-vs-GR-C elimination reading at that scope, disclosed, fail-closed.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 section 5.3; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b assessment item 1
rationale: A design fork plus an unpinned query surface is not an executable control; closing the engine, the tool-call protocol, the expressivity envelope, and the operation mapping is what makes S-vs-GR-C a runnable contrast rather than a promise.
notes: Supersedes the GR-C clause of ASM-0924; absorbs the round-1 A.1 generic-Datalog attribution cell as the SQLite instantiation.

claim: GR-D acceptance-signal discipline (revision 2): exactly ONE generic acceptance signal per instantiation, named in the consuming prereg -- signal sweeps are tuning and are barred. The learned-verifier option is pinned: trained only on the construction split (disjoint from dev-eval and the frozen suite under the P3-D-INDEX split scheme), auto-labels only, calibration reported on a held-out construction fold (reliability curve + ECE), verifier bytes inside the total-system cap and training compute inside T_k, and a supervision-leakage rule -- the verifier never sees frozen-suite items, their gold labels, or S's kernel records. Tool-execution success is flagged the WEAKEST menu item: it can accept a syntactically valid but semantically irrelevant call, so it is licensed only where the executed check mechanically resolves the task's answer type, the prereg states that mapping, and every readout sentence names the signal in use. GR-D and S co-report acceptance and abstention rates (an accept-everything verifier trivially matches loop shape and must be visible).
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 section 5.2; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b assessment item 1 + technical errors
rationale: An unconstrained acceptance menu invites signal shopping, supervision leakage, and vacuous accept-everything verifiers; one named signal with split, calibration, and leakage rules keeps the de-confound itself confound-free.
notes: Supersedes the GR-D menu clause of ASM-0924.

claim: RAGC closed executable scope + compute (revision 2): the dev-scale run matrix per vertical x rung beyond FRONT/1's ledger is the CLOSED 11-config set {R0; GR-A.bm25; GR-A.dense; GR-C; GR-D wrapping GR-A.bm25 with its one named signal; GR-A.bm25+PS; GR-A.bm25+RD; OR x 4 representation renderings (the P6 consumption probe)}, with GR-D the only retry-multiplied config (<= x4, pinned at prereg). GR-B is built and measured inside FRONT/1's ledger. Any additional cell is a registered amendment + manifest version bump. Arithmetic: <= ~22M generated tokens, <= ~8.5 GPU-h per vertical x rung at 135M-class throughput; R-1 total beyond FRONT's ledger, both verticals, including derivations/indexes: <= ~20 GPU-h + CPU + one costed annotation batch (P5 and P5b share it) -- this supersedes the revision-1 3-to-5-config arithmetic under the no-silent-caps rule. R-2 scales ~3-4x; R-3 conditional on a secured allocation; the BM25-only graceful-degradation path is unchanged.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 section 10; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b assessment item 1
rationale: A control whose cost estimate covers a fraction of its own arm matrix will silently shed cells under budget pressure; enumerating the closed config set and re-deriving the envelope from it removes that failure mode.
notes: Supersedes the compute clause of ASM-0927.

claim: The first decisive RAGC experiment is PRE-SPECIFIED at design level (frozen at the consuming prereg; adjustable before freeze only by registered amendment): vertical = the a5/f2b pinned repository; primary contrast = paired accuracy difference Delta = acc(S) - acc(GR-D) on the frozen f2b eval slice, where S = the measured f2b configuration (135M + kernel-verify-retry) rebuilt under the control-arm manifest and GR-D = the same generator and loop shape with acceptance = GR-C check(claim_triple) execution success (the one named signal; weakest-item caveat disclosed). Decision rule under the programme section-2.5 machinery, FWER over the named family: KILL if TOST shows |Delta| within delta_eq = 0.05 absolute (90 percent CI inside the band) -- verbatim kill sentence: the audited f2b gain is reproduced by a matched generic store + generic acceptance signal in the same retry loop; no kernel-content attribution survives on this vertical. SURVIVE if LCB95(Delta) > delta_sup = 0.05. Otherwise INCONCLUSIVE-UNDERPOWERED, disclosed, no attribution sentence either way. Power: sized by P3-D-POWER to >= 0.90 for Delta = 0.10 (two-thirds of the measured +0.1507); if the frozen slice cannot support it, the item set is enlarged before freeze or the experiment does not freeze. Spend stop: worst-case <= ~4 GPU-h at R-1; exhaustion before the primary readout is a scientific stop with salvage, no retry. Scope licence carried verbatim on every sentence: formal-input, G2-class evidence only -- it de-confounds the formal-slice +0.1507 and licenses nothing about natural-input usefulness until the NLB gate passes.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/RAGC.md revision 2 sections 8.3-8.4; poc/gpt56-review/rev-dRAGC-20260711b ranked concern 3; registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json
rationale: Instrument gates test whether the control can be built, not whether generic RAG/tool use explains the one measured sign; the de-confound needs its own frozen contrast, margins, kill rule, power target, and spend stop to be a decisive experiment rather than a delegated intention.
notes: Secondary contrasts (GR-D - GR-A, S - GR-A, S - GR-C) reported in the same FWER family; consuming bead P3-E-RAGC-F2B or P3-E-VL-1.

claim: NTP-GATE interface operationalisation (design choice). The gate operationalises the two NTP-dependent interfaces as: (1) CONTINUATION SET := for a partial kot-query/1 query under the closed grammar in the pinned field order (relational ops: rel->direction->subject->qualifier; instance: entity->concept; define: subject), the per-field admissible-value sets at two grades - G1 = the licensed vocabulary for the next field (engine index read), G2 = the values for which SOME completion of the remaining fields yields status=answer (exists-completion semantics); (2) REPLAYABLE DERIVATION TRACE := an emitted certificate is CLOSED iff a store restricted to exactly the records it names (provenance world-record ids + license axiom files) reproduces the same answer value under the same query; refusals, which carry no record references, have no certificate by construction. A PASS/FAIL on any stronger trace notion (multi-step proof objects, search transitions) is out of scope until a consuming arm registers a stronger contract: this v0 engine is a depth-1 deterministic lookup oracle with a pre-registered validation order, so its complete derivation IS the validation-step-plus-consulted-records sequence.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NTP-GATE.md sections 2, 4; docs/next/lit/NTP.md section 8 (oracle/API gate); tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py (query validation order)
rationale: These are the weakest interfaces the dependent architectures actually consume: P3/H-RULE-CD masks need exactly the admissible-continuation sets (G2 is the mask; G1 is its cheap over-approximation); P6/H-GU proof-trace curricula and the verifier-loop's audit story need self-contained derivations, for which replay-closure is the mechanical test; H-VL retry needs named machine-readable diagnostics. Operationalising the gate at the consuming-arm interface (not at a theorem-prover trace ideal the engine was never specified to have) is what makes the gate decision-relevant and cheaply falsifiable.
notes: Fresh block ASM-0980-0999 opened for the NTP oracle/API gate (P3-E-NTP-GATE-0 precursor); 0983-0999 remain free. Registered by docs/next/design/NTP-GATE.md.

claim: NTP-GATE probe status and licensing boundary (design choice). The poc/ntp-gate/probe.py run of 2026-07-11 is a DESIGN-PHASE DIAGNOSTIC over the pinned l3a-eval/a5-eval sets and the unmodified engine: it licenses interface-existence/absence findings and raw cost context ONLY. It renders no experiment verdict, makes no performance, W1, index, coverage, or kernel-content claim, pins nothing, and is not a registered run. Before any arm budget treats the NTP.md section 8 oracle/API gate as CLEARED, the gate must be re-run as a registered experiment (P3-E-NTP-GATE-0 per round1-steering) with frozen corpus pins, AFTER the B1-B3 emission build lands, with the replay-closure acceptance test (ASM-0982) as its primary endpoint.
rationale: The steering consensus lists this gate among the ~$0 CPU items to run immediately; running it unregistered is acceptable exactly because the conclusions licensed are code-inspection-grade (an API exists or it does not; a certificate replays or it does not on a pinned input set), not statistical or comparative. Anything an arm would rely on quantitatively must go through the registered-run discipline like every other programme number.
notes: Registered by docs/next/design/NTP-GATE.md.

claim: NTP-GATE build spec and acceptance criteria (design choice). The emission surface the dependent arms need is completed by three additive, CPU-only, opt-in engine extensions, none of which may alter the default query() emission bytes (the l3a/a5 frozen instruments consume that surface): B1 TRACE EMISSION - query(q, trace=True) adds a trace field: the ordered validation-step sequence with, per step, the outcome and every consulted record reference (axiom constraint refs, world-record ids INCLUDING licensing-support class assertions, inverseOf canonicalisation flips, definitional provenance plus the mint-bridge entries consulted); B2 CONTINUATION API - continuations(partial) returns the ASM-0980 G1/G2 per-field sets, computed from the engine's indexes analytically, with the probe's brute-force adapter as the frozen reference semantics (equivalence tested); B3 STRUCTURED REFUSAL DIAGNOSTICS - refusals gain a machine-readable diagnostic block naming the terminating validation step and, for ERR_CONFLICT, stable references into the store-violation objects. ACCEPTANCE (mechanical, all required): (a) replay closure 100 percent - the probe's certificate-replay leg, re-run consuming trace-named record sets, yields outcome=exact for every covered answer in both pinned eval sets (baseline measured 464/600 and 438/855 on license+provenance alone); (b) adapter equivalence - B2 output equals the reference adapter's G1/G2 sets on all worked partials; (c) byte-identical default - both frozen instruments' full eval passes are byte-identical pre/post build; (d) determinism - doubled-pass identity on all new surfaces. Estimated cost: ~330-550 added engine/test lines, zero GPU, no schema version change to existing emissions.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/NTP-GATE.md section 5; poc/ntp-gate/probe.py (reference adapter + replay leg)
rationale: The probe measured exactly which records the current certificate omits (licensing-support class assertions; the class-licensing axiom behind instance-true's asserted license; all refusal context), so the build is a bounded engineering task, not research; making the probe's replay leg the acceptance test converts the NTP section 8 blocking requirement from an assertion into a mechanical gate.
notes: Registered by docs/next/design/NTP-GATE.md.

claim: P3-E-DECONF-0 contract and scope: the aligned-generic-store deconfound decomposes along the measured f2b mechanism into a RUNTIME channel (store acts only through the deterministic check() accept seam; owned by this experiment) and an AUTHORED-CONTENT channel (store content reaches the model through regenerated surfaces; owned by knull/knull-v2, untouched). Stage inventory: A1 aligned-generic-answer-key extensional-equivalence audit (CPU, ~USD 0, G1-class census); A2 generic-typed-store + generic-executor reproduction of the a5-llm engine leg (CPU, ~USD 0, GR-C precursor); B strong generic tool/RAG baseline through the identical verify-retry topology on the externally-adjudicated d-qa-t eval prefix (single small GPU run, G2-class attribution). No cell is a W1 claim; nothing is NLB-gated (no natural-language input exists in the design); DECONF amends no frozen record, verdict, audit, or ruling (f2b-replicate, f2b-transfer, knull, knull-v2 all stand byte-identical); DECONF cells are right-sized PRECURSORS of the RAGC manifest cells and are superseded by them for any G4/W1 use.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/DECONF.md sections 0 and 7; executes round1-steering recommended kill (b); relation map to MF0 section 8.5 cell (e) (ASM-0891), RAGC arms (ASM-0920/0924), FRONT rules (ASM-0853)
rationale: Both round-1 subjective analyses independently named the aligned-generic-store deconfound the highest-value early experiment against the programme's single biggest risk (attribution collapse); splitting it where the measured mechanism splits it makes the decisive half runnable now at ~USD 0 without waiting on the knull quality-gate rewrite, the human-annotation queue, or the RAGC/FRONT scaffolding the steering froze.
notes: Block ASM-0960-0979 assigned to this bead; 0967-0979 remain free. External GPT-5.6-class review precedes any prereg freeze.

claim: GS-A derivation rule: the aligned generic answer-key store is a MECHANICAL FIELD PROJECTION of the pinned kernel-v0/molecules-v0 records onto exactly the read-set of the pinned verifier's check() function - per covered concept the four-column row {concept_id, term_label(s), canonical_text, claims[]}, every byte copied verbatim from the kernel's own records, all NSM structure (explication trees, primes, vectors, types, provenance frames, engine hooks) deleted; derivation is one deterministic seed-free script, content-hashed; coverage and item alignment are identical to the kernel store by construction (same record_path keys). Because GS-A authors ZERO new prose, the maintainer language-quality gate on authored control stores (the knull plain-arm ruling, ASM-0700/ASM-0703) has no object here and is not touched; GS-A operationalises the structure-minimal member of the MF0 section 8.5 content-type cell (e) family, complementary to (never replacing) knull-v2's authored plain/nonce members.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/DECONF.md section 2.1; read-set established from poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py (KernelVerifier.check / shown_definition) at the f2b-replicate harness pin; prior field-inertness evidence: registry/experiments/knull.json map M-V (3456/3456 decisions bitwise identical after stripping non-gloss fields)
rationale: The deconfound's generic store must have the same coverage and alignment but none of the kernel's semantics; a byte-projection onto the checker's read-set achieves that with no authoring confound, no LLM, no human hours, and no quality-gate dependency - it is the cheapest store that any 'generic aligned answer-key' reading predicts is sufficient.
notes: Any store or checker version change (content-hash change) invalidates downstream A1 conclusions and forces an A1 re-run per ASM-0965.

claim: Stage A1 endpoint semantics: enumerate the FULL decision grid - three pinned corpora (d-qa 650, d-qa-r 1000, d-qa-t 360) x every admissible answer in each item's closed answer space x both verifier variants (true map; the recorded seed-pinned derangement applied identically to GS-A rows) - and compute the decision triple (extract_ok, decidable, consistent) under the pinned KernelVerifier and under the same checker class over GS-A. Primary statistic C_dec = concordant triples / grid size, an exhaustive COUNT with no sampling error and no CI. Decision rule: C_dec = 1.0 => KERNEL-RUNTIME-GENERIC - by pipeline determinism (store consulted only through check(); no store bytes in prompts; resampling seed-arithmetic store-independent) every F2-line endpoint past and future, membership-gold or external-gold, is invariant under kernel->GS-A substitution and the reproduction statistic R_repro = lift(GS-A)/lift(kernel) = 1.0 identically; the kernel-SPECIFIC runtime claim on the F2 line is dead at this scope. C_dec < 1.0 => every discordant triple is emitted and classified by check-path; a discordance on a trajectory reachable by any logged run raises an interpretation flag (frozen verdicts untouched) and Stage B adds the conditional kernel arm. The stage is registered although its outcome is expected (ASM-0966): it converts the f2b-replicate assessment's ANALYTIC circularity ceiling into a MEASURED, riding, re-checkable fact, per the two external reviews' demand that this deconfound be executed rather than argued.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/DECONF.md section 3; mechanism facts: poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py (build_prompt/answer_once/run_verify_retry/verify_answer) at the f2b-replicate harness pin; registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json does_not_license
rationale: The closed answer spaces make the reachable decision space finite and tiny, so exhaustive enumeration dominates transcript replay (it covers all trajectories, logged and future, at once) and dominates any GPU re-run (which could only sample the same deterministic space); a counting fact is also immune to the power and seed-noise objections that attach to every bootstrap endpoint.
notes: Grid size <= ~16k pairs; local box, nice -n 10, minutes. The invariance lemma's exact rider wording is fixed in DECONF.md section 6 row 1.

claim: Stage A2 (generic-executor cell, GR-C precursor): the a5 code-vertical typed world (no-LLM deterministically extracted records) is loaded into the RAGC-pinned generic deterministic engine (SQLite with a pinned, published recursive-CTE query library; ASM-0924 default honoured) and the pinned 977-query kot-query-code/1 slice is executed CPU-only. Endpoints: A_exec = exact-output agreement with the kot-axiom engine on the same queries; acc_generic = correctness against the pinned slice gold; per-query wall-clock co-reported descriptively (no cost claim tested). Decision rule: A_exec >= 0.99 with point-equal covered accuracy => ENGINE-GENERIC-AT-SCOPE - the a5-llm assessment's 'nothing distinguishes the kernel from ANY typed store + checker' ceiling is measured rather than argued, closing the missing conventional-substrate-arm objection; A_exec < 0.99 => the enumerated, classified residual is the first measured engine-distinctiveness datum and spawns its own follow-up. Anti-weak-control counter: the query library is built to the query-grammar spec, its full source is published with the readout, and any non-reproduced class is checked against the grammar before being credited to engine semantics.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/DECONF.md section 4; registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json + registry/assessments/a5-llm.json does_not_license (restated per feasibility-synthesis section 1); executor default per ASM-0924
rationale: The a5-llm PASS is the programme's flagship formal-substrate comparison and its own assessment names the missing control; supplying it costs a day of CPU-only engineering, and an under-built generic executor is the one way this cell could fake kernel distinctiveness - hence the publication + grammar-conformance requirement.
notes: Runs independently of A1/B; can fill local CPU time while B awaits freeze.

claim: Stage B design (strong-generic-baseline leg on external gold): arms at rung R1 (pinned SmolLM2-135M revision), 250-item externally-labelled d-qa-t eval prefix, 3 seeds, k=4 - R1-alone; GS-A-verify-retry (unchanged loop over the GS-A store); GR-A-lite = BM25 over the pinned common text serialisation of the same records, retrieved context injected via the harness context_docs path at one retrieved-token cap identical across arms (j floats to fill the cap); GR-D-lite = GR-A-lite wrapped in the SAME loop shape with the pinned GENERIC acceptance signal (gloss-self-check over the RETRIEVED text; acceptance never consults the aligned key); kernel-verify-retry added back ONLY if A1 found reachable discordance on d-qa-t. Scoring: every endpoint on the pinned blind external-adjudication labels (d-adj-t), same items/seeds/decoding/frames as the frozen f2b-transfer stage-2 design for labelled-diagnostic comparability; DECONF-B consumes no logged cell of any frozen record - all arms are fresh runs under its own prereg. Gates (inherited shapes): P10 extraction; RT-7a engagement on every retry arm; headroom acc_ext(R1-alone) <= 0.85; bridge gate lift_ext(GS-A-verify-retry) one-sided 95% BCa LB > +0.05 else INSTRUMENT-INVALID-at-B (nothing to attribute; A1/A2 stand). Primary: recovery_RAG = lift_ext(GR-D-lite)/lift_ext(GS-A-verify-retry), paired item-level BCa B=10000, seed pinned at freeze; point >= 0.70 => ATTRIBUTION-COLLAPSE-AT-SCOPE (K-P3v2(4) input at this scope); one-sided 95% UB < 0.30 AND Delta_align LB > 0 => ALIGNMENT-SPECIFIC; else PARTIAL, both bounds reported. Secondary: GR-A-lite prices retrieval-vs-loop; retrieval hit@j co-reported; per-type breakdown descriptive-only; every verdict sentence names its licensing arm. Right-sizing disclosed: no dense retriever, no PS/RD cells, no GR-B/GR-C arm, no R3 arm (f2b-transfer owns the R3 comparison), no PRM (HC3 closed at its size class). Budget: <= ~2 GPU-h, <= ~USD 15, Modal, one container, co-schedulable with the f2b-transfer stage-2 campaign; prereg-freeze + runner-role separation per the knull pattern; thresholds and caps are stipulated planning values, maintainer-adjustable at freeze.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/DECONF.md sections 5 and 8; parity mechanics adopted right-sized from ASM-0853/0923/0924/0925/0926; external-gold asset: registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json stage-1 gate + poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json (A=0.9784, LB 0.9606)
rationale: f2b-replicate never ran a generic-RAG arm, so alignment-vs-retrieval is the one axis of the deflationary hypothesis A1 cannot settle; measuring it on blind external gold with the identical loop shape, matched token budgets, and the 0.30/0.70 recovery conventions makes the readout commensurable with the existing f2b recovery statistics at the smallest spend that can decide it.
notes: BM25 mandatory-lexical-cell rationale per BEIR via RAG.md section 6.2 (arXiv:2104.08663, NeurIPS 2021); dense cell stays with the RAGC manifest.

claim: DECONF verdict + programme wiring: the verdict object carries THREE independent stage readings (A1, A2, B), each with a pre-registered licensed sentence, deflationary outcomes at equal prominence, and the kernel's remaining rescue paths named in the verdict itself (knull-v2 authored-content superiority; A-F0 authoring economics; any A1/A2 discordance residual) - none licensed by DECONF. On A1 C_dec = 1.0 the INVARIANCE LEMMA becomes a mandatory rider on every F2-line consuming claim ('the store's runtime role is an aligned deterministic answer key; all measured F2-line endpoints are invariant under replacement of the kernel store by its four-column projection') and F2-line headline language re-scopes to 'aligned deterministic answer-key + retry' pending the knull-v2 content channel; the lemma is scoped to the checker/store content-hash pair and any version change forces an A1 re-run before the rider may be re-attached. Stage B's contrasts are the right-sized first instance of the ASM-0926 contrast family feeding the K-P3v2(4) attribution-collapse kill AT THIS SCOPE only; the programme-level kill still requires the RAGC manifest cells at G4.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/DECONF.md sections 6 and 7; K-P3v2(4) wiring via ASM-0926; equal-prominence discipline per the knull verdict-enum precedent (registry/experiments/knull.json)
rationale: A deconfound whose deflationary outcome can be narrated away is worse than none; fixing the licensed sentences, the mandatory rider, the re-run-on-version-change rule, and the named rescue paths before any run makes the outcome mechanically consequential in both directions.
notes: The rider enforcement point is readout review, same as the standing coverage disclosure.

claim: Forward expectation (never a premise): Stage A1 will read C_dec = 1.0 - the checker's read-set appears confined to the four projected columns by code inspection and the knull M-V map already measured the vector/AST fields inert (3456/3456); the residual falsification room (term-for-definition path, claim-polarity membership, shuffled-map composition, unnoticed engine consultation) is real but narrow.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/DECONF.md section 3.2; poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py code paths; registry/experiments/knull.json map M-V
resolution_path: Resolved by the Stage A1 run itself (exhaustive enumeration, ~USD 0, CPU); either outcome converts this entry to a measured fact and the design's value does not depend on which.
notes: Registered so the design's expectation is a tracked object, not an implicit premise; no PREMISE/DECISION line in DECONF.md rests on it.

claim: P3-D-CODEVERT contract and licence space: CODEVERT/1 is the critique+synthesis of GPT-5.6's CASK-CODE/1 code-vertical selective-coprocessor proposal (poc/gpt56-review/arch-codevert-20260711/last-message.json). Its entire licence space is an ARCHITECTURE-FAMILY claim (calibrated contract front-end + evidence-gated ambiguity-set execution + exact deterministic substrate vs matched RAG/tool baselines at equal risk and resources, on the code vertical) - never a kernel-CONTENT claim in either direction: nothing in the v1 design consumes NSM semantics, explications, or the encoder (the a5 world is structural-not-NSM per ASM-0007), so kernel-content value remains owned by knull-v2 / A-F0 / the f2b-transfer line regardless of any CODEVERT outcome. The instance-of(symbol, concept) query family is retained with its own stratum as the single seam where kernel concept content could later enter; no G1-G5 rung claims it. Steering-freeze compliance: G4/G5 receive no further design spend until G2 reads out.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/CODEVERT.md sections 0, 5, 10; round1-steering.md convergence item 6; registry/assessments/a5-llm.json does_not_license; ASM-0007
rationale: Both round-1 subjective analyses named the code-vertical selective coprocessor the single most promising direction; the critique's load-bearing addition is that even a full win here is kernel-free, so the claim scope must be pinned before any experiment exists to prevent a later architecture win being narrated as kernel-content evidence.
notes: Block ASM-1000-1029 assigned to this bead, disjoint from all prior blocks (highest prior id ASM-0982); entries 1009-1029 remain free. External GPT-5.6-class review precedes any commit/prereg use.

claim: Front-end unification: CODEVERT's front-end IS NLB-FE/1 instantiated on the kot-query-code/1 IR - same repaired Tier-0, same 110-360M intent+slot Tier-1 under exact product-automaton grammar masks, same trie-constrained entity emission, same five-role data separation and freeze ordering, same executable resource ceilings and packed-artifact manifest (ASM-0946), same API-parser ineligibility. CASK-CODE/1's mandatory-typed-inverse rule (forcibly add the inverse of every directional contract) is REJECTED: it re-imports the NLB rev-1 defect GPT-5.6's own review forced out - it burns the single clarification turn on essentially every directional item including cue-unambiguous ones, inflates matched-resource accounting by construction, and its equal-denotation acceptance (typically both-empty) is a systematic agreement-laundering subclass. Replacement: the evidence-gated ambiguity-set semantics of ASM-0940(1)/(2) (orientation-cue inventory decides; both orientations only on absent/conflicting cues; answer only on denotation agreement, else CLARIFY), with systematic inversion caught by the both-orientation EVAL strata + near-zero per-stratum allowances (ASM-0941(3)) and the deterministic ROLE_DIR repair. Conformal/calibration guarantees are claimed only for the pinned phrasing distribution; the CAL-vs-EVAL source-shift delta is a measured secondary (ASM-0903(3) adopted).
backing_ref: docs/next/design/CODEVERT.md sections 2.4, 2.6, 4.2, 6; docs/next/design/NLB.md sections 3.3-3.4; registry/verdicts/a5-nl.json (ROLE_DIR mechanism)
rationale: Prevents duplicate parser/pipeline scaffolding on the same vertical (the steering's freeze) and removes the one component of CASK-CODE/1 that is known-rejected by the programme's own external-review record.

claim: CODEVERT product boundary and I/O contract (v1): the eight measured kot-query-code/1 families (callers-of, callees-of, imports-of, imported-by, contains, contained-in, where-defined, instance-of) plus - deferred to G5 - the two bounded edit contracts; no free-form generation, no free-form answer renderer. Output is exactly ANSWER(value, provenance) / PATCH(diff, certificate) / CLARIFY(2-3 deterministic template choices) / ABSTAIN(reason_code). Clarification is inside the measured system per the CASK section-5 protocol adopted verbatim: intended readings pre-annotated before systems run; scripted responses reveal only a choice among the arm's displayed readings; every arm including baselines gets the same one turn; malformed clarification scores as failure; turn/tokens/latency charged; an arm that fails to display the intended reading cannot be rescued. Clarification RATE is a gated instrument-validity co-report on every scored run (the clarification-rate pathology counter). Disclosures riding every readout: the BIO-span contract makes descriptive symbol references out-of-contract by construction; the 70/15/15 product distribution is a stipulated workload model, not a measured demand distribution; task gold is backward-authored from a closed grammar (oracle-conditioned at corpus construction, per the ASM-0945(1) discipline) and licenses no natural-traffic claim.
rationale: Adopts the strongest clarification-evaluation design in the programme record while naming and countering its one gameable metric (clarification rate) and pinning the disclosures that keep any later win honestly scoped.

claim: CODEVERT substrate rule: a deterministic conservative Python extractor (CPython ast + symtable + pinned import resolver) emitting four-state facts (proved/disproved/unknown/conflict) with byte-span provenance and per-rule certificates; dynamic dispatch, reflection, monkey-patching, wildcard-import ambiguity and unresolved aliases become unknown, never guessed; extractor precision and coverage are measured on blind repo-disjoint human annotation (AST-derived is never treated as automatically semantically correct); the substrate reuses the measured a5 code-world schema and engine desugaring layer. SQLite and pinned generic-Datalog serializations of exactly the same facts are produced alongside as attribution controls by construction. Scoring rule: results are always reported on the FULL pre-registered product distribution with blind extraction coverage kappa co-reported (covered slice separate); items whose answers depend on unknown-status extraction MUST be answered unknown/ABSTAIN and are creditable only under pre-annotated answerability labels - extraction incompleteness never silently shrinks any denominator.
rationale: Keeps CASK's correct anti-smuggle rules and hardens the one hole (unknown-dependent items) through which extraction incompleteness could inflate coverage.

claim: G1 real-repo extraction census (the cheapest falsifier, ~USD 0 CPU + minimal annotation): a repo-selection rule pinned BEFORE inspection (20-30 permissively-licensed Python repos in a pinned size band, disjoint from all front-end training/calibration material); run the extractor; report per-relation fact counts by status and kappa_q = the fraction of a mechanically-enumerated query census (all eight families over extracted symbols) whose answers are fully proved; blind two-annotator precision check on a ~150-fact sample. Kill floor (planning values, maintainer-adjustable at prereg): kappa_q < 0.5 on the pinned pool, or blind extractor precision < 0.95, kills the full-distribution product claim before any model is trained; the direction then survives only re-scoped as a covered-slice tool. G1 precedes all task authoring and all model work.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/CODEVERT.md section 7 G1; coverage-wall record: registry/verdicts/g8.json 0/1000 + registry/verdicts/m0b.json 0.3542 per ASM-0001
rationale: The programme's measured existential risk is coverage; CASK-CODE/1 authored evaluation material before measuring real-repo extraction coverage anywhere - the census is cheaper than the pilot and can kill the product claim outright, so it goes first.

claim: G2 merged mechanism pilot (<= 5 free-pool GPU-h + ~USD 25 API): ONE pilot absorbing NLB-0-B on the code vertical - synthetic-only training per ASM-0947(6), frozen pilot operating point, evaluated once on the legacy 855-query K=1 blind corpus (labelled diagnostic, outcomes public) plus ~300 fresh blind phrasings from two disjoint author sources - extended by three arms: evidence-gated ambiguity-set + scripted clarification; the aligned-SQLite tool arm over GS-C (the DECONF Stage-A2 artifact, ASM-0963, shared not rebuilt); a shuffled/deranged-store smoke variant. Kill set (adopting CASK section 8): no operating point with >= 0.80 completed-task coverage at point dangerous-error <= 1 percent; correct DSL absent from the emitted set on > 10 percent of answerable items; set-execution < +5 points completed coverage over top-1 at matched risk; systematic directional inversion in any supported stratum; aligned-SQLite arm within 2 points at the same risk and resource cap (early attribution-collapse signal at pilot scope). Epistemic status: a NON-DECISIVE screen under the ASM-0944 discipline - consumes no registered redesign cycle; pilot numbers are neither floors nor ceilings at G3/G4. Gate-bar ordering: G2 screens on the CASK bar; G3 = G-NLB (ASM-0901/0941) decides parser clearance; G4's binding win is the CASK coverage-at-risk condition set; no number from a lower rung is quoted against a higher rung's bar.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/CODEVERT.md sections 4.2, 7 G2; docs/next/design/NLB.md section 7.2; docs/next/design/DECONF.md section 2.2 (GS-C)
rationale: CASK's pilot duplicates ~80 percent of the already-designed NLB-0-B code pilot; merging preserves every kill CASK wanted while spending one pilot instead of two and reusing DECONF's generic-executor artifact.

claim: G4 matched end-to-end comparison commitments (prereg DEFERRED until G2 GO per the round-1 steering freeze): 20-30 held-out repos frozen before task authoring; ~1,200 base tasks x 3 independently-sourced phrasings; edit share deferred to G5 with its mass re-allocated to relational + ambiguity strata; arm ladder = CASK section 7 (R0, RAG-BM25, RAG-dense, TOOL-native, GENERIC-store, GENERIC-retry, CASK-top1, CASK-set) minus retry arms, PLUS a seed-pinned deranged/shuffled-store control behind the identical CASK stack; parity via the registered FRONT mechanics (ASM-0852 T_k arm-table meter; ASM-0853 matched-retrieval rules incl. shared/matched AND native retriever cells, token caps, index bytes charged); statistics via ASM-0941 verbatim (one-sided exact Clopper-Pearson on cluster-collapsed indicators for every dangerous-error and directional-stratum leg; cluster bootstrap for non-rare coverage with conservative CP co-report binding; enumerated Holm family; analytic integer boundaries pinned before any scored run); binding win = CASK conditions 1-3, 5, 6 so re-based; GENERIC-store within the pre-registered 2-point non-inferiority margin is the measured attribution-collapse input to K-P3v2(4) at this scope. Task gold is blind, repo-disjoint, and externally adjudicated (the f2b-transfer stage-1 insurance pattern).
backing_ref: docs/next/design/CODEVERT.md sections 3.4, 4, 7 G4; poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json + registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json (stage-1 endorsement PASS); docs/next/design/FRONT.md section 5; docs/next/design/NLB.md section 4
rationale: CASK's binding-win inventory is honestly falsifiable but under-specified on statistics, parity metering, and the misaligned-store control; registering the repairs now prevents them being negotiated after data exist, while deferring the full prereg honours the scaffolding freeze.

claim: CODEVERT edit path: v1's two edit contracts (rename(symbol_id, new_identifier), insert_import(module_id, imported_module)) execute SYMBOLICALLY - accepted contract -> deterministic applicability check -> deterministic transform (the proved reference closure names every rewrite span; the new text is the contract's own argument) -> mechanical verifier (parse, span authorization, symbol resolution, edit-specific postcondition, repo compile, pinned tests) -> PATCH + certificate; no neural drafting and no retries because nothing is sampled. Rename admissibility is fail-closed on analysis completeness: admissible ONLY when the symbol's full reference closure is proved with ZERO unknown-status references naming or plausibly naming it (dynamic attribute access, getattr strings, __all__ re-exports, shadowing -> ABSTAIN(analysis_incomplete)); a passing certificate certifies mechanical properties only, never NL-interpretation correctness. The neural span-draft + bounded verify-retry loop (structured violation traces, <= 2 retries at matched token budget) is DEFERRED to a later genuinely generative G5b edit contract, where CASK section 6's mechanics are adopted as written including the Pareto-dominance condition for any speculative-decoding replacement. G5 exists only if G4 survives.
rationale: CASK's verify-retry loop guards machinery its own v1 workload never exercises - both v1 edits are fully mechanical once a contract is accepted - and its applicability language omits the fail-closed-on-unknown rule without which Python rename cannot honestly carry a near-zero dangerous-error contract.

claim: Forward expectation (never a premise): real-repo extraction coverage kappa_q will clear the G1 floor (Python relational facts being a far friendlier extraction target than the measured g8/m0b coverage walls), and the NLB-FE/1 recipe with evidence-gated ambiguity sets will clear the G2 screen bars (0.80 completed coverage at <= 1 percent point dangerous error; +5 points set-over-top1; correct-DSL-in-set miss <= 10 percent).
rationale: Registers the direction's optimism where the register can watch it die: the closest published numbers (110M whole-frame 88.2 ATIS; TeCoD 83.6-89.2) sit at or below the interesting bars, and no cited work reports retention-at-S2-bound for a <= 360M parser on a comparable grammar.
resolution_path: Resolved directly by P3-E-CODEVERT-G1 (kappa_q census, ~USD 0) and P3-E-CODEVERT-G2 (merged pilot, <= 5 GPU-h + ~USD 25); no decision may premise on this entry while open.
notes: Non-load-bearing counterpart of the ASM-1004/1005 kill floors.

claim: A1 licence narrowing: A1 is an executable equivalence/regression certificate over an exact projection of kernel-authored answer strings — not a test of independently-generic content, not G1; C_dec = 1.0 reads as structural-field runtime-inertness at the pinned scope (never 'kernel content is generic'); C_dec < 1.0 triggers the four-step implementation-cause triage ladder (projection bug / incomplete read-set / init-order / schema) before any semantic-candidate label; the §1 invariance lemma is scoped to pinned harness/checker/output-space/init/store hashes; R-1/135M context only, no extrapolation to 100M–2B.
rationale: Answers the review that A1 was overstated as a falsifiable test of independently-generic content and mislabelled G1; narrows it to an equivalence/regression certificate with an implementation-cause triage and a hash-scoped invariance lemma.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0962

claim: GS-A keying + state contract: primary key = urn with record_path/record_sha256 pins; canonical_text/claims per the pinned _load rule; lowercased-label map built in the pinned index_labels traversal order with later-load-wins replicated and all collisions enumerated + published; A1 procedure extended with dual-initialization-order enumeration and logged-trajectory replay (pinned log hashes).
rationale: Fixes the review's schema/keying error (the checker keys by urn/record_path, not a four-column row) and pins duplicate-label/collision semantics plus init-order enumeration and logged-trajectory replay.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0961/0962

claim: GR-D-lite acceptance + retrieval contract: rev-A's gloss-self-check WITHDRAWN (oracle leak via it['urn']; not in the frozen menu); acceptance = RAGC menu item (i) self-consistency, m = 3 probes, ≥2/3 agreement, closed-space option-scoring parser, one signal, no sweeps, calibration + probe-cost co-reported (plain adoption of ASM-0924/0955, no amendment); retrieval query from model-visible question text only with a run-time leak check (ERR_QUERY_LEAK), pinned zero/one/multiple-hit behaviour, evidence-budget parity worded as allowed-budget-where-applicable.
rationale: Removes the ranked-#1 oracle leak (the gloss-self-check selecting the aligned gloss via it['urn']) by adopting a non-oracular self-consistency acceptance and a leak-checked model-visible retrieval query.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0964

claim: Stage-B statistics: primary = absolute paired Δ_align with RAGC §8.4 machinery — TOST equivalence at δ_eq = 0.05 (collapse input), LCB95 > δ_sup = 0.05 (alignment-specific), else inconclusive; power ≥ 0.90 at Δ = 0.10 sized before freeze (enlarge the adjudicated set or do not freeze); Holm FWER over the named contrast family; the recovery_RAG ratio demoted to a non-verdict-bearing descriptive diagnostic.
rationale: Replaces the too-weak point-only recovery_RAG ≥ 0.70 collapse rule with RAGC §8.4 paired Δ/TOST/LCB/power/FWER, resolving the ratified-statistics conflict.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0964/0965; replaces the 0.70/0.30 point-ratio rule

claim: KOT-FAIR slotting + NL wording correction: A1/A2 = pre-ladder CPU attribution diagnostics (not G1); Stage B = formal/oracle-addressed covered-slice G2 diagnostic (not W1/G4); BM25-only = cheap lexical precursor, never 'strong generic RAG baseline'; Stage B contains English and is NLB-exempt by evidence class, not by NL absence.
rationale: Corrects the mislabelled KOT-FAIR/2 slotting (A1/A2/B were called G1/W1/G4), the 'strong baseline' overclaim on BM25-only, and the NL-discipline wording at the two flagged spots.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0960/0964/0965

claim: Lifecycle-accounting contract: '$0' always qualified as '$0 marginal compute'; mandatory per-stage ledger — model/probe tokens + FLOPs, artifact bytes, peak RSS, latency distribution, BM25 build CPU, query-library + conformance-suite implementation effort (logged), store-derivation effort.
rationale: Answers the review that accounting was incomplete and '$0' was unqualified by mandating a per-stage lifecycle ledger.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0964

claim: A2 conformance-first rule + portability licence: the published grammar-conformance suite must pass before any engine-side reading; A_exec < 0.99 defaults to LIBRARY-INCOMPLETE, with ENGINE-DISTINCTIVE-RESIDUAL-CANDIDATE only for residual classes proven outside the grammar-conformant library's expressible space; A2's positive reading is conventional-runtime portability via the published library, never 'SQLite supplies equivalent semantics'.
rationale: Answers that A2's <0.99 ⇒ engine-distinctive branch is invalid without a conformance proof, and that A2 shows conventional-runtime portability, not SQLite-equivalent semantics.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0963

claim: Stage-B verdict rider: every B verdict sentence carries verbatim 'self-authored items, kernel-covered slice, oracle-addressed store; external adjudication removes membership-gold circularity, not item-generation or store-addressing circularity'.
rationale: Ensures external adjudication is not overclaimed: it breaks membership-gold circularity only, so every B verdict retains the self-authored/kernel-covered/oracle-addressed rider.
notes: registered from DECONF.md rev-B review-gate revision; amends ASM-0965

claim: Replay is CONSTRAINT-granular: axiom files sliced to cited constraint indexes; rev-1's 77.3%/51.2% closure is FILE-granular and an upper bound; constraint-granular closure TBD after B1.
rationale: Review technical error #1: 'exactly the records it names' must be true of the implementation, which strips #constraint and reloads whole axiom files.
notes: registered from NTP-GATE.md rev-2 review-gate revision; amends ASM-0980 (clause 2) and the ASM-0982 gate build

claim: Reject-trace dual contract: positive-witness (restricted replay, same code+step) vs snapshot-relative (digest-pinned full-snapshot replay, same code+step); every ERR_* code assigned to exactly one class.
rationale: Review technical error #2: reject traces had no replay semantics or acceptance endpoint, and restricted replay cannot prove absence.
notes: registered from NTP-GATE.md rev-2 review-gate revision; extends the ASM-0982 gate build/acceptance

claim: Trace dependency taxonomy: query-local witness records enumerated by stable id; build-time/global deps (indexes, CWA scan) referenced once via store snapshot digest + engine version.

claim: The PRIMARY size x medium interaction is computed at verifier OFF (the pure medium leg); the verifier-on interactions are named reported secondaries (M2+M4 composed). Holm across the two structured mediums; reject iff one-sided 95% BCa LB > 0 for >=1 medium.
backing_ref: analysis/casc_0.py (inter_off_* primary, inter_on_* secondary); casc-0.json endpoints[primary]
rationale: M2 asserts a representation effect; folding the verifier into the primary would let the already-measured M4 loop masquerade as M2 — the exact attribution error DECONF-A1 warns against.

claim: Co-primary practical-shrink test: acc(R2, medium, verifier on) non-inferior to acc(R3, nl) at margin 0.05 (DRAFT pin, maintainer-adjustable AT FREEZE only) AND measured cost (formula FLOPs incl. metered verifier CPU) strictly below the R3-nl cell's; Holm across the two mediums; CONDITIONAL on the separation gate (unevaluable, never a fail, when separation is invalid).
backing_ref: casc-0.json endpoints[coprimary-ni]; f2b-transfer verdict noninferiority_vs_r3 = FALSE (the predecessor read)
rationale: This is the honest successor to f2b-transfer's failed noninferiority secondary, now WITH the internal structured medium that experiment lacked — the maintainer's 'small model + aligned authored store' reading given its pre-registered second chance.

claim: TTC deflator protocol (the K2' instrument, replacing CASC-0's arithmetically impossible A2 contract): 360M-NL + self-consistency majority vote over N fresh full-protocol samples, N = clamp(round(ref_flops/nl_flops), 2, 9) computed from the SAME run's seed-0 measured cells; the cost-match REFERENCE is FIXED pre-registered as R2/gloss/verifier-on (the raw-kernel Option-A medium — never post-hoc 'best'); ties broken by pinned option order; /gates/ttc_match_valid requires relative flops gap <= 0.30 (DRAFT band; integer-N quantization bounds the achievable gap by ~0.5/N plus reference retry variance — 0.25 was measurably too tight in mock).
backing_ref: casc0-manifest.json design_constants.ttc; analysis/casc_0.py TTC_MATCH_BAND; cascade-synthesis section 3 (corrected cost-matched control)
rationale: Cost-matching at the SMALL scale is the only feasible deflator; an unmatched deflator would let purchased compute masquerade as a medium effect or vice versa.
notes: A 1.7B-NL+TTC reference MAY be co-reported at its own cost, never as a matched baseline.

claim: Kill wordings binding, adopted from cascade-synthesis section 3 verbatim: K1' (both primary interaction LCBs <= 0 AND co-primary NI fails => M2 dead at scope; H-CN SHELVED; V-DEC/V-LL do NOT unlock; cost goal reverts to the a-e2 trim bound); K2' (medium effect within the cost-matched deflator's reach => purchased compute; treated as K1'); K3' (gloss ~ plain TOST => kernel-content attribution dead at scope; NON-FATAL, reported-only; family continues GENERIC; kernel claim routes to knull-v2/A-F0). NULL requires TOST of both interactions within +/-0.05 of 0.
rationale: Pre-registered kills that close the family at scope are the condition under which the synthesis recommended running anything at all.

claim: Tuning symmetry decided at freeze as ALL CELLS STOCK (T_k = 0 on every side; ASM-0852 satisfied trivially); if dialect tuning is ever added, the run is re-labelled an informative screen in the prereg itself (the critique's closing rule, adopted verbatim). Consequence disclosed: this measures the static PROMPTED case only — 'naturalising the content FOR a smaller model' via tuning is out of scope.
backing_ref: casc0-manifest.json models.tuning; cascade-synthesis section 3 tuning clause; ASM-0852
rationale: Stock cells keep the probe inside the <=15 GPU-h envelope and remove the tuning-asymmetry confound entirely; the tuned case is a separate, gated follow-up.

claim: Statistics constants: paired item bootstrap preserving pairing, one shared index stream across all contrasts, B=10000, PRNG seed 20260711, BCa bounds (jackknife acceleration); Holm within each pre-declared 2-member family; TOST margins 0.05 (attribution) and 0.05 (M2-null); one-sided alpha 0.05; float64 end-to-end, no pre-rounding (P8 C-7).
rationale: Carried from the f2b-line P8 discipline; the shared index stream preserves cross-contrast pairing exactly.

claim: Deranged-closure house control: ONE verifier-on-topology cell (R2/gloss/verifier-on) re-run against a Sattolo cyclic derangement of the 3 atomic relation types (perm_seed 20260711; map + sha recorded in deranged-map.json and on every record); identical retry topology and cost, closure content decoupled from items; EXPECTED collapse of the verifier benefit; REPORTED-ONLY, never verdict-bearing; a NON-collapse is a red flag for answer-key leakage through the elicitation protocol and quarantines the verifier secondaries pending investigation.
backing_ref: casc0-manifest.json deranged_control; f2b-transfer shuffled-kernel arm (the pattern's provenance)
rationale: The house oracle-content control carried to the closure setting at minimum cost.

claim: Pin reuse, no new infrastructure: the Modal image is built from the SAME pinned poc/modal/requirements-image.txt bytes as the f2b/f2b-transfer image im-6uXR6RyVQV15h2B3gtpOG2 (no new dependency; digest compared at collection in provenance); the 1.7B model revision, kernel-v0 and molecules-v0 corpus hashes, and the encoder hash are pinned VERBATIM from the f2b-transfer frozen record; kernel-v0/molecules-v0 are anchors only (the runner does not read them — d-casc0 is self-contained).
rationale: Reuse keeps the harness inside an environment already smoked on the exact GPU classes and removes an image-drift axis from the audit surface.

claim: Budget contract: <= 15 GPU-h, <= $30, wall-clock <= 36 h, Tier-1 cap $80, Modal single GPU (A100-40GB or A10G); dry-plan (A100, 2026-07-11, planning constants): 4.58 h point, 9.16 h at 2.0x overhead, $19.24 worst case — inside every cap; engine/analysis legs CPU on the local box under standing nice/checkpoint discipline; ESTIMATES never emitted as measurements.
rationale: The synthesis prices CASC-0' at <=~15 GPU-h; the dry-plan confirms the factorial fits with margin.

claim: Rung naming: this record adds R2 = SmolLM2-360M-Instruct to the f2b-line R1/R3 naming, matching the programme's R-2 anchor (300-500M class); R1 (135M) is not used here — the shrink question is 360M-vs-1.7B per the synthesis factor table.
rationale: 360M is the smallest rung with plausible headroom on depth-2-4 composition; 135M would risk a floor-effect instrument failure.

claim: Instrument-gate set (never hypothesis outcomes): engagement (RT-7a structural over factorial verifier-on cells: decidable_fraction >= 0.70 with decidable = depth>=3 items = 0.75 of the prefix; attempt-0 rejection in [0.05, 0.95]; >= 1 final-differs); headroom acc(R2,nl) <= 0.85; separation (R3-R2 on nl >= 0.05 with LB > 0) gating ONLY the co-primary's family membership; ttc-match <= 0.30. The P10 extraction gate is NOT APPLICABLE: constrained key selection over pinned option sets makes extraction failure structurally impossible; fail-closed structural asserts (ERR_GOLD_SURFACE, ERR_ENGINE_GOLD, ERR_ORACLE_CHECK, ERR_EVAL_FLOOR, ERR_PIN) replace it.
rationale: Every known degeneracy of the f2b line (vacuous verifier, saturated baseline, unseparated sizes) is pre-registered as INSTRUMENT-INVALID so it can never read as evidence.

claim: Gold scope: item gold is ENGINE-DERIVED from the self-authored store by construction; NO external adjudication exists in this probe; therefore NO ground-truth-independence claim is made or licensed, and the SELF-AUTHORED / kernel-STYLE / engine-derived-gold rider rides EVERY verdict sentence. The ground-truth-independence residual remains the f2b-transfer line's (discharged there pending audit, at ITS scope).
rationale: This probe prices a representation effect where engine-derivable gold is the point (it is what makes the paired factorial affordable and exact); pretending otherwise would overclaim.

claim: Kernel-coverage mapping: the d-casc0 relation concepts (parent/child/sibling/grandparent/grandchild/pibling/nibling and gendered surfaces) are STIPULATED as kernel-STYLE-style content pending an explicator pre-freeze pass that (a) maps them to kernel/molecule records where they exist and (b) upgrades the gloss templates to explicator-grade Option-A authoring; if the mapping fails, the 'kernel-STYLE' clause of the rider is weakened to 'kernel-STYLE' in the frozen record — a wording change, not a design change; the rider rides regardless.
rationale: Kinship molecules are classic NSM territory, but stipulating coverage without the explicator's check would be exactly the self-flattery the house style forbids.
notes: EXECUTED 2026-07-11 (explicator): mapping FAILED - 0/7 core relation types kernel-record-backed in the relational sense (surfaces mother/father map to urn:molecule-v0:mother / urn:molecule-v0:father; molecule 'child' is the age sense); rider weakened to 'kernel-STYLE'; gloss templates upgraded; mock + dry-plan re-verified green.

claim: M2 (the reasoner-shrink bet, registered as ASM-1078): its RESOLVER is reassigned to casc-0's primary endpoint (the verifier-off size x medium interaction), then G3/G4 as before; the corrected register stands (M2 untested directly; f2b-transfer NI FALSE pending audit and nsk1 net-harm are adverse neighbours; DECONF-A1 deflates kernel-specific attribution). The e2e composition with the F front-end (acc ~ ret_F x core x fid_R) is published only as a product of tagged quantities, never as a number of its own. NEVER a premise of this or any design.
rationale: Extrapolations get resolvers, not weight; this record is the resolver.
resolution_path: casc-0's primary endpoint (the verifier-off size x medium interaction, Holm-2 paired BCa) is the direct measurement that resolves the M2 reasoner-shrink bet, then G3/G4 as the register states; this record IS that resolver. Never assumed as a premise.

claim: g2 LLM-proxy stand-in: the g2 scored result (poc/g2/result.json sha256 96a23be5) fills the frozen two-human g2.gold GATE-H role with a blind cross-family LLM pair (judge-pA GPT-5.6-Sol primary stand-in; judge-pB claude-haiku-4-5, vendor-family overlap disclosed, never sole gold) over the full Pi dump of pinned kernel-v0 (n_gold=84; the frozen n_planned=500 and gate n_gold>=500 are unattainable on this corpus for any annotator). Every gold-dependent number is PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; kappa is pair stability not human-agreement; proxy labels quarantined from future human annotation; the human g2.gold remains the sole adjudicator of HS2 and any Pi demotion.
rationale: enacts maintainer directive #11 (GPT-5.6 as human-annotator stand-in) for the g2 Pi-soundness leg
notes: registered from g2.md; block 1180-1189; PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY, human gold reconciles later

claim: g2 proxy cross-reading (interpretive colour only; never a premise): the proxy readout (precision 0.3929, one-sided 95% Wilson UB 0.4825; bracket 0.2857-0.4524) lies decidably BELOW the pre-computed (0.86,0.94) not-well-decidable band (FAIL side of the 0.9 gate at proxy strength) while all deterministic legs pass (litmus recovered, 0 partition leaks, 0 sidecar conflicts). With g3-llmproxy-v3 this is a second independent proxy warning on the Pi/necessity line, favouring option (b) (authored sidecar normative, Pi as lint) in DIRECTION; it cannot fire the demotion, cannot resolve HS2, is compatible with common-mode LLM literalism, and licenses NO feasibility conclusion.
rationale: records the proxy-strength direction as interpretive colour, never a premise
resolution_path: the human g2.gold panel (2 annotators, blind adjudication over the same 84 items, proxy labels quarantined) resolves the ordinary-meaning-soundness question; until then this is proxy colour only
notes: registered from g2.md; block 1180-1189; EXTRAPOLATION, never a premise

claim: data/axioms-kinship-v1 is minted as: 5 AUTHORED molecule-style concept records (parent, person, grandparent, grandfather, grandmother) content-addressed under the exact molecules-v0 kot-mol/1 identity algorithm (profile header, JCS/NFC identity payload, sha2-256 multihash multibase32; mint self-check reproduced the committed mother/father/man/woman URNs byte-exactly before minting), plus 8 kot-axiom/1 records using extension kinds {subPropertyOf, coveredBy, propertyChain, domain, classDeclaration}, each citing its endorsing explication sha. Explicator loop COMPLETED 2026-07-11: parent, person, grandfather, grandmother ENDORSED unchanged; grandparent REVISED — groundingNote 'the parent' -> 'a parent' (the definite article implied a uniqueness that is false under ordinary meaning; no functionality axiom licenses it, unlike 'the father'/'the mother' in the gendered records) — and re-minted urn:kot:bciqlzfmtaa5t3l6sfvh7uci3qno335zb7sy3yp36v33oohyvyudklpa -> urn:kot:bciqobx6ivvuntsotdxrxovep5uvhabut2rfu3s53tv4caztkeykwpya, with the dependent chain-* axiom records regenerated; corpus re-pinned (kot-corpus-hash/1 be3f2d40c1ad06fe7d706c0e36b4f9290d515e798d8e9e2fbb94dc3a02a1508d) in registry/experiments/rules-1.json. Maintainer endorsement rides the freeze approval. The frozen tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py refuses the extension kinds (fail-closed), per ASM-1126 notes.
rationale: Fills the named parent/person gap on the approved MD-1 path; content-addressing keeps identity honest; the explicator revision removed a false uniqueness implicature before freeze, and the re-pin keeps endorsement authority with the explicator/maintainer.
notes: grandparent/grandfather/grandmother added beyond ASM-1126's ~6 because E3's answer vocabulary is gendered; see ASM-1191. ASM-1121 pointer fix applied: the covering premise now resolves to data/axioms-kinship-v1/cover-parent.json, whose endorsement block cites the endorsed explication concepts/parent.json (sha 605084322c1e7bb23decdec9758cb0c0717c9f6b2a6717ae7da949b64b7d5cf2; covering clause endorsed as read RELATION-LEVEL — to be someone's parent is to be that someone's mother or that someone's father, the exact form R-COVER-ELIM uses; a disclosed stipulation, never claimed analytic), superseding the bare docs/design-l3a-rules-engine-oracle.md#7 pointer in ASM-1121's backing_ref.

claim: E3 gendered-grandparent completion is encoded as pure OWL-RL property chains — parent∘father ⊑ grandfather, parent∘mother ⊑ grandmother, parent∘parent ⊑ grandparent (each with range typing) — rather than a post-hoc gendering Horn rule. Consequence: every E3 proof is regime owl-rl; the policy regime is confined to E1's R-COVER-ELIM; the horn-def regime is unused in RULES-1 (disclosed, not hidden).
rationale: Keeps the epistemically strongest stratum carrying the 858-item third-party-gold leg; the contested premises (UNA, covering) touch only the E1 cells that need them.
notes: Realisation of the §3 'gendered via range' sketch. Chain records regenerated 2026-07-11 to cite the revised grandparent explication (endorsement cascade).

claim: Engine identity for the certificate: decisions computed by the Python differential twin (poc/rules-1/twin_engine.py, sha 399fcd8d946b585e6a73317238e049602ae5e86aef7c4ed1b0243ff887e8dea8); sparq-reason (primary, MD-4a) built on-box (after installing gcc + a minimal rust toolchain) and its N3 fixpoint exactly agreed with the twin on Cl(S) for all 1,207 conformance cases over the same compiled rules.n3 — repeated 2026-07-11 under runner-role separation on the ENDORSED+REVISED corpus (1207/1207, regenerated rules.n3 sha 9857da8918d8d87823b6d21b248bef83bf5a8515cedeec6ff5dd5c3b26a22f98); the twin-governs fallback (ASM-1124) was NOT used. Scope limit: the N3 entry point was exercised, not the OwlRl materializer; sparq commit 0ab87b2a5910fe0c783a73dcc043e93ed39c9f58.
rationale: ASM-1124's exact-agreement gate executed and disclosed rather than assumed; the entry-point scope limit is named so the conformance claim is not over-read.
notes: The registered runner-role re-run on the revised corpus is done (2026-07-11); conformance result bytes are identical to the pre-revision run because the agreement counts are corpus-count-invariant.

claim: The certificate's stated-bytes comparator (GS-B) is operationalised as the aligned flat lookup over the item's stated kot-world/1 records projected onto the query read-set: for a relation query (a,b), the lexicon word of a STATED relation record linking a to b (else no-answer); for a class cell, membership of the STATED class record — DECONF-A1's GS-A construction, inverted (the engine's decisions are the reference the projection tries to reproduce).
rationale: 'No projection of the stated bytes' must be made mechanical to be measurable; this is the same projection family the inertness verdict used, so the flip is measured on the maintainer's own definition.

claim: E1 cells are constructed from world-v0 by ABSTRACTION: for each of 124 children with both gendered parent edges stated and distinct, the held-out gendered edge is restated as plain parent (2x124 cells, both directions) with the item-scoped UNA sidecar; gold is the held-out world-v0 stated edge — kernel-authored, NOT third-party. The external-gold soundness bar (Wilson-LB >= 0.98) therefore rests solely on the E3/CLUTRR leg.
rationale: Gives R-COVER-ELIM a measured, held-out-by-construction cell family at zero authoring cost while keeping the gold-provenance distinction explicit.
notes: Gender class records are EXCLUDED from E1 stated sets so the cover-elimination premises are exactly the §3 triple.

claim: ASM-1163's mutation gate is realised as three arms: CF-2a chain-only swap -> PREDICTED outcome is ERR_CONFLICT on every covered decision (the mutated definitions contradict the stated gender records; CAX-DW fail-closed), measured 858/858; CF-2b coherent chain+range label swap -> predicted exact grandfather<->grandmother flip with zero conflicts, measured 858/858; CF-3 meaning-preserving no-op (record + constraint order permuted) -> byte-identical decisions, measured. A chain-only swap CANNOT produce clean flips because range(father)=Man regenerates gender typing — conflict is the only sound outcome; this replaces the naive 'flip' prediction.
rationale: Makes the 'changes exactly the predicted outputs' gate well-posed; the CF-2a arm additionally measures that the definitions bind against the data (contradiction surfaces, never resolves).
notes: Extends PROPOSED-ASM-1163. Re-measured 2026-07-11 on the endorsed+revised corpus: all three arms pass identically.

claim: The RULES-1 engine's expressivity boundary is documented in the architecture record: the closed safe function-free inventory cannot express mathematics (arithmetic, induction, quantifier alternation) and refuses rather than approximates; the future-work seam is the axiom/world-layer Lean route (Lean libraries were once brought into core: data/math-lean-sample, data/mathlib-1000-sample, the kot-pm-* math corpora), entering at the same authored/endorsed C1 boundary with proof-carrying regime-tagged output — a much-later consideration, OUT of RULES-1 scope; no RULES-1 claim extends past the registered inventory.
backing_ref: docs/next/arch/world-model-rules-engine.md section 'Expressivity boundary and the Lean seam'; maintainer note issue #19
rationale: Records the maintainer's requested boundary verbatim as a named seam instead of scope silence.

claim: Role/provenance ledger for the RULES-1 precondition: the 2026-07-11 pre-revision certificate + conformance executions were EXPLORATORY pre-freeze runs by the build role; the 2026-07-11 registered $0 CPU RE-RUN on the explicator-endorsed+revised corpus was executed by the runner role and is pinned in registry/experiments/rules-1.json pins.artifact_hashes as the PASSED precondition (certificate-result sha e0071e9e4952f915c461206d514afa555d683bc22985fad2273821f37176d379: success_asm_1131=true, gates_asm_1163_all_pass=true, kill_a_fired=false; conformance 1207/1207) — a precondition pin, NOT a results-log append (rules-1 was not frozen at re-run time). The record is FREEZE-READY (prereg-freeze dry-run DRY-RUN-OK, zero pause flags); freezing, the GPU campaign, verdict-gen and audit remain the coordinator/experiment-runner/analyst/auditor roles' respectively. No result in poc/rules-1/ licenses any claim until the frozen campaign is graded.
backing_ref: poc/rules-1/RESULT.md (incl. freeze finalization addendum); registry/experiments/rules-1.json runner_constraints.roles; deconf-a1 exploratory precedent
rationale: Run-vs-audit separation and frozen-design discipline preserved while still delivering the decisive free result now; the re-run closes the stale-corpus follow-up under the correct role.
notes: Block ASM-1190..1209 reserved; 1198..1209 unused.

claim: Option-B ruling adopted (maintainer decision issue 6, 2026-07-11): the knull plain arm is authored at natural concise dictionary length; G-1 clause L-3 (the +/-25% word band) is DROPPED and L-4 is RELAXED to >=1 admissible claim segment for the plain store; all other G-1 clauses unchanged; the ASM-0703 blind quality gate is re-run on the v3 store as a freeze precondition. Store: poc/knull/inputs-v3/plain-authored.json v3.0.0.
rationale: Adopts the maintainer's ruling as the named governing premise for the knull-v2 plain arm so the relaxed authoring gate is a recorded stipulation, not a silent change to G-1.
notes: Emitted in the knull option-B analysis PROPOSED-ASM block; block ASM-1080..1089 reserved to that deliverable, 1089 unused.

claim: Length-confound control: the verdict-bearing control is the experimental length-matched plain-padded arm; token-count covariate/regression adjustment is DESCRIPTIVE only (no within-concept identification; intercept-at-parity is an extrapolation premise), never verdict-bearing.
rationale: Preserves the no-extrapolation-as-premise discipline: regressing to token parity projects outside the design and may not carry a verdict; only an actually-run matched arm may.
notes: Knull option-B block.

claim: plain-padded generator: cyclic whole-own-segment repetition joined by '; ' into the kernel-gloss word band [0.75*wc, max(1.25*wc, wc+8)]; degenerate no-pad allowed in-band; fail-closed (band landing, segment-set equality, LC1, uniqueness); deterministic transform, no authoring gate; feasibility MEASURED 108/108 on v3.0.0; disclosed bias direction: any answer-key-repetition effect favors the padded arm — conservative against the content claim.
rationale: A deterministic fail-closed transform (opaque-generator precedent) supplies the length-matched control without a second authoring gate; the disclosed bias direction runs against the kernel claim, so the control is conservative.
notes: Knull option-B block; feasibility leg is MEASURED, the design choice is the stipulated part.

claim: Superiority guard (IUT): kernel_superior_beyond_margin = [LB95_1s(D_full) > +0.05] AND [LB95_1s(D_matched) > +0.05], D_matched vs the eligible token-matched arm (plain-padded, else opaque) with larger point lift; conjunction at fixed margin — no Holm resize; no eligible token-matched arm => length_guard_available=false and superiority forced false.
rationale: Intersection-union conjunction at fixed margin keeps the superiority claim honest against both the full and matched comparisons without alpha resizing; forcing false when the guard is unavailable fails closed.
notes: Knull option-B block.

claim: NULL and FAIL readings are unchanged in form and licensed a fortiori under the plain arm's smaller budget; the NULL relabel adopts the clause 'at no greater token budget'; the extrapolation envelope gains the natural-length scope sentence.
rationale: A null or fail found against a cheaper comparator is at least as strong as the pre-registered reading; the added clauses keep the envelope exact about the natural-length scope.
notes: Knull option-B block.

claim: Gate scope: flops-parity (run-time +/-20%; pre-freeze G-3 +/-10%) binds plain-padded and opaque vs kernel; the concise plain arm is exempt by design, its ratio metered and reported DESCRIPTIVE (/gates/flops_ratio_plain); difficulty-band and extraction gates extend to plain-padded.
rationale: Parity gates bind only the arms that claim parity; the concise arm's budget advantage is the design's point, so its ratio is metered for disclosure rather than gated.
notes: Knull option-B block.

claim: plain-padded cell budget: alone-R1 + verify-retry-R1, seeds {0,1,2}, same 1000 paired skeletons; excluded from the alone-R3 bridge and the shuffled control (role-limited length control).
rationale: The padded arm exists solely to control length on the verdict-bearing cells; excluding it from bridge and shuffled cells caps cost without weakening the control's role.

claim: Quality-gate scope: ASM-0703 applies to the authored concise store only; the padded arm is disclosed REAL non-authored arm content (deterministic transform; opaque-generator precedent).
rationale: The blind quality gate certifies authored content; a deterministic transform of already-gated content needs disclosure, not a second gate — the opaque-generator precedent.

claim: Pre-freeze token evidence status: the substitution projection (poc/knull/inputs-v3/token-projection.json — plain 0.626, plain-padded 0.931 of kernel mean prompt tokens; gloss-level plain 0.309 MEASURED) is pre-freeze evidence only; binding resolution = the v3-build G-3 artifact and the run-time F0 FLOPs ledger.

claim: CODEVERT-FL/1 = primary families {contains, contained_in, imports_of, where_defined} (FL-4); callees_of = disclosed sensitivity slice (annotated and reported identically, never in the primary aggregate); callers_of/imported_by/instance_of excluded from the re-scoped product claim (PY-STAT/2 territory); the maintainer-gloss 'kappa~=0.72' vs G0-option-(a) family ambiguity resolved as pinned in §1, not post-hoc.

claim: G1 pool: the §2.1 pinned-before-looking 20+5 repo list (names and order fixed at design-pin time, disjoint from the G0 six), size band [300,120000] analyzed *.py LOC, mechanical backup substitution in pinned order on clone/license/band failure, shallow-HEAD SHAs recorded; agent-selected disclosure — every cross-repo band is a resampling sensitivity band, never a generalization CI; all-*.py analyzed scope + package-source-only co-report.
rationale: Pinning the pool by name and order before any repo content is seen removes selection-after-looking; the agent-selected honesty clause keeps cross-repo bands from being read as population CIs.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: G1 census = G0 census.py generator logic (content-hash-pinned, extractor-independent) with fresh pinned seed 20260716, generated and hashed before the extractor runs; extractor PY-STAT/1 byte-identical to poc/codevert-g0/extractor.py (any edit = extractor version change per ASM-1031, voiding the run); FL-4 primary aggregate (query-pooled + family-macro), all-8 families co-reported; repo-cluster bootstrap (seed 20260716, 10k) = sensitivity band only.
rationale: Census-before-extraction ordering and a byte-identical extractor make the G1 measurement a pure new-data replication of the pinned instrument.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: Proxy gold: two LLM annotators (fable-a Claude-family; gpt56-b gpt-5.6-sol via pinned codex@0.144.1 invocation, effort medium — a disclosed deviation from the judge-1p 'low'), blind and independent, byte-identical instructions and context bundles; disagreements adjudicated by the Fable main agent with per-item logged rationale (annotation/adjudication.jsonl); ALL gold-dependent endpoints PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY, void the moment a human Pass-B annotation of the same sample lands.
rationale: LLM stand-in gold is the maintainer-approved instrument for this leg; the blindness, logged adjudication, and void-on-human-gold clause keep it honestly provisional rather than silently authoritative.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin; g3-humangold pattern.

claim: Sample: 2 queries per (family x repo) cell over 5 measured families x 20 repos = 200 queries, seed-pinned (20260716) from the frozen census universes; cells smaller than 2 take all and log the shortfall; 120 KB context-bundle cap with pinned next-index replacement rule (logged, never silently dropped); no-label >10% per endpoint => instrument-invalid for that endpoint.
rationale: A seed-pinned cell design with mechanical replacement and a pre-stated invalidity threshold makes the proxy-gold sample auditable and fail-closed.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.

claim: Element normalization rules of §4: contains/contained_in and repo-internal callees compared as relpath::qualpath symbols (module target = relpath); imports_of as repo:relpath for repo-internal (with parent-package closure + self-edges per ASM-1058) and ext:<base> for external per the PY-STAT/1 emission rule; where_defined as (relpath, lineno) sites with engine spans mapped to lines mechanically; callees_of precision/R_q computed on repo-internal elements only (disclosed).
rationale: Pinned normalization removes scorer discretion at comparison time; the repo-internal-only callee scoring is the one disclosed narrowing, taken because external-callee naming is not reliably normalizable.
notes: CODEVERT G1 design pin.
