claim: Stage-time digest attestation (review fix 2): ablation_runner.py:stage() re-digests the k1/k2 SOURCE input directories (kot-corpus-hash/1) against the REGISTERED pins in the rules-1-knull-ablation record and refuses post-freeze drift fail-closed (ERR_PIN_MISMATCH); staged copies must reproduce the source digests; every payload carries an attestation block {record id/status/frozen_sha256, arm identity, per-arm harness shas, source+staged TBox dir-digests, shared-corpus file shas, pinned decision-payload sha}; analysis/rules_1_knull.py REJECTS unattested, mis-armed, or drifted payloads (/gates/harness_pins_valid false => INSTRUMENT-INVALID). Mock-validated: drifted-digest and missing-attestation payloads are rejected.
rationale: The analysis must be able to prove the graded bytes are the frozen bytes — silent post-freeze substitution of inputs or harness can never be graded.

claim: Pre-freeze parse-only smoke (review fix 4): ablation_runner.py --smoke loads every arm's staged TBox through the pinned twin_engine.load_tbox and records load success + compiled-rule-kind counts (inputs/parse-smoke.json, pinned). NO closure is computed, NO item evaluated, NO decision exists — the survival predicate stays blind (ASM-1409). smoke.pass==true is a mandatory component of /gates/compilation_lint_valid. MEASURED 2026-07-12: k0/k1/k2 all load; rule-kind counts identical across arms.
rationale: A trivially-unparseable comparator must be caught pre-freeze as an authoring bug, never surface post-freeze where it could masquerade as an outcome.

claim: Independent definition->constraint mapping review (review fix 4): inputs/def-constraint-mapping.json (mechanical per-constraint extract: kind, decoded slots, licensed_by span+reading, full definition) is reviewed by an INDEPENDENT reviewer role (reviewer-1; authored neither knull_kinship_defs.json nor build_knull_tbox.py) for (a) per-constraint semantic licensing under a plain-dictionary reading and (b) completeness vs the kernel target grammar (compiler-induced-collapse hazard). Verdict inputs/mapping-review.json (pinned) — MEASURED 2026-07-12: 20/20 licensed, missing_content=[], 7 disclosed hazards, pass=true; mapping.pass==true is a mandatory component of /gates/compilation_lint_valid.
rationale: Lexical span traceability alone does not establish a competent comparator; an independent semantic review does, and it must exist BEFORE freeze so a collapse can be attributed to content, not compilation.

claim: Engine-identity disclosure (review fix 6): k1/k2 ablation decisions execute ONLY the pinned Python differential twin (twin_engine.py 399fcd8d…). The inherited certificate_result.engine_identity text ('BOTH engines … sparq-reason … EXACTLY agreed', commit 0ab87b2a) describes the RULES-1 conformance run over the KERNEL TBox and is FALSE for the substituted arms — sparq-reason is NOT executed over the knull/scrambled TBoxes; the runner stamps engine_identity_ablation_disclosure into every k1/k2 payload and no reader may cite the inherited text for k1/k2. Substituted-TBox differential conformance is a NAMED FOLLOW-ON, not part of this record.
rationale: An inherited two-engine claim riding on single-engine ablation payloads would be a false provenance statement in the graded bytes.

claim: Phase-2b statistical discipline (review fixes 8/9): hostlift() requires EXACTLY 858 paired items x 3 seeds x all four (host_tbox x host_arm) cells (10,296 rows; duplicates, missing rows, extras, or non-binary scores => /analysis/hostlift_rows_valid false, ALL stats null — complete-case dropping is a preregistration violation); Holm within {h1,h2} is implemented on one-sided bootstrap p-values (smallest at alpha/2=0.025, other at 0.05 only if the first rejects) and each hypothesis passes only if its registered CI criterion AND its Holm-adjusted rejection both hold; activation is machine-enforced on the pinned parent rules-1 /analysis/primary_pass == true; kernel cells reused verbatim from the frozen rules-1 run-records. Mock-validated on full, incomplete, and parent-fail inputs.
rationale: Closes the review's inference defects: no unadjusted twin tests, no silent row dropping, no self-activated GPU leg.

claim: Runtime/generation ESTIMATES (never load-bearing for any verdict): certificate leg ~4s CPU per arm at ~$0; phase-2b <=6k generations (858 items x 3 seeds x {A1, A3 k=4} on the two knull cells only, kernel cells reused) inside the <=$5 / 2 GPU-h / 6h-wall caps. Extrapolated from the rules-1/nsk1 cost data (linear-scaling PROPOSED-ASM-1134 lineage).
rationale: Review fix: estimates must be quarantined as EXTRAPOLATION with a resolution path, not ride inside STIPULATED rows.
resolution_path: measure wall-clock, CPU time and generation counts in the registered run's provenance logs (run-log + Modal provenance JSON) and compare to these estimates in the analyst readout; kill nothing on mismatch — re-tag this row resolved with the measured values

claim: Concrete harness manifests (review fix 1): pins.harness_manifest carries the full CPU manifest (every executable + staged harness byte + dataset with sha256; python3 stdlib-only; zero model calls) and the full GPU phase-2b manifest (rules1_runner.py 020394f2…, modal_rules1.py 209dd08f…, rules1-manifest.json a4c947bf… pinning f2bt loop 810dcbc5… + image reqs 0fac7243…; model R1 = HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct@12fd25f77366fa6b3b4b768ec3050bf629380bac). MODEL-REVISION PROVENANCE CORRECTION: the FROZEN revision carriers are f2b-replicate/f2b-transfer pins.model_revisions; the previously-inherited 'revision pinned at freeze from the nsk1 frozen record' text was false (nsk1 is DRAFT with placeholders) and is corrected in this record.
rationale: No PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder survives on the execution path: both legs are runnable today against pinned bytes, and the one false provenance citation is named and fixed.

claim: RULES-2 is gated on the RULES-1 GPU readout: PASS => proceed as designed (s3' live); KILL-b => no auto-run, explicit maintainer re-authorization required under the changed rationale, s3' struck; INSTRUMENT-INVALID/INCONCLUSIVE => blocked pending instrument fix, no spend. Refines PROPOSED-ASM-1136.
rationale: Sequencing the train slot after the memory slot avoids paying for the follow-on before the engine's derivations are shown to matter anywhere; the KILL-b branch is named rather than silent.

claim: Training worlds are world-v0 plus a seeded synthetic expansion generated by the nsk1-eval family generator with fresh seeds and families disjoint from the eval twin; data/nsk1-clutrr remains strictly EVAL-SIDE (CC-BY-NC-4.0, ASM-1125) and no CLUTRR bytes enter any training corpus; per-item fresh entity names on the eval slice guarantee zero string overlap with training.
rationale: License discipline and the memorisation split are the same mechanism here: the primary eval stratum must be unreachable by string recall.

claim: The RULES-2 training corpus is generated CPU-only and deterministically by a materialisation pass over the training default world using the PINNED RULES-1 twin engine and rules.n3 (byte-exact pin verification, fail closed); example families are {stated, entailed Cl(S)\S, refusal E5, optional proof-augmented}; canonical verbalisation, explicit generator seed, corpus pinned by sha; why() provenance and regime tags {owl-rl,horn-def,policy} retained in a sidecar (outside B2 training text).
rationale: The treatment content is exactly the non-inert engine's output, pinned end-to-end, so any measured internalisation is attributable to the certified closure and stratifiable by semantic regime.

claim: Claim assignment across eval strata: only S-out (out-of-world, fresh-name, prompt-supplied temporary-world facts, no engine anywhere) carries the internalisation claim; S-mem (trained facts) is lookup-in-weights, the train-slot analog of A4's runtime-inert lookup (ASM-1139), reported as a memorisation descriptive never an inference claim; S-held is a partial-generalisation descriptive.
rationale: Prevents the train slot's easiest number (accuracy on facts it was trained on) from being read as inference; mirrors the A4 claim restriction one level up.

claim: RULES-2 primary decision rule: B2 - B0 on S-out entailed cells vs the pinned third-party CLUTRR gold; paired item BCa bootstrap B=10^4, one-sided 95% LB > 0. KILL-d fires on LB <= 0: fine-tuning on the materialised closure does not internalise the inference; the closure remains a data product; the engine remains required at inference; route to the RULES-1 memory-slot architecture; no model claim.
rationale: Same external-gold, sign-safe, pre-registered discipline as RULES-1's primary, applied to the stratum that memorisation cannot reach.

claim: Training-exposure control B1 (fine-tune on stated+refusal families only, no entailments, matched corpus size) is mandatory; Holm secondary s2' requires B2 - B1 LB > 0 on S-out, separating 'saw the inference content' from 'saw the domain/format'.
rationale: Fine-tuning alone can lift a 135M model on a narrow format; without B1 the treatment effect is confounded with exposure.

claim: Content control c1': a fine-tune on a Sattolo-deranged closure (entailment targets shuffled across items at identical corpus size and token count) must not retain the lift — recovery UB95 < 0.30, H-STRUCT discipline applied at the train slot.
rationale: Distinguishes content-driven internalisation from bulk/format artefacts of fine-tuning; threshold carried verbatim from the estate discipline.

claim: Control c8 (train-bytes projection): the DECONF-A1/GS flat-lookup projection family applied to the pinned TRAINING corpus must score at approximately chance on S-out; if it recovers S-out answers above the pre-registered ceiling the train/eval split is broken and the experiment is INSTRUMENT-INVALID before any arm is interpreted. This is the internalisation instrument: the claim attaches only where the model answers correctly AND no projection of its own training bytes does.
rationale: Runs the estate's inertness instrument one level up: with entailments deliberately placed in the training bytes, 'not reproducible from a flat projection of those bytes' is the mechanical content of 'internalised a rule rather than a lookup table'.
notes: CPU-only; gates all GPU spend together with the pin gate.

claim: Gap-closure secondary s3': noninferiority of B2 (fine-tuned, no engine, k=1) vs B4 (RULES-1 A3 engine verify-retry k=4) on S-out, absolute margin 0.05, sign-only language; CONDITIONAL on RULES-1's A3 lift having been positive (else unevaluable: null, never a fail); descriptive companion gap fraction (B2-B0)/(B4-B0).
rationale: 'Does fine-tuning close the host-lift gap' is the follow-on's headline question; conditioning keeps it well-posed if the gap never existed.

claim: The efficiency deliverable is a pre-registered ledger, not a claim: measured LoRA training GPU-h/$/FLOPs per arm and rung, one-off closure materialisation CPU cost, per-query inference cost (B2: one forward pass k=1, zero engine dependency; B4: up to k=4 passes + engine CPU in the 5.3us/query class), and the break-even query count N*; with the named caveat that the engine's marginal compute is microseconds on CPU so the ledger may show the engine cheaper at any realistic N, in which case the train slot's efficiency case rests only on resample reduction (k=4 to k=1) and removal of the deployment dependency. No direction is presumed.
backing_ref: registry/verdicts/l3a.json (5.3us/query); poc/rules-1/RESULT.md (materialisation <2s CPU); WMRE-1 2.2 KOTC train-slot row
rationale: The maintainer's train-time/prompt-time split gets its full price table; pre-registering the possibly-unflattering outcome prevents post-hoc efficiency spin.
notes: Fills the training_compute V-vector slot rules-1 fixed at 0.

claim: Degradation guard s4' (Herron caveat): B2's stated-cell accuracy and E5 refusal correctness must each be within 0.05 absolute of the better of B0/B1; breach flags SIDE-EFFECT and caps any deployment-shaped language without affecting the primary. Scope per MD-R2-7: in-domain strata plus, if a pinned external anchor is reusable at ~zero authoring cost, a small non-kinship catastrophic-forgetting probe.
backing_ref: Herron et al. 2025 (materialised entailments can degrade downstream) via WMRE-1 2.2 and PROPOSED-ASM-1136 backing
rationale: The published failure mode of exactly this intervention is downstream degradation; it must be measured, and it must not be able to masquerade as neutral.

claim: Fail-closed behaviour is TRAINED and SCORED: the training corpus includes E5-style insufficient-premise examples with named-refusal targets, and refusal correctness on control cells is a scored stratum for every fine-tuned arm; a fine-tuned model that answers where the engine refuses is a measured regression, never a bonus.
backing_ref: RULES-1 E5 stratum (100/100 named refusals, certificate); L3a control-refusal discipline
rationale: Internalising the inference without internalising its refusal boundary would trade correctness for coverage silently; training and scoring the boundary keeps the fail-closed discipline intact across the slot change.

claim: Fine-tuning is LoRA with hyperparameters (rank, lr, epochs, batch) PINNED at prereg-freeze; fine-tune seeds {0,1,2}; any hyperparameter search is confined to a synthetic dev split disjoint from all eval strata, is disclosed in the record, and its selection rule is pinned before the final runs.
backing_ref: estate prereg discipline (kot-reg/2 pins); PROPOSED-ASM-1134 LoRA cost row
rationale: HP tuning against eval is the train slot's cheapest way to manufacture a lift; pinning and a disclosed dev split close it.

claim: Host rungs: R1 = SmolLM2-135M-Instruct and R2 = SmolLM2-360M-Instruct (revisions pinned at freeze from existing pinned records), both fine-tuned; R3 = SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct is a no-fine-tune comparator (B5) only. Two rungs license a SIGN, not a slope; scale language is sign-only.
backing_ref: docs/design-a5-llm.md R2 pin precedent; rules-1 R1/R3 pins; estate scale-language rule
rationale: The efficiency framing needs the estate's minimum two-rung discipline; fine-tuning R3 would answer a different (and costlier) question than 'does a SMALL model internalise'.

claim: RULES-2 cost bands: data + c8 gate ~$0 (CPU); smallest decisive (R1: B0/B1/B2/c1' x 3 FT seeds + S-out evals) $4-8; full skeleton (+B3, B4 re-slice, B5, R2 rung) $10-18; worst case $25-35. Compute source: Modal single A10G under the standing Tier-0/F2 authorization, same instance class and harness lineage as nsk1/f2b/rules-1.
backing_ref: nsk1 B' measured datum (32,958 rows = 0.457 A10G-h ~= $0.50); WMRE-1 2.2 LoRA estimate $2-5/fine-tune
rationale: Eval generations scale from the one measured throughput datum on the same instance class; training bands from the KOTC-lens estimate widened for seed replication; no new provisioning.
resolution_path: measure the actual per-arm GPU-hours, USD and wall-clock in the registered RULES-2 run's provenance logs (run-log + Modal provenance JSON) and compare to these bands in the analyst readout; kill nothing on mismatch — re-tag this row resolved with the measured values
notes: MD-R2-5. Modal hygiene per bd memory: nohup+setsid; modal app stop ap-<id> after killing any client.

claim: The decode-time logit-mask arm (A6) remains DEFERRED in RULES-2: the answer surface is the same closed 23-word kinship vocabulary, so DV-5's tautology condition (|L|=1 => the mask is the engine's answer in model skin) is unchanged and DV-5's deferral condition (an open answer surface) is not met; the |L| decisiveness ledger remains mandatory if the arm ever runs. Amends PROPOSED-ASM-1137's notes.
rationale: Deferral was conditioned on the surface, not the slot; the slot changed and the surface did not.

claim: Eval strata S-mem (trained facts), S-held (in-world held-out 10% of entailed cells, pinned split), and S-out (out-of-world fresh-name items) are reported separately and never pooled; the memorisation-vs-rule confound named as the reason for RULES-2's deferral is resolved by this split plus c8, or the experiment is invalid, never by argument.
rationale: Pooling strata is the single easiest way for a train-slot result to overclaim; the split is the confound's registered resolution mechanism.

claim: RULES-2 reuses the RULES-1 frozen pins byte-exact — twin_engine.py 399fcd8d..., rules.n3 9857da89..., sparq commit 0ab87b2a..., corpus digests (axioms-kinship-v1 be3f2d40..., axioms-v0, world-v0, nsk1-clutrr, nsk1-eval), certificate sha e0071e9e... as a standing precondition — verified fail-closed before corpus generation; no encoder ALGORITHM_VERSION change, no frozen object modified, no new axiom minting in this experiment.
rationale: The treatment's provenance chain must terminate in the already-certified engine; any drift there voids attribution of the internalised content.

claim: ASM-1138's claim cap carries over verbatim: until the phase-2 knull-sourced ablation runs (whose RULES-2 analog is a fine-tune on a knull-sourced closure), any positive RULES-2 result is 'engine-materialised entailments are internalisable by a small host', NEVER 'kernel-specific value'. Extends PROPOSED-ASM-1138.
rationale: The kernel-specificity question is unchanged by moving the slot; the cap is free and preserves honesty.

claim: Eval decoding is greedy fp32 with a byte-identical repeat gate per arm; fine-tune seed variance across {0,1,2} is disclosed per arm (mean and range); the primary is computed on the per-seed-paired statistic, not a best-of-seeds selection.
backing_ref: rules-1 frozen design (greedy, fp32, seeds); estate repeat-gate discipline
rationale: Fine-tuning adds a seed axis RULES-1 did not have; best-of-seeds selection is a named overclaim path and is excluded up front.

claim: The RULES-2 harness is poc/rules-2/{materialise_closure.py, rules2_runner.py, inputs/rules2-manifest.json, modal/modal_rules2.py} + analysis/rules_2.py, implementing the design's arms as B0/B1/B2/B3/B4/B5/c1p plus the CPU artifacts c8 (train-bytes projection gate) and c4 (trivial floors, descriptive); poc/rules-1, poc/f2b-transfer, poc/f2b, poc/deconf-b and poc/modal trees are NOT modified — rules-1 drivers and the f2bt scorer are imported byte-identical under sha pins.
rationale: One new module tree; every reused byte pin-verified; the frozen estate untouched.

claim: Training-corpus construction rule: one example per (component, cell); context = ALL stated facts of the component (kinship inventory) verbalised in the RULES-1 canonical rendering + the UNA line; families = {1 stated, 2 entailed {chain E3, cover E1 via parent-abstraction, typing E2 via class-abstraction}, 3 refusal E5 (first 4 sha-ordered insufficient-premise pairs, engine refusal asserted per cell)}; every family-2 example carries why() proof text, regime tag {owl-rl, policy} and proof depth in sidecar fields that NEVER enter B2 training text.
rationale: The treatment content is exactly the pinned engine's output over prompt-shaped contexts — the same temporary-world regime S-out evaluates.

claim: The synthetic expansion replicates the nsk1-eval family SHAPE (3 generations, 8 people, 8 gendered-parent edges) with FRESH seed 20260712 and FRESH invented name pools; surface-name disjointness from BOTH eval corpora (nsk1-clutrr item lexicons, nsk1-eval lexicon incl. name tokens) is asserted fail-closed (ERR_NAME_COLLISION); world-v0 components whose real-person names collide with an eval name are excluded AND counted (0 in the real build).
rationale: Zero string overlap between training names and the primary eval stratum is the memorisation split's mechanical premise; asserted, not assumed.

claim: world-v0 enters training filtered to the RULES-1 closed inventory (mother/father relations, man/woman classes); out-of-inventory records (72 relations, 45 classes) and the 3 components conflict-closed by world-v0's planted violations are EXCLUDED WITH COUNTS in data/rules2-train/manifest.json exclusion_ledger — never silently.
backing_ref: data/world-v0/manifest.json plantedViolations; twin_engine conflict detection (CAX-DW, PRP-FP+UNA)
rationale: The engine fail-closes on conflicted components by design; a disclosed deterministic exclusion preserves both the fail-closed discipline and corpus reproducibility.

claim: Splits: dev = the last 20 synthetic families (loss-sanity ledger only, never selection); S-held = family-2 cells with sha256('sheld|'+id) fraction < 0.10 among non-dev cells (pinned, 1250 cells in the real build). DISCLOSURE: cover (E1) cells share their (pair, answer) string with trained family-1 stated cells of the same component and ARE flat-lookup-recoverable (c8 S-held by_kind: cover 142/142, chain 0/125, typing 0/33); S-held is descriptive-only and read stratified by kind — the S-out claim is untouched.
rationale: The one string-leak the split construction creates is measured and disclosed by the instrument itself rather than argued away.

claim: c8 operationalisation: GS-family FLAT lookup over the pinned B2 TRAINING bytes — a QA index (trained question pair -> answer) plus an aligned fact-line index ((subject-name, object-name) -> stated word), NO joins, NO inference; gate G2 = S-out recovered accuracy <= 0.10 (chance-class ceiling; measured 0/858 = 0.0 in the real build), run pre-GPU together with the pin gate; failure => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, no spend.
rationale: The internalisation instrument executed at $0 before any GPU: the claim can only attach where the model's own training bytes cannot answer by lookup.

claim: The refusal surface is the named phrase 'cannot be determined from these facts' scored as a 24th forced-choice option appended to the 23-word CLUTRR menu, with a refusal_note line in every rules-2 prompt (train and eval); sum-of-token-logprob scoring length-penalises the longer refusal option UNIFORMLY across all compared arms — disclosed, not corrected.
rationale: Trains and scores the fail-closed boundary on one commensurable surface; the known length bias cancels in every between-arm contrast.

claim: B1 size matching (ASM-1425 'matched corpus size') is implemented as deterministic cyclic repetition of the sha-sorted stated+refusal train ids until |B1| == |B2| (pinned in data/rules2-train/b1-upsample.json); token-count parity is approximate (repeated stated examples are shorter than entailed ones) and the actual per-arm train token counts are printed by --dry-plan and recorded in the training ledger.
rationale: Example-count parity with full disclosure of the residual token-count delta beats an undisclosed mismatch or fabricated filler content.

claim: c1' is a Sattolo cyclic derangement (seed 20260713, fixed-point-asserted) of family-2 TRAIN targets WITHIN each menu class (rel23 and mw2 separately), pinned in data/rules2-train/c1shuf-map.json; corpus size and total token count are IDENTICAL to B2 (the answer multiset is permuted, not altered).
rationale: Content destruction at exactly identical bulk — the cleanest form of the estate's structure control at the train slot.

claim: LoRA hyperparameters are PINNED a priori in rules2-manifest.json (r=16, alpha=32, dropout=0, target q_proj/v_proj, lr=2e-4, 2 epochs, batch 16 via micro-batch 8, warmup 20, weight_decay 0, max_seq_len 512, AdamW, completion-only loss, fp32): NO hyperparameter search was performed anywhere in this build; the dev split feeds only a per-epoch loss ledger. Any pre-freeze HP change requires re-pinning the manifest and re-running --mock; any search must be dev-split-only and disclosed.
rationale: Closes the train slot's cheapest lift-manufacturing path exactly as the design demands.

claim: B4 is the RULES-1 A3 loop executed VERBATIM through the imported, sha-pinned rules1_runner.py bytes (frames from the rules-1 manifest, k=4, fp32 R1, f2bt seeded resampler SEED_BASE 20260710, gold-leak guard) over the identical 858-item S-out slice, seeds {0,1,2}; its E5 control rows are engine-decided named refusals emitted under arm=B4. B4 is exempt from the rules-2 in-process repeat gate: its determinism instrument is the rules-1 decision-sha double-run discipline, unchanged.
backing_ref: PROPOSED-ASM-1428; registry/experiments/rules-1.json A3; poc/rules-2/rules2_runner.py B4 branch
rationale: The gap-closure comparator must be the frozen comparator, not a reimplementation.

claim: Eval decoding: 'greedy fp32' is implemented as the f2bt forced-choice scorer at attempt 0 (argmax over option logprobs — greedy on the closed surface, deterministic); G4 is an IN-PROCESS byte-identical repeat per arm (first seed) over the pinned first-200-by-sha S-out covered subsample, recorded as repeat_shas in the results bytes and read by the analysis repeat gate.
rationale: Identical scorer bytes to every prior estate host run; the subsample repeat is the same determinism instrument at 23% of the token cost.

claim: Prompt-format assignment: B0 and B5 evaluate S-out in the A1 discipline (padding to the A2-shaped injected token count, ASM-1127 carried over); fine-tuned arms evaluate on the UNPADDED rules-2 prompt (their training format). B3's proof is rendered as a TRAINING-prompt block (scaffold), not a generated rationale, so its eval surface stays commensurable with B2. Both asymmetries are disclosed design choices of this build, flagged for the pre-freeze skeptic pass.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/rules-2-train-time.md 2.3 B0 row ('RULES-1 A1 prompt format, token-matched'); rules2-manifest.json deviations_note
rationale: B0 is specified as A1-verbatim by the design; FT arms must be evaluated in the format they were trained on for the primary to measure internalisation rather than format transfer.

claim: Host revisions: R1 12fd25f7... and R3 31b70e2e... verbatim from the rules-1 manifest (f2b-replicate/f2b-transfer FROZEN chain); R2 = HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-360M-Instruct@a10cc1512eabd3dde888204e902eca88bddb4951 from the a5-llm pinned chain (docs/design-a5-llm.md).
rationale: All three rungs resolve to already-pinned estate revisions; nothing newly floating.

claim: Budget: usd_cap $18 / 14 GPU-h / 24 h wall for the DEFAULT R1 tier (dry-plan on actual corpus chars: est $8.99, worst $13.48 on A10G); the R2 rung (B0/B1/B2, MD-R2-3) is a SECOND coordinator-authorized launch (dry-plan worst $25.10 combined) inside the $35 outer ceiling, matching the design's $25-35 worst-case band; dry-plan fails closed on either cap.
rationale: The binding caps are registry numbers verified by a $0 plan against the real corpus, not the design's estimate bands.
resolution_path: replace the dry-plan estimates with the measured GPU-hours/USD/wall-clock from the registered RULES-2 run's provenance logs and re-tag this row resolved with the measured values; the binding caps themselves are enforced fail-closed by the $0 dry-plan gate, so no verdict ever rests on the estimate

claim: Mock mode is a SYNTHETIC stub (no torch/peft) whose draw streams are shared across arms of the same class so the paired analysis sees the planted contrast, not stub noise; the pins, c8 gate, corpus loading, prompt construction, B4 engine loop (real twin engine), row emitter, repeat gate and the full analysis contract are the REAL code paths; mock numbers are labelled MOCK end-to-end and are never measurements.
rationale: Green mock = harness mechanics + analysis contract validated at $0, with no risk of a stub number being read as evidence.

claim: E2 typing cells use the two-word menu {man, woman} (+ the refusal option) and appear in training and in the S-mem/S-held descriptives only; the primary and all secondaries are computed on rel23-surface cells; typing internalisation is reported under the regime stratification, never pooled into S-out.
rationale: Keeps the primary on the single third-party-gold surface while retaining the design's E2 regime coverage.

claim: Descriptive strata are evaluated on pinned sha-sorted subsamples (s_mem 200, s_held 200, stated_guard 150, refusal_guard 100 from data/rules2-train/eval-samples.json) and on the FIRST fine-tune seed only; the primary stratum S-out keeps all 858 items x all 3 FT seeds. The s4' guard therefore reads seed-0 stated/refusal cells (disclosed).
rationale: Concentrates the GPU budget on the claim-carrying stratum; every cut is pinned and disclosed rather than silent.

claim: Verdict mapping (kot-reg/2 verdict_rules): INSTRUMENT-INVALID unless (pin_gate AND c8_lookup_gate AND headroom AND repeat) all hold; FAIL on /analysis/kill_d_fired (KILL-d: B2-B0 S-out BCa LB95 <= 0); PASS on primary AND s1p AND s2p; else INCONCLUSIVE. Holm family {s1p, s2p, s3p, s4p}; s3p conditional on the RULES-1 A3 lift (null when unevaluable, never a fail); analysis output fields fixed in analysis/rules_2.py OUTPUT_FIELDS.
rationale: The design's decision-rule sketch made mechanical, mirroring the frozen rules-1 rule shapes.

claim: Freeze-time pin protocol: pins.harness_manifest is PINNED-AT-INPUTS (the staged-bytes sha printed by modal_rules2.py --print-manifest); materialiserPySha256 and analysisRules2PySha256 are PINNED-AT-FREEZE (the coordinator hashes the reviewed bytes, regenerates data/rules2-train + c8-result from them, and verifies the corpus digest and c8 sha match the record before prereg-freeze). Any post-freeze byte change requires a correction record before a final-phase run.
rationale: The generator-manifest-embeds-its-own-sha loop means the corpus digest is only final once the materialiser bytes are; the protocol makes that ordering explicit and fail-closed.

claim: g2-import proxy readout record: over the SAME frozen 84 kernel-v0 slots as g2, judge-pA construction, vacuity-zeroed, the soft imported type arms scored A1 55/84=0.6548, A2 53/84=0.6310, A3 57/84=0.6786 (Wilson [0.5728,0.7687]; McNemar vs the frozen A0 33/84 baseline b=6 c=30 p=6.96e-05) with informativeness guard passed (A3 non-vacuous 83/84, R3 42/42, zero hard laws) — but the mechanical verdict is INSTRUMENT-INVALID because kappa_A3 = 0.2859 < 0.40 (sole failing channel; decisive 1.00, probes 0.0, pins ok; kappa_A1 0.527, kappa_A2 0.430). Per the frozen decision table this is NO CONCLUSION: no PASS/GO exists; repair and mint a new frozen run. Every number is PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY (judge-pA GPT-5.6-Sol primary stand-in, judge-pB Claude Haiku 4.5 sensitivity, vendor overlap disclosed); kappa is pair stability, never human agreement; the two-human panel on the SAME frozen package is the sole authority for permanent adoption. Rider verbatim: PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; same 84 self-authored kernel-v0 slots; point-estimate engineering gate, not statistical superiority; soft non-binding typing only — never hard laws; no feasibility conclusion.
rationale: Pins the g2-import proxy readout numbers and the mechanical INSTRUMENT-INVALID no-conclusion status in the register so no later narration can upgrade a kappa-failed run into a PASS/GO.

claim: g2-import gradient reading (interpretive colour only; never a premise): at proxy strength the imported SOFT BFO/SUMO/FrameNet typing nearly doubles blind ordinary-meaning soundness over the hand-authored HARD 4-sort scheme on the identical slots, with the recovery concentrated exactly where the hard scheme failed (R3 27/42 vs ~0), and even the conservative pair-concordant bracket (42/84) clears both gate and baseline — strong DIRECTIONAL support for H-SOFT (the g2 over-constraint is a property of hard-law routing, not of typing as such). This is a GO-SHAPED signal, not a licensed GO: the verdict is INSTRUMENT-INVALID, the gate is a point-estimate engineering gate, and no superiority or adoption claim is licensed. No feasibility conclusion.
resolution_path: human-gold re-adjudication of the SAME frozen 84-slot package (all arms hash-pinned) PLUS a repaired-instrument powered re-run under a NEW frozen record (kappa_A3 stability failure diagnosed/fixed; n sized so stability and soundness are both decidable)

claim: g2-import breadth-control caveat (equal prominence): the A1 BFO-only breadth control also lifted (55/84, within noise of A3's 57), so at n=84 the bulk of the improvement over A0 is attributable to SOFT routing plus broad categorial anchoring rather than to imported source-specific content, whose measured contribution is informativeness coverage (non-vacuous 67->83; R3 26->42) plus a small A3-A2 edge (+4). A1 can never authorize adoption (frozen design) and would flunk the R3 informativeness guard; this caveat sharpens H-SOFT while flattening any claim that the ontologies' specific content carries the effect.
resolution_path: the powered re-run must include an A1-vs-A3 separation test (or a pre-registered A3-vs-A1 paired endpoint) so breadth-plus-softness is distinguishable from source-specific SUMO/FrameNet content

claim: UFO-CHECK-0 items are synthetic, formal, templated, seed-pinned (generator seed 'ufo-check-0/1|two-situation-modal-rigidity|20260712'): 600 scored two-situation modal/rigidity items across families F-RIG/F-ANTI/F-DISJ/F-SPEC (150 each, ~E:C:U balanced, 300 near-miss companions differing by one load-bearing fact) + 30 OUT-OF-PROFILE probes. Meta-typing (kind/subkind/role/phase) is first-class stated facts, never inferred from names. NO natural-input-reach claim.
rationale: Two-situation shape is the smallest carrier of the distinctive full-UFO modal content; templating keeps the instrument deterministic and cheap.

claim: Gold = the disposition computed by the twin_ufo full inventory — ENGINE-DERIVED GOLD, same engine family as the AU checker; the record is labelled ORACLE-DIAGNOSTIC (ASM-0814 lineage). The benchmark-circularity failure mode is answered by controls (AD derangement, AN representation null, AG taxonomy-only), not by argument; residual circularity is disclosed on every readout. Promotion of any UFO-checking claim beyond diagnostic requires the independently-authored, human-reconciled CK-UFO gold set (arch-synthesis decision D2), which proceeds in the annotation lane in parallel and does not block this record.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ufo-check-0.md section 4.2; scratchpad arch-synthesis section 1 item 1 + section 4 D2
rationale: First signal in about a week at <=$20 vs 1-3 weeks + reviewer-days for reconciled gold; the label caps the licence honestly.

claim: twin_ufo.py implements a CLOSED UFO-SN3 rule inventory {U-RIGID, U-ANTI-WIT, U-KIND-DISJ, U-SPEC-MASK, U-EXIST, U-CLOSED, U-OOP} with regime tags {owl-rl, horn-def, ufo-modal, validation, reference-only} and the CK-UFO four-valued contract ENTAILED/CONTRADICTED/UNDERDETERMINED/OUT-OF-PROFILE; UNDERDETERMINED and OUT-OF-PROFILE are NEVER converted into a negative answer or a rejection; missing-witness checks fire only inside declared closedFor scopes; anything outside the inventory refuses fail-closed (ERR_RULE_UNIMPLEMENTED).
rationale: Keeps 'the logic lacks facts' distinct from 'the commitment exceeds the profile' and blocks illegitimate closure — the two named modal-validation hazards.

claim: All checker decisions are PRE-MATERIALISED on CPU by the Python twin (gold + per item x arm x answer accept/reject tables + exact rejection-message bytes + trivial-policy floors) with a double-run canonical-JSON sha determinism proof (ERR_FIXTURES_PRECONDITION gates all GPU spend); the GPU runner does lookups only. NO sparq code is touched, built, or invoked on this path; twin<->sparq conformance is deliberately out of scope (PR-5 is separately gated).
rationale: Keeps the $5-20 rider independent of the L-band engine work and makes the checker deterministic and auditable.

claim: Five arms — A0 no-checker / AG gUFO-taxonomy-only / AU UFO-SN3 / AD deranged-meta-typing / AN representation-matched-null — over IDENTICAL prompt bytes; the first pass is greedy and SHARED across arms per (item, host), so pairing is exact by construction and seeds {0,1,2} govern retry sampling only. The only arm-varying surface is the rejection message, token-matched to AU's band (+/-20% at the pinned tokenizer; pre-freeze artifact + run-time gate).
rationale: Isolates checker CONTENT as the only manipulated variable; the shared first pass also cuts generation cost ~4x.

claim: AD = AU's rules over a seed-pinned Sattolo derangement (seed 20260712) of the meta-type assignment: a Sattolo CYCLE over the meta-label alphabet {kind, subkind, role, phase}, so every type's kind/role/phase label MOVES; dispositions and accept tables RE-DERIVED under the deranged assignment. The coincidence rate with the true tables is MEASURED at materialisation over the rejection-active scored slice (items with d_AU in {E,C}; gold-U items coincide trivially because neither arm ever rejects there) and gated <= 0.35 (ERR_AD_COINCIDENCE); a deterministic attempt-bump re-derangement fires otherwise and the attempt is recorded in fixtures-sha.json. Built value: attempt 0, active-slice coincidence 0.250 (all-scored 0.417, reported).
rationale: Content destruction at identical topology and token cost — the anti-circularity control from the derangement direction.

claim: AN = the representation-matched null (CK-UFO A1 fold-in): the checker sees the same materialised proposition/situation representation but applies NO UFO rules — reject ENTAILED unless the candidate is literally stated in-situation, reject CONTRADICTED unless its notHolds is literally stated, accept UNDERDETERMINED always (the GS-B stated-bytes projection family, inverted to the accept seam); rejection messages carry no rule content and are token-matched. MEASURED, not asserted (review fix): materialise.py emits the exact per-item and total node/edge/reifier/byte census of the representation each checker path consumes and refuses on any AU-vs-AN inequality (ERR_REP_MISMATCH; run-time gate /gates/an_representation_match), and gates AN accept/reject nondegeneracy — at least one accepted AND one rejected E answer and likewise for C over the scored slice (ERR_AN_DEGENERATE; run-time gate /gates/an_nondegenerate). Built census: 5391 nodes, 7851 edges, 1305 reifiers, 264250 bytes, identical for both consumers.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ufo-check-0.md section 5; docs/next/arch/full-ufo-kernel-ck-ufo.md section 3.3 arm A1; DECONF GS-B lineage
rationale: A lift over A0 that AN reproduces is attributable to 'a rejection channel over explicit structure', not UFO inference — the second anti-circularity direction.

claim: Retry budget k=1 with the licensed-rejection contract (d=E rejects {C,U}; d=C rejects {E,U}; d=U and d=OOP reject NOTHING). Rationale: the 3-valued answer space makes any k>=2 exact-oracle verify-retry gameable by pure answer elimination; at k=1 the trivial-policy floors (uniform-over-non-rejected / always-U / cycle-next) are analytic from the accept tables + actual first answers, and the content-over-bit contrast (s1) is well-posed. s1 failure is a CLAIM CAP ('rejection bit, not reason'), never a kill. k>1 cells are not run; if ever run they are quarantined exploratory.
rationale: Blocks the strongest trivial confound before it can masquerade as checker use.

claim: dangerous_wrong = final answer ENTAILED on gold CONTRADICTED, OR a hard answer (E or C) on gold UNDERDETERMINED. The safety secondary s5 is non-inferiority: one-sided UB95 of dangerous_wrong(AU) - dangerous_wrong(A0) < +0.02; failure is KILL-U2 (no-go regardless of accuracy).
backing_ref: docs/next/design/ufo-check-0.md section 6; docs/next/arch/full-ufo-kernel-ck-ufo.md section 3.4 go rule
rationale: CK-UFO's 'no dangerous false-accept increase' go-rule component, made mechanical at this scope.

claim: Statistics: paired-item BCa bootstrap B=10000, PRNG seed 20260712, one-sided alpha=0.05; every secondary carries a bootstrap-INVERSION p-value (the smallest alpha at which its registered one-sided BCa decision rule fires; for s3 this inverts the registered conservative ratio rule UB_a(lift AD)/LB_a(lift AU) < 0.30 by bisection); HOLM step-down over {s1,s2,s3,s4,s5} with adjusted p <= 0.05 as the OPERATIVE pass for every secondary; the registered 95% bounds are additionally reported verbatim. The primary is standalone: decision = LB95 > 0, inversion p reported. Wilson bounds for rate gates. The n=600 power claim is resolved by the frozen simulation (PROPOSED-ASM-1494), and realized CI width is still reported at analysis. Two host rungs (135M, 360M) license a SIGN, not a slope.
rationale: Same statistical machinery as the frozen rules-1/knull-v2 records; nothing bespoke to audit.

claim: Cost band 2-6 GPU-h / $5-20 (single a10g, ~16k short generations incl. shared first pass and k=1 retries) scales from the nsk1/rules-1 datum (32,958 rows ~= 0.457 A10G-h ~= $0.50) with a ~3x planning margin for model loads, the 360M rung and checkpointing; caps usd 20 / 6 GPU-h / 12 h wall. Planning estimate, never a measurement; the dry-plan gate re-checks before spend. ENFORCED, not merely declared: fixture-tokenized dry-plan gate + in-run BudgetGuard hard stops at the registry caps (named ERR_BUDGET_* exits, partials flushed).
rationale: Keeps the rider inside the arch-synthesis Q6 ratified band.
resolution_path: Pre-launch: ufo_check0_runner.py --dry-plan (FIXTURE-TOKENIZED at the pinned tokenizer — built value 6,956,100 worst-case tokens => 0.886 GPU-h / $0.97 worst case at the 3x overhead factor) must be green vs the caps. In-run: BudgetGuard hard stops at usd_cap/gpu_hours_cap/wall_clock_cap (ERR_BUDGET_USD/ERR_BUDGET_GPU_HOURS/ERR_BUDGET_WALL_CLOCK), flushing completed cells. Post-run: the realized gpu_hours/usd_total ledger in the run sidecar resolves the band; re-tag on readout.

claim: Hosts: SmolLM2-135M-Instruct at the f2b-pinned revision (12fd25f77366fa6b3b4b768ec3050bf629380bac, carried verbatim) and SmolLM2-360M-Instruct with revision PINNED AT FREEZE (PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder in the DRAFT). Scorer/sampler: HFLM scorer + seeded retry sampler imported READ-ONLY from poc/f2b-transfer/runner/f2bt_runner.py at its pinned sha; poc/f2b*, poc/rules-1 and poc/knull trees are byte-untouched.
rationale: Maximum reuse of already-Modal-smoked machinery; the 360M rung is the only new pin.

claim: Role + gating discipline: this DRAFT was authored by the experiment-designer role, which never runs, grades or audits it; build (sections 8/10.2) and freeze (10.4-10.5) may proceed at ~$0 while the coordinator HOLDS the GPU run pending the maintainer's issue-#22 decision (KUFO/1 adoption + D2 + Q6); the run is the experiment-runner role's on Modal lane M1 (coordinator confirms the free lane at launch under the one-lane-per-account stipulation); grading is verdict-gen + analyst; audit is the cross-vendor Codex auditor. ASM rows 1480..1495 are registered centrally by the coordinator; the design bead wrote nothing to registry/assumptions.jsonl.

claim: Scope: families F-RIG/F-ANTI/F-DISJ/F-SPEC only; relators/qua-individuals, identity criteria, dispositions and events are NOT tested; the axioms-as-text null is NOT run at this scope (AN covers representation, not rule-text) — a disclosed limitation any promotion experiment must repair. Every claim is bounded by the section 7.4 envelope; the record is ORACLE-DIAGNOSTIC; no outcome licenses a feasibility conclusion on CORRECTNESS or EFFICIENCY (both INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING).
rationale: Names what the $5-20 rider does NOT buy so a PASS cannot be over-read.

claim: Power: the n=600 claim is demonstrated by the FROZEN seed-pinned simulation poc/ufo-check-0/power_sim.py (numpy default_rng(20260712), M=2000 datasets/scenario, the SAME BCa B=10000 statistic via the exact multinomial-over-value-classes equivalence), committed result poc/ufo-check-0/inputs/power-sim.json and pinned in pins.artifact_hashes. Registered decision rule: n=600 stands iff min primary power over the a0 grid {0.20,0.35,0.50,0.65,0.80} at the +0.05 margin >= 0.80. Result: min power 1.000 (the shared-first-pass paired diff is structurally one-sided under the licensed-rejection contract); type-I at zero true lift 0.000; s5 non-inferiority power >= 0.955 at zero true increase (0.824 at a true +0.01 increase, low churn — disclosed sensitivity). The simulation is a planning instrument, never a measurement of the run.
rationale: Cross-vendor review fix 1: an unsupported power stipulation cannot freeze; the simulation replaces it with a registered, reproducible artifact.

claim: Machine-enforced instrument gates (review fixes 4/5/6): (a) COMPLETENESS — the analysis terminally gates the exact item x arm x host x seed factorial (630 x 5 x 2 x 3 = 18,900 rows, no missing/duplicate/unbalanced rows, the fixed 600-item scored set per cell, verified against the fixtures' committed item-id shas) as /gates/completeness_valid; (b) REPRESENTATION CENSUS + AN NONDEGENERACY per PROPOSED-ASM-1486; (c) AD COINCIDENCE per PROPOSED-ASM-1485; (d) MESSAGE DISCIPLINE — twin_ufo.lint_message prohibits disposition labels, replacement answers and conclusion-equivalent text over EVERY rejection-message byte; materialise refuses at build (ERR_MESSAGE_DISCIPLINE), the runner re-lints the loaded fixture bytes before any generation, and the analysis carries /gates/rejection_message_clean. Any gate failure is INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never a hypothesis event; rejection AND retry rates are reported per arm.
rationale: The cross-vendor review required these asserted properties to be measured and gated by machine, not narrated.

claim: Round-2 arch-exploration transcription mapping: the workflow journal for run wf_3870703d-0bb carries no label field; agent outputs were mapped to the script's agent labels by structural schema match plus the name field inside each result, cross-checked against the persisted workflow script; the four extracted arch docs rest on this mapping.
