claim: The training corpus is re-rendered as rules-1-c ENTITY-form QA: relation cells ask 'Who is the <rel> of <base>?' with the object NAME as target and a per-cell 2-option anti-echo decode set (both names in the cell's context, base excluded); family-2 chain cells restrict their context to the derivation's stated support (proof leaves + support gender facts + UNA — the 3-name two-hop nsk1 item shape) because the entity question is 2-way ambiguous in a full 3-generation family; the unique-answer property is asserted per cell in the restricted closure and violations are skipped WITH COUNTS (0 in the real build); family-1/3 keep full component context; typing cells keep the man/woman 2-option form.
rationale: Entity-form uniqueness forces the support restriction ('Who is the grandmother of X?' has two true answers in a full family); the restriction reproduces exactly the eval item shape, keeping the train->eval form match that is the point of the rework. Mixed context sizes (family-1 full vs family-2 restricted) are disclosed in the corpus manifest.

claim: Eval decode is a uniform 3-option forced choice on every rules-2 arm: the cell's 2-option anti-echo set + the trained named refusal ('cannot be determined from these facts'), scored per-option-logprob after the rules-1-c direction-explicit entity infill cue; chance among the content options is 0.5, DISCLOSED; NO menu/option enumeration appears anywhere in any prompt; the refusal continuation is agrammatical after the entity cue — a marked continuation, identical across arms and across train/eval (family 3 trains exactly this surface), disclosed, not corrected.
rationale: Carries the rules-1-c no-enumeration contract and the rework-2 refusal-surface disclosure onto the entity form; like-for-like across every compared arm.

claim: c1p is re-operationalised for the 2-option entity surface as the FORCED FLIP: every family-2 train target is replaced by the item's other decode option (man<->woman for typing cells). shuffled_answer != original_answer holds for EVERY item BY CONSTRUCTION (11,411/11,411 in the real build); corpus size and token cost preserved up to name-length variation. The control is ANTI-CORRELATED — it trains the complement rule, a strictly stronger content destruction than a shuffle — and a negative c1p lift reads as recovery ~0 under the registered s1' ratio test (UB95 < 0.30, ceiling chance-floor-invariant).
rationale: At exactly 2 candidate options no cross-item label permutation is well-defined (targets are per-item family NAMES) and the only derangement is the flip; the anti-correlation and its interpretive limit are disclosed rather than hidden (see ASM-1806).

claim: The c8 train-bytes projection re-derived on entity keys ((base, rel) QA index + entity-directional fact-line index, no joins) recovers 0/858 S-out items (recovered_acc 0.0 <= ceiling 0.10, GATE PASS) on the REWORK-3 corpus; s_mem/s_held projections are stratified by kind with the entity-key ambiguity disclosure (chain (base, rel) keys AMBIG across support contexts => the s_mem lookup anchor is <1.0 on the entity form); c4 floors at chance 0.5: abstain-all 0.0, random-2-option 0.5, measured sorted-first-option incidence. The 0.10 ceiling is chance-floor-independent: it bounds lookup LEAKAGE, and eval surface names are token-disjoint from training names (fail-closed guard), so a lookup cannot emit an in-lexicon eval name at all.

Chance-0.5 re-derivations: the primary SESOI 0.05 and KILL-d (LB <= 0) carry UNCHANGED because both are paired-lift quantities, not absolute accuracies; G3 headroom stays acc(B0) <= 0.85 (expected plain-host floor ~0.70 per the rules-1-c A1 descriptive read 0.7028; 0.85 sits above the expected floor and leaves >= 3x SESOI of measurable headroom below saturation); the s1' 0.30 recovery ceiling is a lift ratio, chance-floor-invariant; c1p derangement re-derived per ASM-1803.
rationale: The pre-registered decision quantities are contrasts and ratios; only the absolute-accuracy floors (c4) and the saturation gate needed re-argument at the new chance floor, and both are re-derived rather than silently carried.
backing_ref: analysis/rules_2.py header + G3 comment; docs/next/design/rules-2-train-time.md Appendix B.4

claim: DESIGN-OPEN (registered, blocks freeze until reviewed): on the entity form the gold NAME is in-context and on 2-hop items gold = chain-top by construction, so a trained host could learn the positional 'pick the non-bridge name' shortcut (ceiling 1.0) instead of the inference. B1 (no family-2) does not teach it; c8 cannot see it (not a train-bytes lookup); and the ASM-1803 forced flip trains the complement rule, so a c1p collapse CANNOT separate content-driven lift from shortcut learning. This residual confound must be argued or gated through the GPT-5.6 review gate BEFORE the coordinator freezes rules-2.
rationale: Honest registration of the one confound the rework introduces and its controls only partially cover; deferring it silently would be a prereg lie.
resolution_path: GPT-5.6 review gate pre-freeze: either an argued bound (e.g. B1-vs-B2 shortcut-transfer analysis, bridge-mention statistics) or an added gate/stratum (e.g. items where the bridge heuristic disagrees with gold) registered before freeze

claim: Sequencing-gate correction (amends PROPOSED-ASM-1420): modal_rules2._launch_gates reads registry/verdicts/rules-1-c.json (a rules-1-b verdict will never exist — superseded pre-GPU 2026-07-12); ONLY a PASS verdict opens the gate automatically; every other branch — including the PREDICTED INSTRUMENT-INVALID from the landed rows — requires the maintainer's issue #24 host-integration slot decision (rules-1-d repair vs rules-2 as the replacement slot, B4/s3' fate included) and a record amendment re-registering the gate; KILL-b additionally requires explicit maintainer re-authorization with s3' struck. GPU is HELD pending that decision; this build performed NO freeze and NO run.
rationale: Fail-closed re-registration of an unsatisfiable gate onto the real predecessor, deferring every non-PASS branch to the maintainer exactly as the governance requires.

claim: B4 INHERITS THE RULES-1-C VERIFY-RETRY VACUITY (coordinator flag): on the landed rules-1-c campaign every A3 row has attempts=1 and A3 is correctness-identical to c1 on all 2574 item x seed cells — the verify-retry channel never fired. Mechanism, read from the imported bytes: twin_engine.Closure.query_relation returns the relation WORD, but licensed_rejection (rules1_runner.py:342) tests urn2word.get(payload.answer) != rel_word — a URN-keyed lookup on a word — so the verifier abstains unconditionally BEFORE any acceptance ground is consulted. B4 imports these bytes byte-identically (frozen, not editable here), so B4 degenerates to attempt-0 entity rows and its 3 seeds are expected byte-identical. Consequences: s3' is conditional on a positive rules-1-c primary (ASM-1428) and DROPS OUT of the Holm family under the predicted branch (B4 rows descriptive only); the B0-B5 train-time internalisation arms do NOT use the verify-retry channel and are UNAFFECTED. Repair (word-comparison fix + an acceptance ground with teeth at 2 options) belongs to rules-1-d under the issue #24 decision.
backing_ref: poc/rules-1/results-incoming/20260712-142704-rules1b-parallel/ (RUNNER_EXIT rc=0, 13,470 rows, all A3 attempts=1); poc/rules-1/rules1_runner.py sha256 91d780f344019f5948533973348873aadeb9803f85695abc5854198b690a23ab lines 338-345; poc/rules-1/twin_engine.py query_relation (returns (word, why))

claim: IP-1 (separation gate) operationalises 'the eval surface separates a trained host from B0' as four mechanical conditions on n=60 sha-ordered pilot S-out covered items scored on the uniform 3-option entity decode with a B2-pilot LoRA (deterministic family-stratified 6000-example subsample of the pinned corpus, pinned HPs verbatim, seed 0): (a) acc_B0 <= 0.90 (headroom exists; not both saturated), (b) acc_B2pilot >= the exact one-sided binomial 95% bound vs chance 0.5 at the pilot n (37/60 = 0.6167; not both floored), (c) acc_B2pilot - acc_B0 >= 0.05 (the contrast moves), (d) covered-refusal rate <= 0.5 on both arms (the channel is answers, not abstention). All four are required; thresholds are pre-declared constants in instr_pilot.py and changing any of them after a pilot result has been read requires a fresh pilot.
rationale: The named risk is the rules-1-c degeneracy recurring on the 2-option surface: both arms floored at chance 0.5 (training signal cannot show) or both saturated (no headroom for the campaign contrast). The exact-binomial floor bound makes 'above chance' a mechanical statement at the pilot n rather than an eyeballed one.
backing_ref: poc/rules-2/instr_pilot.py IP1_* constants + binom_min_k; correctness assessment SS2.2 condition (i)

claim: IP-2 (audit-teeth gate) requires the c8 train-bytes projection to be shown NON-VACUOUS at the operating point: (a) the REAL flat lookup over the pinned B2 training bytes recovers <= the pinned ceiling (0.10) on the pilot S-out slice (re-confirming c8's 0/858 on the exercised items), AND (b) a PLANTED exploiter — the pilot items' (base-name, relation-word) -> gold entity keys injected into the QA index, exactly the shortcut the audit exists to catch — pushes recovered accuracy above the ceiling with recovery >= 0.90, i.e. the gate predicate FIRES. The projection semantics (ent_key / qa+factline indexes / AMBIG rule) are duplicated verbatim from materialise_closure.c8 rather than imported from its nested closure — the same duplication precedent as materialise_closure.query_pair_item vs rules1_runner.query_pair — and drift is caught by the runner's pinned c8ResultSha256 verification. Both halves are $0 CPU and run before any GPU second is spent.
rationale: A shortcut audit that cannot fire is not a gate (the assessment's condition (ii): 'the shortcut audit actually fires on a planted shortcut exploiter'). The planted recovery lands at 0.9167 (55/60), not 1.0, because the small eval name pool reuses (base, rel) keys across items and the collisions are honestly AMBIG — disclosed, above the 0.90 teeth threshold. The 'pick-the-non-bridge-name' structural shortcut remains OUT OF SCOPE for IP-2, exactly as it is for c8: it is the registered DESIGN-OPEN ASM-1806 for the pre-freeze review.

claim: IP-3 (c1p control gate) operationalises 'the shuffled-derivation control is non-degenerate' at the 2-option forced-flip re-operationalisation (ASM-1803): the c1p-pilot LoRA (same subsample, same pinned HPs, seed 0) must show (a) covered-refusal rate <= 0.5 (no collapse to abstention) and (b) acc_c1p <= acc_B2pilot - 0.05 (no collapse onto the treatment). IP-3 is meaningful only conditional on a live IP-1 channel: when IP-1 is degenerate the paired gap is noise-dominated, so the pinned mock-validation asserts the degenerate-stub PILOT-FAIL through IP-1, not IP-3.
rationale: The assessment's condition (iii) verbatim ('its arm neither collapses to abstention nor to the treatment'). The anti-correlated forced-flip construction predicts c1p at or below the B0 floor; landing WITHIN 0.05 of the treatment would mean the derangement control cannot discharge its s1' role and the freeze must wait for a re-operationalisation.
backing_ref: poc/rules-2/instr_pilot.py IP3_* constants; PROPOSED-ASM-1803/1426; correctness assessment SS2.2 condition (iii)

claim: IP-4 (B4 vacuity flag) runs the imported rules-1-c A3 verify-retry loop (k=4, twin engine at inference, byte-identical drivers) on 24 pilot items and sets b4_vacuous=true iff every emitted row has attempts==1 — the measured rules-1-c vacuity (ASM-1808) inherited at the operating point. The flag downgrades the verdict to PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS but is NOT a pilot FAIL: s3' is already conditional (ASM-1428) and the B4/s3' disposition belongs to the maintainer's issue #24; the pilot's job is to MEASURE the inheritance rather than argue it. The flag firing in mock (where the stub host goes through the REAL imported licensed_rejection) is the detection channel working, never a measurement.
rationale: B4 is the one rules-2 arm that imports the failed verify-retry interface; the assessment demands its vacuity be flagged by an exercised channel, not inferred from the rules-1-c readout alone. Expected outcome at pilot time: flag FIRES (attempts=[1]), confirming ASM-1808 on rules-2's own instrument, which the coordinator carries into the issue-#24 decision.
backing_ref: poc/rules-2/instr_pilot.py IP-4; PROPOSED-ASM-1808/1428; poc/rules-1/rules1_runner.py licensed_rejection (line ~342 urn2word comparison)

claim: Pilot scale and licence limits, disclosed: (a) the pilot trains on a deterministic sha-ordered family-stratified subsample (6000 of 19931 train examples, ~30%) at the pinned HPs and ONE seed, so a pilot IP-1 PASS licenses only 'the instrument has dynamic range at the operating point' — it is NEVER a preview of the campaign effect size, direction, or significance, and no pilot number may be quoted as evidence for any registered hypothesis; (b) an IP-1 FAIL at pilot scale could in principle reflect under-training rather than surface degeneracy — the fail-closed policy is that EITHER reading blocks the freeze until resolved (redesign, a larger pilot, or an explicit coordinator override record naming the risk accepted); (c) worst-case pilot cost is capped fail-closed at $2 (PILOT_USD_CAP, enforced by --dry-plan exit code and the wrapper's launch gate; planned worst $0.27 on A10G at build time); (d) the pilot S-out/B4 slices (60/24 sha-ordered covered items + 10 control) overlap the campaign's S-out stratum by construction — acceptable because the pilot result gates the instrument, not the hypothesis, and eval items are never trained on in either.
rationale: Keeps the pilot honest in both directions: cheap enough to always run (the assessment's 'insurance the programme declined to buy'), and epistemically fenced so its numbers cannot leak into the campaign's evidence base.
backing_ref: poc/rules-2/instr_pilot.py pre-declared constants + verdict_semantics field; poc/rules-2/results/instrpilot-mock-validation.json dry_plan check

claim: g2-import-v2 two-pilot disagreement structure (pilot-20260712-ac1 AC1_A3 0.6909 table 29/2/6/3; real-20260712-auth AC1_A3 0.6222 table 28/1/7/4, kappa 0.0000, AC1 = its own independence ceiling to machine precision): the disagreement core is a FIXED 9-item set persisting in both pilots — pA-yes/pB-no on g2:pi:016,021,025,033,064,089 (6 items); pA-no/pB-yes on g2:pi:036,037,070 (3 items). judge-pB flipped 0/40 items between pilots; judge-pA flipped 2/40 (g2:pi:068 no->yes, g2:pi:071 yes->no), and those two pA flips are exactly the 31/40 -> 29/40 agreement drop that moved AC1 across the 0.65 gate. At pilot-#2 marginals AC1 >= 0.65 <=> >= 30/40 agreements; one converted disagreement passes.
rationale: Item-level diff of the four committed pilot response files; makes PROPOSED-ASM-1690's located channel item-precise and shows the gate-straddle is pA noise atop a stable one-sided pB-strict core.

claim: The pB hedge-strictness channel localises to MULTI-CLAIM hedged composites, not to hedges as such: both judges are 12/12 semantically correct on the repaired simple-hedge calibration set in both pilots (including the world-typicality anchor 'Normally a chair has legs'), while all 9 core-disagreement items carry hedged sentences with linked details (crude census over the 40 pilot items: agreed-yes items average 0.96 linked details per hedged sentence, n=28; disagreement items ~1.7, n=11). The signature features are multi-claim bundles, tacked-on contrasts ('— not before'), elliptical hedged fragments ('Normally a person speaking or writing.'), and embedded second-order qualifiers ('often'); the 3-item pA-no/pB-yes mirror set carries concessive widenings ('or at least', 'But Y can also be'), showing the ambiguity cuts BOTH directions.
rationale: Discriminates the instrument ambiguity (hedge scope over compound sentences, under-determined by the v2 rubric text) from a blanket pB hedge defect; grounds the symmetric repair.

claim: v2.2 rubric refinement (prompt-template-v2.2.txt sha 60d3403721135410b51177545e637f8c12e44e5f18f4ccf8c2ade867dc4cfde1): the v2 template is byte-carried except ONE added bullet — 'A hedged sentence may link several details together — with dashes, commas, "or", "but", or a tacked-on contrast (like "— not before"). The hedge covers the WHOLE sentence: judge it as ONE picture of the usual case, true if that picture, taken as a whole, is right about the usual run of cases, and false if the picture as a whole misdescribes the usual case. Do NOT split a hedged sentence into parts and demand that each detail hold separately or hold always; and do NOT read any part of a hedged sentence (including a tacked-on contrast) as an unhedged ALL-cases claim. A hedged sentence written without its own subject (for example "Normally with a key.") continues the topic of the sentence before it, still at hedged strength.' — plus the mirroring clause in the no-instruction ('and never merely because one linked detail inside a hedged sentence is not, on its own, the usual case, when the sentence's whole picture of the usual case is right'). Unhedged sentences keep the ALL-normal-cases standard verbatim; the change is a no-op on the hedge-free baseline/A1.
rationale: Sentence adverbs take clausal scope in ordinary English; the clarification fixes the rubric to what the sentences already meant. Symmetric: the same whole-sentence standard blocks per-part splitting (the 6-item pB channel) AND keeps whole-picture falsity fully in force (the 3-item mirror channel; cal:hedge-8 anchor).

claim: v2.2 calibration extension (calibration-hedge-v22.jsonl sha 63c247a9e7e7edc8769739753ef3b4c33b502f1b93dd01e12e6a948a4f97f57f): the 6 repaired v2 hedge-calibration items byte-carried + 2 new COMPOSITE-hedge known-answer anchors exercising the new bullet at the operating point, one per failure direction — cal:hedge-7 (umbrella, expected yes: whole picture right though linked details vary; elliptical + dashes + tacked-on contrast) and cal:hedge-8 (bicycle 'Normally ridden indoors', expected no: whole picture misdescribes the usual case though some linked details are true — guards against gestalt-leniency). Cal gates become 10/10 per judge at preflight (2 v1 + 8 hedge) and 16/16 across judges at the pilot. Hedge-flip probe families untouched: the whole-sentence reading rejects their absurd flips as decisively as the per-part reading.
rationale: The exercised-not-argued lesson (five instrument failures at untested interfaces): the new rubric line gets prevalence-free known-answer coverage in BOTH directions before any real item is scored.

claim: MCP-leak calibration artifact: pilot-20260712-ac1 failed its 12/12 cal gate (11/12) solely because a claude.ai MCP connector nondeterministically attached to 1/62 pB headless sessions despite --tools '' --setting-sources '', tripping the fail-closed tools==[] contract on a semantically correct answer (cal:hedge-6, raw '{"answer": "no"}'; 5/5 diagnostic repeats clean). Two-part repair implemented in commit bf75ff84 and NOW documented (closing the dangling 'section 11.8' reference): (i) --strict-mcp-config on every headless claude invocation, validator unchanged; (ii) calibration retry symmetry — the cal block goes through the same process_item retry ladder as real/probe items (it was the only single-attempt channel, so one 1/62-rate process artifact could fail the gate); content contract unchanged (first valid answer final). PIN-DRIFT DISCLOSURE: the frozen record pins run-ontg2v2.py ce2ab5a6... but sanctioned pilot #2 ran the hardened cd8e2f89... (committed 13:56Z, post-freeze 13:20:02Z) with no ops amendment — the sanctioned pilot ran off-pin.
rationale: The infra artifact and the pin drift must be on the record before any successor freeze; the drift is an independent reason the frozen record cannot be re-entered by amendment.

claim: PRE-COMMITMENT (recorded before any v2.2 judge call; to be carried as a verdict-bearing clause of the successor record): if the v2.2 Stage-P pilot's Gwet AC1_A3 on the 40 pinned items is < 0.65 — a SECOND sanctioned AC1 pilot failure for this record family — the GPT-5.6-Sol + Haiku-4.5 proxy pair is RETIRED as an adoption-arm (A3) instrument for the g2-import family: no further proxy-pair rubric iteration may be funded for A3, and the adoption decision passes to the two-human adjudicated panel on the 84 slots — the envelope's sole authority for permanent adoption, under which the v1 primary signal holds on every label bracket including pair-concordant 42/84 >= 34/84. Retirement binds the proxy pair's adoption-arm authority only: no conclusion about H-SOFT/H-SRC/H-SEP in either direction, no retirement for unhedged arms or other families, no binding of the human panel. A PASS licenses exactly the pre-registered ~$5-6 full 796-call run under the successor record, nothing else. Rider verbatim: PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; same 84 self-authored kernel-v0 slots; point-estimate engineering gate, not statistical superiority; soft non-binding typing only — never hard laws; no feasibility conclusion.
rationale: Implements the instrument assessment's closeability call ('fund exactly one v2.2 iteration... with a pre-commitment that a second AC1 failure retires the proxy-pair instrument'); either branch terminates the proxy iteration loop and protects against rubric-iteration shopping.

claim: Mechanism recommendation: v2.2 requires a SUCCESSOR record (registry/experiments/g2-import-v2.2.json, kot-reg/2), NOT a pre-final amendment of the frozen g2-import-v2. Grounds: (i) the rubric is constitutive, not ops — frozen_sha256 covers pins.artifact_hashes including prompt-template-v2.txt, and the only amendment channel the record sanctions is the harness_manifest banner fill; (ii) the Stage-P gate has FIRED — the sanctioned pilot mechanically returned INSTRUMENT-INVALID (pilot AC1 channel) with a pilot-only result assembled, and re-entering Stage-P under the same id would put two authoritative pilot outcomes under one frozen record (a gate-shopping surface); (iii) the record's own verdict-name mapping prescribes 'repair and mint a new frozen run', and design section 11.7's disjunction resolves to 'a new record after' freeze; (iv) v1 -> v2 precedent (same estimand, new record). The successor inherits the estimand identity chain and ALL v2 gates verbatim (pilot AC1_A3 >= 0.65 on the SAME 40 pinned items, decisive >= 36/40, hedge-flip <= 2/8, full-run gates), quarantines both prior pilots as instrument evidence, and pins the v2.2 instrument files + harness. NOT frozen by this build; coordinator action.
rationale: Preserves prereg discipline while enabling the one bounded repair; both standing off-record facts (pin drift, section-11.8 gap) are lawfully absorbed at the successor freeze instead of compounding.

claim: v2.2 call/cost arithmetic: preflight (2 v1-cal + 8 hedge-cal) x 2 = 20; Stage-P pilot (8 cal + 40 real + 8 flip) x 2 = 112; pilot stage total 132 calls ~= $1.06 at the v1-measured <= $0.008/call, <= $1.58 at the pinned conservative $0.012 bound (the ~$1.4 shape preserved). Full-campaign ceiling rises 788 -> 796 (+4 preflight cal + 4 pilot cal); bound-implied worst case 796 x $0.012 = $9.552 <= the $10 hard stop, enforced by the unchanged live dollar/call abort. Pilot operating characteristics unchanged (same 40 items, same AC1 gate, pilot-manifest.json OC block carries over). DISCLOSED RISK (v1->v2 lesson): the whole-sentence reading may shift prevalence further yes-ward from pi ~ 0.84, where every pair coefficient discriminates weakly; the burden-shifted known-answer channels remain load-bearing on an equal footing with AC1, and ASM-1825 bounds the family to this ONE further iteration.
rationale: Sizes the tripwire and the ceiling before the coordinator commits the ~$1.4; licenses nothing.
resolution_path: Resolved by the sanctioned v2.2 pilot AC1_A3 measurement (and, on a second fail, by the two-human panel per ASM-1825); the prevalence-shift risk is re-measured on the fresh pilot contingency table.

claim: v2.2 build pass 2026-07-12, $0 (mock only, zero judge calls): run-ontg2v2.py extended with the flag-guarded --rubric v22 path (v22 pins added to the fail-closed pin set; PILOT_CAL_MIN 12 -> 16 and BUDGET_MAX_CALLS 788 -> 796 under the flag only; rubric recorded in preflight-status/pilot-status; rubric-mix guards abort any run mixing v2 and v22 phases), new sha efe134ad0a51c9b49d2ea93d1f2e3341cfcd6a945cfcf492bd9a7e621d1d18e9 — to be pinned by the successor record with both judge CLI banners at its ops amendment. Validation: all five mock verdict paths GREEN under --rubric v22 (go PASS / nogo FAIL / instrument INSTRUMENT-INVALID / pilotfail INSTRUMENT-INVALID with pilot_valid=false / breadth FAIL with breadth_confound=true; runs/mockcheck-v22-20260712, pilot-status rubric=v22, cal 16/16); default-path regression GREEN with rubric=v2 and cal 12/12 (runs/mockcheck-v2default-20260712); rubric-mix guard exercised directly (ERR_ONTG2V2_RUBRIC abort); verify_pins green over the v22 pin set. All frozen v2 files byte-identical: prompt-template-v2.txt 6ba9a6f4..., calibration-hedge.jsonl f8d4e03b..., pilot-manifest.json e4ac7c95..., design doc 5829fad9....
rationale: Green-mock-before-freeze discipline; records exactly what changed on disk and that nothing frozen moved.

claim: THE RULE, programme-wide: no experiment may prereg-freeze until a RECORDED blocking instrument pilot has PASSED at the operating point. Recorded = a pinned kot-pilot/1 artifact whose sha256 the DRAFT record carries in pins.blocking_pilot before the freeze is attempted; PASSED = verdict PILOT-PASS or PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS with every required generic check (ASM-1831) mechanically true; at the operating point = the pilot exercised the exact pinned configuration the campaign will run (same model revisions, sha-pinned prompt surface, same item construction, same gate thresholds) — a pilot at drifted pins is STALE and satisfies nothing. A PILOT-FAIL blocks the FREEZE (the spend), never pilot-scale exploration itself; the only escape is the explicit coordinator override record of ASM-1833. Applies to every kot-reg/1 and kot-reg/2 record frozen after adoption; already-frozen records are untouched. Reconciliation with the 2026-07-09 maintainer philosophy ('stop false conclusions, not experiments'): an invalid instrument cannot run the experiment in any epistemically meaningful sense — it can only burn budget and emit rows no verdict may consume — so this gate blocks waste, not inquiry, is itself cheap (capped ~$2) and fast (hours), and open-EXTRAPOLATION premises remain PAUSE-not-refuse exactly as today.
rationale: Five instrument events (rules-1 VOID, rules-1-b form-dead, rules-1-c INSTRUMENT-INVALID, g2 v2 pilots #1/#2) share one process gap: no gate channel was exercised at the operating point before freeze. The correctness assessment names this gate the cheapest insurance the programme has repeatedly declined to buy and then paid for, and weights adopting it above any single next-experiment ranking entry.
backing_ref: docs/next/protocol/blocking-pilot-before-freeze.md SS1; docs/next/analysis/correctness-track-instrument-assessment.md SS2.2 + SS4 item 1 + meta-recommendation; poc/rules-2/instr_pilot.py (exemplar); docs/next/analysis/rules1c-instrument-invalid-interpretation.md

claim: The five generic pilot checks PC-1..PC-5, each MECHANICAL (predicate over emitted pilot rows against pre-declared constants; no model judgement), FAIL-CLOSED (absence of evidence = FAIL), and EXERCISED at the operating point: PC-1 NO DEGENERATE ARM — every measurement arm clears an exact-binomial (or equivalent) bound above its structural floor AND the strongest arm sits below a pre-declared saturation ceiling, with abstention/refusal bounded on every arm; PC-2 SEPARATION NON-VACUOUS — the treatment channel moves relative to its baseline by a pre-declared minimum gap and is not row-identical to a control (the rules-1-c A3==c1 vacuity); PC-3 CONTROLS NON-DEGENERATE — every mandatory control lands where its construction predicts (no abstention collapse, no collapse onto the treatment) AND every gating statistic clears its own degeneracy floor (chance rate; independence ceiling for agreement statistics) with a margin GREATER than its observed replicate noise at pilot n — a gate passing inside its own noise band is flagged, never silently passed; PC-4 VALIDITY GATES HAVE TEETH — for every in-run validity gate, a PLANTED violation (injected into a copy of the pilot inputs, never campaign bytes) must FIRE the gate predicate while the un-planted run stays under the ceiling, and any engagement channel must show at least one non-trivial engagement on the plant (attempts>1); PC-5 GOLD STRUCTURALLY ELICITABLE — a scripted oracle arm scores near ceiling under the pinned parser/scorer and the pilot host emits parseable output at bounded failure rate (all-arms-zero-including-oracle = dead surface, not a null). Thresholds are pre-declared runner constants; changing any after a coordinator has read a pilot result requires a fresh pilot. A check may be not_applicable only with a written structural reason, which downgrades the verdict to PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS.
rationale: Generalises the rules-2 exemplar gates (IP-1 -> PC-1/PC-2, IP-3 -> PC-3, IP-2/IP-4 -> PC-4, host-validity floors/oracle -> PC-5) and adds the margin-vs-replicate-noise clause of PC-3, which is exactly what the g2 AC1 pilot #1 near-miss (0.6909 vs gate 0.65, +/-2-item run noise spanning the margin) shows a generic gate must additionally do.
backing_ref: docs/next/protocol/blocking-pilot-before-freeze.md SS2; poc/rules-2/instr_pilot.py IP-1..IP-4 + asm-instrpilot-1814-1819.json; correctness-track-instrument-assessment.md SS0.1 + SS2.1

claim: The pilot-pass artifact contract kot-pilot/1: one committed canonical-JSON file (convention poc/<exp>/results/instrpilot-result.json) pinned from the DRAFT record as pins.blocking_pilot={path,sha256}, carrying: schema_version kot-pilot/1; experiment (MUST equal the record id — a predecessor's pilot never carries across ids); mode REAL (MOCK artifacts validate pilot mechanics only and never satisfy the gate); verdict PILOT-PASS|PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS|PILOT-FAIL with instrument-validity-only verdict_semantics; a checks block keyed by the five generic names (no_degenerate_arm, separation_nonvacuous, controls_nondegenerate, gate_teeth, elicitable_gold), each {pass:true, evidence:{...}} or {not_applicable:true, reason}; enumerated flags; operating_point{description, n_pilot, seed, pins{model_revisions, harness/corpus shas, prompt_surface_sha256}} whose pins MUST byte-equal the record's same-named pins; thresholds_predeclared; runner {path,sha256}; records_file/records_sha256 (raw per-row evidence); cost with a fail-closed worst-case cap (default $2, per-experiment override with justification); date; pseudonymous emitted_by (RT-14). Immutable once read by a coordinator: iteration means a fresh pilot run and fresh artifact. Evidence fence: no artifact number may be cited for or against any registered hypothesis; a PASS licenses exactly one sentence — 'the instrument has dynamic range at the operating point'.
rationale: The freeze tool needs a mechanically checkable object, not a narrative; the contract makes the pilot's verdict, identity, operating point, and cost verifiable by sha and predicate, and reuses the shape instr_pilot.py already emits (a superset needing only schema_version/checks naming alignment).
backing_ref: docs/next/protocol/blocking-pilot-before-freeze.md SS3; poc/rules-2/instr_pilot.py result-JSON writer; registry/schema/kot-reg-2.json pins block

claim: The coordinator override path, for pilots that are genuinely INFEASIBLE (not inconvenient): a top-level blocking_pilot_override={correction_id, path, sha256, justification_verbatim, approved_by} on the DRAFT record, pointing at a committed correction record whose justification names, check by check against PC-1..PC-5, what is waived, why no pilot (or partial pilot, always preferred) can exist at this operating point, which degeneracy risks are accepted unexercised, and what post-hoc check substitutes for each. The freeze then proceeds but the tool appends a permanent non-fatal kind=PILOT-OVERRIDE flag line to registry/pause-flags.jsonl, threads the waiver into the freeze summary, and verdict-gen plus the cross-vendor auditor see the flag — an override is never silent. Maintainer signoff is additionally required whenever the record already needs Tier-5 signoff.
rationale: Fail-closed gates without an explicit escape get worked around informally; an on-record override with enumerated accepted risks preserves coordinator agency while keeping the waived gate permanently visible in provenance, mirroring the existing correction-record discipline (c-2026-07-08 precedent).

claim: The prereg-freeze wiring is PROPOSED, not applied, by this build: (a) schema additions — an OPTIONAL pins.blocking_pilot {path, sha256} property in kot-reg-1.json and kot-reg-2.json (necessary because both schemas pin pins with additionalProperties:false, so the field cannot be added without a schema edit; optional, so legacy records validate unchanged and no frozen bytes move) plus an optional top-level blocking_pilot_override object; (b) a check_blocking_pilot(record, root) called inside check_record AFTER the P-6 pin checks and BEFORE the D9 reuse machinery, with five named fail-closed refusals — ERR_P2_PILOT_MISSING (neither pin nor override), ERR_P2_PILOT_PIN (artifact/correction bytes missing or sha mismatch), ERR_P2_PILOT_SCHEMA (not kot-pilot/1, wrong experiment id, or mode!=REAL), ERR_P2_PILOT_FAIL (verdict PILOT-FAIL or a required check neither pass:true nor explicit not_applicable-with-reason), ERR_P2_PILOT_STALE (an operating_point.pins entry disagrees with the record's same-named pin) — and an OVERRIDE branch that verifies the correction record's sha and returns a waiver flag for the pause-flags plumbing; (c) --dry-run exercises the gate identically; (d) fixture tests one-per-named-error mirroring the ERR_P2_SCALE_LANGUAGE test discipline. Full pseudocode in the protocol doc SS5.3; the coordinator applies, tests, and commits. First consumer: the rules-2 DRAFT record, whose runner needs only a checks-naming alias onto its existing gates block.
rationale: Role separation: this governance-design build may not edit the freeze tool; a diff-shaped proposal with named error codes, a fixed call site, and a test plan lets the coordinator apply it as a reviewable mechanical change rather than a re-design.
backing_ref: docs/next/protocol/blocking-pilot-before-freeze.md SS5.1-5.4; tools/registry/prereg-freeze.py check_record + docstring error table; registry/schema/kot-reg-1.json + kot-reg-2.json pins.additionalProperties

claim: Licence limits of the gate, disclosed: (a) a pilot PASS licenses ONLY 'the instrument has dynamic range at the operating point' — never a preview of campaign effect size, direction, or significance, and no pilot number may enter any evidence base (the ASM-1819(a) fence, made programme-wide); (b) a pilot FAIL at pilot scale can in principle reflect pilot-scale limitations (e.g. under-training on a subsample) rather than surface degeneracy — the fail-closed policy is that EITHER reading blocks the freeze until resolved by redesign, a larger pilot, or an ASM-1833 override naming the accepted risk; (c) the five checks are a FLOOR, not a ceiling: they encode the failure modes the programme has already paid for (elicitation death, form death, verifier vacuity, boundary-sitting gate statistics), and a genuinely novel sixth failure shape may pass all five — the correct response then is a protocol amendment adding a PC row, never a claim that the gate was sufficient; (d) the gate adds a small fixed cost (~$2 + hours) and one freeze-cycle latency to every experiment, accepted deliberately against the measured alternative (three consumed campaigns in one lane).
rationale: Keeps the gate honest in both directions: epistemically fenced so pilot rows cannot leak into evidence, and scoped so a green gate is never read as a validity guarantee beyond the five exercised channels.
backing_ref: docs/next/protocol/blocking-pilot-before-freeze.md SS2 + SS6 coverage-honesty note; poc/rules-2/asm-instrpilot-1814-1819.json ASM-1819

claim: The failure-to-check mapping (protocol doc SS6) is a COUNTERFACTUAL: had a blocking pilot with PC-1..PC-5 been run honestly at each as-frozen operating point, rules-1's VOID would have been caught by PC-5 (the scripted oracle arm scored 0.000 — a dead elicitation surface fails in minutes) with PC-1 failing simultaneously on every arm; rules-1-b's host-frame death by PC-1 (the unaided host at 0/24 cannot clear the exact-binomial floor vs chance at pilot n); rules-1-c's verify-retry vacuity by PC-4 (a planted violation produces no attempts>1 row — the engagement gate provably cannot fire; the same signature IP-4's b4_vacuous predicate detects on the inherited arm today) corroborated by PC-2 (A3 row-identical to c1 on the pilot slice); and the g2 AC1 pilot-#1 near-miss would have been FLAGGED (not failed) by PC-3's margin-vs-replicate-noise clause (clearance over the independence ceiling smaller than the +/-2-item run-to-run movement at n=40). This mapping is motivation for the rule, never evidence of the gate's sufficiency, and rests on the assumption that each pilot would have been designed without inheriting the same blind spot it was meant to catch.
rationale: Stating the mapping as an open EXTRAPOLATION keeps the protocol's own epistemics clean: the catch claims are what-ifs over measured failure signatures, not measurements of the gate's performance. It is deliberately non-load-bearing — the rule (ASM-1830) stands on the measured process gap alone.
resolution_path: Prospective, never retrospective: each future freeze that runs the gate updates the mapping — a pilot that catches a would-have-frozen degeneracy is a confirming instance; an INSTRUMENT-INVALID verdict on a campaign whose pilot PASSED is a disconfirming instance and triggers the ASM-1835(c) protocol amendment. Track both counts in this entry's notes at each occurrence; the entry can close only by supersession, since the historical counterfactuals themselves are not resolvable.

claim: Feasibility-synthesis-v6 CORRECTNESS state: INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING. Licensed constituents: verdict-grade audit-confirmed authored-content lift (f2b-transfer +0.2507, endorsement 320/333; DECONF-B delta_align +0.2697, GS-A-kernel identity 1.0 — value in item-aligned content, no kernel-specific runtime structure measured) and the RULES-1 CPU certificate (engine 858/858 vs third-party gold, stated/entailed C_dec 1.0/0.0 — machinery non-inertness only). Open and UNINSTRUMENTED, not killed: kernel-specific structure (knull ablation never run), engine-verify-retry host lift (three instrument failures culminating in rules-1-c INSTRUMENT-INVALID; no valid carrier at the current operating point), typing soundness (g2/g2-import/g2-import-v2 all instrument-invalid or pilot-stopped; human panel pending). No programme feasibility conclusion follows from any constituent.
rationale: Records the post-v5 correctness state without converting content or certificate results into thesis verdicts, and fixes the v6 discipline that uninstrumented affirmative quantities are UNMEASURED in both directions, never 'adverse' and never 'killed'.

claim: Feasibility-synthesis-v6 EFFICIENCY state: INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING on ZERO live data — no experiment whose primary endpoint is an efficiency quantity has ever produced a valid grade (noninferiority_vs_r3=false is a not-shown, not a datum for; CASC-0-prime instrument-invalid; rules-1-c s3 unevaluable). DDC T0 is in flight as the thesis's first live execution. First-datum claim caps, fixed before any number exists: ddc0 licenses only admission/non-admission of kernel-aligned directions (gates ddc1's aligned arm; no ability/efficiency/correctness claim); ddc1 at most 'kernel-guided direction choice beats magnitude/random at matched sparsity on SmolLM2-135M/360M BASE, public benchmarks, training-free' — sign not slope; a TOST-equivalent ddc1 is a valid adverse datum at that scope only. Under every DDC branch, parity vs a larger host and lifecycle economics remain separate unaddressed legs and EFFICIENCY remains short of a thesis verdict.
rationale: Prevents the first efficiency datum, whichever way it lands, from being reported beyond its pre-registered scope, and makes the zero-data status of the thesis explicit rather than implied.

claim: V6 reading discipline for the instrument ledger: the correctness gap is treated as an INSTRUMENTATION gap before an evidence gap. The assessment's explanatory weighting (scale-artifact 45% / design-space 40% / genuinely-hard 15%) is carried as SUBJECTIVE opinion, binding on nothing and never a premise. Promotion rule, fixed in advance: if the deflationary/affirmative asymmetry persists through VALID instruments (a pilot-validated rules-2 campaign and a human-gold g2 panel), the genuinely-hard reading is promoted to primary in the next synthesis; a single valid affirmative result instead re-opens the attribution chain (knull analog mandatory before any kernel-specific wording).
rationale: Pre-commits how the next valid results update the framing, so the synthesis verdict wording cannot drift opportunistically after outcomes are known.

claim: SCALE-1 S0 (10k rung) state as carried by v6: the deterministic import-vectoriser's crosstalk model holds at scale (disjoint-pair sigma within 3% of 1/sqrt(D) at D in {8192,512,576}; sqrt(2 ln m / D) a respected upper envelope at m=10^4; byte-determinism verified), and four measured blockers stand: WordNet-only typing yields 0% identity/dependence so the 0.95 UFO-typing gate is unreachable in principle on this source; exact O(m^2) cleanup is infeasible at 1M (~324 CPU-h) making the ANN + >=0.99 recall gate mandatory between 100k and 1M; the SemCor selection rule exhausts at 27,210; duplicate structural mass (20.1% of records, worsening down the tail) — not crosstalk — is the binding margin constraint. All figures scoped to kot-enc-import/*, never construction B. Thesis-relevant demonstrations remain at <100 concepts (84 g2 slots; closed kinship inventory; coverage 0.3542 at molecules-v0): the scale barrier-not-result backdrop stands.
rationale: Records that S0 qualified the substrate and named the machinery gaps, and that neither S0 nor S1 can by design yield thesis evidence.

claim: rules-1-d design identity and status: a PAPER DESIGN for a fourth inference-time verify-retry instrument whose surface is sibling-bridged CLUTRR k=3 kinship items on the measured-working ENTITY form, 3-option in-story forced choice (chance 1/3, disclosed), retry budget k=1 (= n-2), attempt-indexed scoring, surface-matched attempt-0 prompts, and an information-theoretic leak criterion (a rejection removes at most log2(3/2) bits of option-set entropy, asserted fail-closed per feedback line, lexical gold-token guard retained as backstop). claim: CLUTRR k=3 scoping facts, read from the sha-pinned EMNLP release zip (data/clutrr-cache/clutrr-emnlp-release.zip, archive_sha256 b4029f68...) this tick: (1) the CLEAN bundle data_089907f8 contains 5,103 k=3 rows across 321 distinct edge-type combos and ZERO combos composed only of {father, mother} — great-grandparent is not in CLUTRR's 23-relation vocabulary, so pure 3-up chains are never generated and the naive depth-3 great-grandparent route has NO third-party gold. (2) The sibling-bridged route (exactly one {brother,sister} edge plus two {father,mother} edges, target in {grandfather, grandmother}; 16 combos) IS covered with third-party proof_state gold: data_089907f8 245 coverable / 184 gender-competitive items; data_db9b8f04 248 / 186; approx 493 / 370 pre-dedup. Counts are one-shot pre-dedup reads; the builder must re-derive them under pinned code.

claim: Non-vacuity arithmetic at the rules-1-d operating point: at n=3 a maximally informative licensed rejection (naming the rejected proposal, never the gold) removes log2(3/2) ~= 0.585 of log2(3) ~= 1.585 bits, leaving genuine residual uncertainty — non-leaking and informative are jointly satisfiable, dissolving the n=2 leak-vacuity dilemma; at k=1 <= n-2 acceptance cannot be reached by exhaustion and a feedback-blind resampler's final-accuracy ceiling is p0 + (1-p0)/2 < 1, so oracle filtering is bounded away from a manufactured PASS; the c7 matched-budget uninformative-rejection arm makes filtered-vs-taught separable, demoting the abstaining c1 from load-bearing separation duty to content-destruction duty. Both degeneracy directions of the interactive arm are gated: never-engages (engagement gate) and always-decides (decisive/oracle-capture flag).
rationale: Fixes the arithmetic answer to the three rules-1-c boxes so the design's non-vacuity claim is a stated derivation, not an argued property.

claim: Rule-LANGUAGE sufficiency: the rules-1-d inventory extension (concepts brother/sister/sibling; axioms brother/sister subPropertyOf sibling, range(brother->man), range(sister->woman), propertyChain(sibling,father->father), propertyChain(sibling,mother->mother)) is expressible entirely in the EXISTING closed rule language — R-CHAIN is length-2 only but the twin engine's fixpoint rebuilds its index over ALL derived rels each pass (twin_engine.py Closure._run), so k=3 derivations compose from len-2 applications (stated sibling edge -> subprop -> shared-parent chain -> existing parent-chain grandparent -> existing coveredBy + gender elimination). NO engine code change, NO new rule kinds, NO encoder ALGORITHM_VERSION bump. The full-sibling shared-parent premise (true in CLUTRR's generator by construction, not in the world) is a policy-regime assumption disclosed on every proof node that uses it, exactly like UNA (ASM-1120) and covering (ASM-1121). Residual engine risks (functional-conflict interaction, derivation-budget growth on 4-entity worlds) are certificate/CF matters, verified not argued.
rationale: Separates 'inventory extension' (cheap, minted, endorsable) from 'rule-language extension' (expensive, refused by design) — the cost model of the whole tranche rests on this reading of the pinned engine bytes.

claim: Attribution controls fixed at design time: (1) c7 (uninformative rejection 'Rejected; answer again.' at the SAME accept/reject oracle, matched retry budget and token cost) is MANDATORY — the primary P1 = A3 - c7 is the only licensed carrier of a feedback-content claim; (2) attempt-0 prompts are surface-matched byte-identical across A1/A3/c7/c1 (pad both or neither), designing the measured +0.171 padding non-neutrality out of every registered contrast; (3) the primary stratum is gender-competitive only (>= 1 distractor sharing the gold's gender, decidable from edge_types at build time); items whose gold is the sole gender-consistent option are a quarantined descriptive stratum; (4) c1 (Sattolo-deranged TBox) is retained for content destruction with its extended-module behaviour re-measured and disclosed either way.

claim: Budget under pB=Opus: call counts are unchanged (pilot stage 132, full-campaign ceiling 796; the flag changes WHICH model answers pB, not how often), and the pinned conservative $0.012/call dollar bound still covers the opus half. Measured prompt envelope this tick: max assembled prompt + system prompt over all 282 instrument items = 3,126 bytes (mean 2,916) ~ <= 900 input tokens at a conservative 3.5 bytes/token; at Opus-4.8 API pricing ($5/$25 per MTok) that is ~$0.0045 input + ~$0.0005 for the contract-conformant ~20-token JSON answer ~= $0.005/call point estimate; even a pathological 300-token output lands at ~$0.012, i.e. AT the pinned bound. Additionally pB calls draw NO API dollars at all: the headless claude path strips ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/AUTH_TOKEN and the identity check REQUIRES apiKeySource == 'none' (OAuth subscription), so the bound-implied ledger remains a conservative accounting device, and the $10 usd_cap arithmetic (796 x $0.012 = $9.552) is unchanged. Known pre-existing looseness, disclosed not introduced: _budget_check counts items, not content-retry attempts (frozen semantics, <= 3 attempts/item).
rationale: Sizes the opus swap against the frozen review-fix-5 dollar-abort machinery without touching it; the pinned bound was derived from the haiku+codex ledger, so its coverage of opus is arithmetic on this tick's measured prompt bytes plus published pricing, not a measurement of opus calls.
resolution_path: The v2.2 pilot's per-call token usage is read back from the pB events.jsonl result records (modelUsage token counts) at pilot completion; if any call's API-equivalent cost exceeds $0.012 the successor's ops amendment re-derives the bound (and the ceiling arithmetic) before the full 796-call run is authorized.
backing_ref: poc/ontology-import-g2-v2/run-ontg2v2.py (_budget_check, PRICE_BOUND_USD_PER_CALL, run_claude env stripping, validate_claude apiKeySource check); docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md section 6 (pilot cost)

claim: The g2 instrument's sole sense-scoping device is the concept label's parenthetical: the frozen prompt template instructs 'the parenthetical, if any, tells you which sense of the word is meant' (v1 and v2.2 alike), and the rendered items' 'as described above' anaphora resolves to nothing beyond the Concept line (two-line item, verbatim substitution into {{ITEM}}). Where the parenthetical is a sense description ('lie (the words)', 'birth (the event)', 'reminder (the made something)') the claim is well-scoped; where it is bare valence ('break (X breaks Y)', 'make (X makes Y)', 'find (X finds Y)') the instrument delegates sense scope to the judge.
rationale: Mechanism by which kernel-level sense-underdetermination surfaces as judge disagreement; grounds the section-B reclassification and corroborates the prior panel diagnostic's render reconstruction.

claim: Precise pi:011 trace: record urn:kot-soft-type:g2i:g2-pi-011 types the RANGE of urn:kernel-v0:break (the patient Y) as BFO material entity (BFO_0000040), from SUMO Damaging/Object + FrameNet Cause_to_fragment (frame-414), hand-curated in build-softtype.py's per-concept table (break entries, lines ~222-251, with hand-drafted arm texts). Break's domain record types X as entity (BFO top) and the existential record types the TIME of breaking as temporal region. NO record in any layer types the breaking event itself as a material entity. The underlying concept is a single hand-authored NSM explication of the physical-shatter sense, listed in the kernel manifest's knownWeak, whose authoring note concedes the many-small-pieces clause 'fits shattering better than e.g. a broken machine'.
rationale: Corrects the 'break -> material entity' compression carried from the #25 table (the maintainer's 'breakage is never a material entity' responds to the compressed form) while confirming the defect is real at the range slot ('break a promise / the silence / a record' defeat the unhedged material gloss; both judges + prior Fable read rejected).
backing_ref: data/onto-softtype/soft-type-candidates.jsonl records g2-pi-010/011/012; poc/ontology-import-g2/build-softtype.py lines 222-251; data/kernel-v0/concepts/break.json; data/kernel-v0/manifest.json (knownWeak); docs/next/analysis/g2-panel-vs-assessment.md row 011

claim: Maintainer design requirement (2026-07-12, in response to the pi:011 trace on issue #25; the thread itself carries no comment — provenance is the session working-memory record, disclosed): a kernel concept is a SENSE, not a word; polysemous words must be split into one concept per sense at construction time (break-physical / break-sound / break-trust are distinct concepts); and typing must answer to the concept's own meaning — a breakage, in any sense, is an EVENT, never a material entity. The hand-built kernel-v0 does not sense-split.
rationale: Maintainer decision recorded with provenance; the binding requirement this adjudication applies in sections B-D. Not a measured claim about any artifact's current content beyond the final sentence (which ASM-1883 grounds).
backing_ref: working-memory record kern-sense-split-concepts (verbatim maintainer quote); issue #25 (the maintainer/kernel-of-truth, comment state verified empty this tick)

claim: Sense-as-unit is the settled norm of mature lexical-semantic resources: WordNet's unit is the synset; FrameNet's is the lexical unit (lemma-in-frame), and FrameNet separates the physical Cause_to_fragment from the norm-violating Compliance frame that covers 'break a promise / the law'; NSM practice explicates one sense per explication; 'break' is among the most polysemous English verbs (commonly cited count: 59 WordNet verb senses). UFO/gUFO typing (Kind/Role/Phase/Mode/Event) presupposes a determinate sense — a word cannot be UFO-typed, a sense can. DISCLOSURE: these are training-knowledge citations, not pinned against local source files this tick.
rationale: Grounds section C's claim that the maintainer's requirement is field-standard rather than idiosyncratic, and that ontology-derived construction implicitly sense-splits at frame choice.
backing_ref: UNPINNED training-knowledge citations (WordNet 3.x, FrameNet frame index, NSM literature, UFO/gUFO); resolution: pin WordNet/FrameNet source files (senses for break, make, find; Compliance vs Cause_to_fragment LUs) at the sense-split materials build

claim: Decomposition basis for the sanctioned pilot's 12 non-clean items (real-20260712-auth, table 28/1/7/4, AC1 0.6222): 7 splits are the hedge-scope channel (016,021,025,033,064,068,089 — deterministic across three response sets, targeted by the v2.2 rubric); 4 splits (036,037,070,071) plus the sole both-no (011) all sit on bare-parenthetical heavy-polysemy verbs (find x2, make x2, break), while no sense-fixed-parenthetical item split through the sense channel; bare-but-centrally-dominant verbs (give 046, help 053, lose 065) passed, so bare labels are a necessary-not-sufficient condition. g2:pi:070's render already contains an explicit breadth escape ('But Y can also be words, a plan, or a happening') and pA still rejected on non-creation idioms — prose alone cannot close the sense channel. COUNTERFACTUAL arithmetic on the observed table: landing the 7 hedge items with all 4 sense items still split gives 36/40 raw agreement, AC1 ~0.88 >= 0.65; repairing the 4 alone gives ~0.78.
rationale: The measured pattern behind section B's 7/5 decomposition ruling and section D's claim that the untouched pA channel does not by itself fail the AC1 gate.

claim: Adjudication ruling A+B (this agent's conclusion, confidence A 0.85, B 0.9/0.8): (A) ordinary-meaning soundness of the non-binding soft layer is a legitimate but SECONDARY quality gate — the load-bearing measurements are (i) sense-scoped soundness of the BINDING referent sorts (0.3929 stands only as a word-scoped lower bound; sense-scoped truth unmeasured) and (ii) engine-inference correctness under the typing via a deterministic-channel, ranking-sensitive instrument; neither is licensed by any outcome of the current soundness test. (B) The #25 framing 'English rendering ambiguity, not in the kernel' is correct for the 7 hedge items and WRONG for the 5 sense items: those show kernel-level sense-underdetermination, a construction defect prose iteration cannot repair. Principal correction to #25 recorded accordingly; secondary correction: 'break -> material entity' must everywhere read 'break's RANGE -> material entity'.
rationale: Adjudicator's own ruling (owned here, not the coordinator's), grounded in ASM-1880..1886; stipulated because it is a judgment about measurement priority and framing, not a measurement.
backing_ref: docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md sections A-B; issue #25 body (quoted phrases verified this tick)

claim: Adjudication ruling C+D (this agent's conclusion, confidence C 0.9, D 0.7): (C) sense-splitting is a PARTIAL BLOCKER on typing-soundness measurement — binding for polysemous word-labelled concepts (the measured property is ill-posed there: 'unsound' conflates typing error with scope error), already satisfied for sense-fixed concepts whose numbers stand; construction methodology becomes sense-split-FIRST (sense inventory imported from the pinned resources: FrameNet LUs, WordNet synsets, SUMO WordNet mappings), with NSM explication and typing authored per sense; UFO-typed construction depends on, and does not compete with, sense-splitting. (D) RUN the built-and-unspent v2.2 Opus pilot when the pA quota returns; on PASS run the full arm within the approved ceiling (no re-surfacing; this adjudication is the sanctioned decision point) with the licensing caps: a pilot PASS validates the INSTRUMENT not the typing; a full-arm PASS licenses only engineering adoption of a currently-unconsumed rank-only module, its soundness number carrying a sense-scope caveat (understates sense-scoped soundness by an unmeasured margin). On a second AC1 FAIL the pre-commitment retires the proxy pair for A3; the human-panel path should then be pointed at sense-split materials, not the current renders. Either way: no further prose iteration or judgment spend on the polysemous subset until it is sense-split, and the correctness track's marginal measurement effort moves to engine-inference-under-typing plus a sense-split re-measure of the binding sorts.
rationale: Adjudicator's own ruling; stipulated. Tradeoff disclosed in the document: the pilot's marginal cost is ~trivial (sole launch attempt died at preflight on pA quota, ~$0.12 bound), a validated proxy-judge instrument is the programme's scarcest commodity, and the pre-commitment bounds the downside to an endpoint the redirect reaches anyway.
backing_ref: docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md sections C-D; poc/ontology-import-g2-v2/runs/pilot-v22-20260712-171919/{driver.log,call-count.json} (aborted preflight, unspent); docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md (one funded Stage-P pilot; section-4 pre-commitment)

Heavy tail: break 59 verb synsets (75 across all POS), make 49, give 44, take 42, dead 17, find 16. Of the 39 g2-panel concepts, 24 words have >=4 same-POS senses. This pins locally the adjudication's previously LIT-BACKED-unpinned '59 WordNet verb senses for break' (ASM-1885 resolution path partially discharged).
rationale: Fixes the raw size of the sense inventory the construction must reconcile, the raw sense-multiplier ceiling (434/51 ~ 8.5x), and the Stage-B polysemous-panel-subset count (24 words).

claim: Exactly 8 pinned FrameNet 1.7 frames carry a 'break' lexical unit (data/onto-framenet/frames.jsonl): Cause_to_fragment (frame-414, agentive physical), Breaking_apart (1862, inchoative physical), Breaking_off (1860), Render_nonfunctional (416), Cause_harm (112), Experience_bodily_harm (384), Compliance (117, 'Acts and State_of_Affairs for which Protagonists are responsible' — the break-a-promise/law norm-violation sense), Opportunity (2350, break.n). The adjudication's claim that FrameNet separates the physical Cause_to_fragment from the norm-violating Compliance is confirmed at source; frame boundaries partition break's coarse senses and every break frame is eventive (or, for the nominal, an event-adjacent schema).
rationale: Grounds the frame-disjointness split criterion (R3) and the structural satisfaction of the maintainer's 'a breakage is an EVENT in every sense' clause; discharges the FrameNet half of ASM-1885's pinning path.
backing_ref: data/onto-framenet/frames.jsonl (frames 1862,1860,112,414,117,384,2350,416 with lexicalUnits break.*); data/onto-framenet/manifest.json (FrameNet 1.7, CC BY 3.0, sha-pinned source)

claim: Sense-inventory reconciliation rule for kernel-v1 construction: (R1) candidate senses = all same-POS WordNet 3.1 synsets of the lemma, enumerated mechanically; (R2) each candidate is projected onto the FrameNet frame carrying that lemma's LU where one matches, else frame:null; (R3) a kernel-v1 concept is a cluster of >=1 candidate synsets that share ONE FrameNet frame (or are all frame-null), one truthful NSM explication, and one argument structure (causative/inchoative never clustered) — the frame boundary is a HARD split criterion, within-frame clustering of WordNet's over-fine senses is permitted with member synsets listed; (R4) mint only kernel-needed senses, with every non-minted candidate recorded in an explicit excludedSenses list on the lemma's sense-index record (scope closed by construction, not prose); (R5) single-source candidates (FN LU without WN synset or vice versa) enter with disclosed single-source provenance, nothing silently dropped.
rationale: The load-bearing design choice: reconciles WordNet's over-fineness (59 break verb synsets) with FrameNet's coarser 8-frame partition without either re-merging distinct senses (g2's defect) or forcing 59 explications per verb. R4's exclusion lists are what convert 'this concept means the word' into 'this concept means this synset cluster and not those'.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 1.2-1.3 (worked break table); docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md section C (sense-split-first ordering ruling)

claim: Minting rule: alias URN urn:kernel-v1:<lemma>.<senseTag> (senseTag a short stable per-lemma-unique mnemonic, e.g. break.shatter / break.violate / break.malfunction; annotation, outside identity); identity remains the content-addressed urn:kot: mint over the kot-ast/1 explication object via the pinned mint tool (mintToolHash e07bc2ac...), so kernel-v1 requires NO encoder change, NO schema change, NO ALGORITHM_VERSION bump and NO X0 golden regeneration. Each concept carries an annotation sense record {senseTag, wn31Synsets, framenetFrame, glossQuote}; each lemma a sense-index record with excludedSenses. Fail-closed corpus gates: G1 duplicateIdentityGroups must stay 0 — two senses receiving byte-identical explications are merged and the merge REPORTED as a measured profile-1 expressivity limit, never silently absorbed; G2 every lemma with >=2 same-POS WN senses must have >=2 minted senses or 1 minted sense + non-empty excludedSenses, else the build fails with ERR_SENSE_UNDERDETERMINED.
rationale: Keeps sense-splitting entirely off the encoder-version critical path (data-corpus change only, per the repo convention that the content-hash pin governs {schema, algorithm, D, codebook, weighting} — none touched) while making unacknowledged polysemy a build error rather than a judge-discovered defect.
backing_ref: data/kernel-v0/manifest.json (minting block: identityPayload, mintToolHash, duplicateIdentityGroups); CLAUDE.md encoder-version convention; docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 2.1

claim: Migration policy: kernel-v0 stays frozen forever (frozen experiments reference it); kernel-v1 is a new corpus at data/kernel-v1/ with a TOTAL per-concept disposition map recorded via supersedes/supersededBy — CARRY (already sense-fixed concepts, e.g. 'lie (the words)': explication byte-identical, hence the SAME content-addressed urn:kot: mint, continuity for free; sense record seeded from the existing hand alignment rows), SEED (polysemous word-level concepts whose explication describes one sense: deprecated, explication becomes that sense's draft — v0 break -> break.shatter, per its own authoring note), REVIEW (latent flagged cases: friend 040/041; broken, whose downstream repair reference semantically wants break.malfunction not break.shatter, demonstrating the reference DAG is sense-sensitive and must be re-pointed edge by edge). WN/FN alignment files, soft-type records and molecules-v0 references are re-emitted against v1; a repo grep gate in the Stage-C build fails on any consumer still pointing at a deprecated word-level URN.
rationale: Preserves reproducibility of every frozen lineage while making the deprecation total — a dangling word-level reference would silently re-import the exact scope bug the split exists to remove.
backing_ref: data/lexical-wn31/alignment-kernel-v0.json (existing per-concept hand sense picks); data/kernel-v0/manifest.json (references DAG: broken->break, repair->broken); docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 2.2

claim: Per-sense explication + typing scheme: the authoring brief (WN glosses, FN frame definition, Core FEs, examples) is auto-assembled and the valency skeleton auto-suggested, but the NSM explication body is ALWAYS authored through the encoder/validator loop (auto paraphrase never promotes to Explicated, per scale-track section 3.4 rule 7). Typing is two-level and per-sense: (a) concept-level CK-UFO ontic category assigned via the deterministic cascade — all break.* senses are events (the maintainer clause satisfied structurally, since the category hangs off a sense, not a word), broken.* are modes/qualities, friend senses role/relator patterns, with 'underdetermined' a legal value; (b) argument-level: HARD referent sorts stay inside the existing profile-1 3-sort grammar (SomeoneRef/SomethingRef/TimeRef, unchanged — no encoder bump) but are committed per sense and hence well-posed, while FINE selectional typing (break.shatter patient -> material entity; break.violate object -> social/normative object, never material; break.interrupt patient -> process/state) is soft, rank-only, per-sense, under the same forbidden-effects discipline as the g2 import. The pi:011 defect class (sense-specific preference on a word-scoped concept) becomes unmintable because the record's subject IS the sense.
rationale: Separates what the maintainer's requirement governs (identity-bearing per-sense meaning and category) from what stays advisory (selectional preferences), and does both without touching the encoder pin or promoting soft content to hard laws.

claim: Cost anchors for the cheap validation, read from the frozen v2.2 build record: full 84-slot adoption arm worst-case ~ $9.552 (capped $10), Stage-P pilot ~ $1.06, per-judgment ~ $0.008-0.012 on the proxy pair; the sole v2.2 launch attempt died at preflight on pA quota having spent ~$0.12. Therefore the V-A re-judge (~36 slots x 2 judges) and V-B binding-sort measure (~30 slots x 2) each price at ~$1-3 of proxy spend, with the human panel (~1-2 h) as the pre-committed fallback channel if the v2.2 pilot fails its AC1 gate a second time.
rationale: Establishes that judge spend is negligible for Stage A — the binding constraint is explication authoring time, not model dollars — and that the validation inherits an already-priced, already-pre-committed instrument path either way.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md (worst-case $9.552/$10, pilot $1.06, unit $0.008-0.012); docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md section 0.3 and D (aborted preflight ~$0.12; retirement pre-commitment; human-panel fallback)

claim: Central empirical prediction of the design (inherited from adjudication ASM-1889b, now given a concrete build): re-doing the five g2 sense-channel items (011, 036, 037, 070, 071) with sense-split materials (break.{shatter,violate,malfunction}, make.{create,cause}, find.{locate,discover}, friend.{person,figurative}; ~10-12 sense concepts, ~36 soft-type slots) resolves >=4/5 of the judge disagreements and yields coherent per-sense verdicts (break.shatter range->material entity plausibly judged sound; break.violate range->material unmintable/absent), AND the sense-scoped soundness of the binding referent sorts — the load-bearing quantity ASM-1887 identifies as unmeasured, for which 0.3929 is only a word-scoped lower bound — becomes measurable on the same materials for ~$1-3 additional judge spend. Falsifier: persistent disagreement on sense-split items relocates the defect to the instrument or to the typing content itself — a genuine, non-artifactual correctness signal. Resolution path: the Stage-A build + one re-judge run (proxy pair if the v2.2 pilot passes AC1>=0.65, else human panel). Rider: DESIGN-ONLY; PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; no feasibility conclusion.
rationale: States the design's testable bet separately from its measured grounds so it can never be laundered as a premise; >=4/5 is a pre-stated expectation for reading the outcome, not a pass/fail gate on any thesis.
resolution_path: resolved by the Stage-A V-A/V-B run

claim: Scale and cost expectations, each with its resolution path: (a) under the R4 selection rule the full kernel-v1 mints ~110-140 sense concepts from the 54 v0 words (raw same-POS ceiling 434; heavy-tailed — break/make/give/take contribute 4-6 minted senses each, most words 1-2) — resolved by the Stage-C build; (b) stage cost bands: Stage A <= $10 model spend + 2-3 agent-days; Stage B (24 polysemous panel words, ~60-90 senses) ~$25-40 proxy or human-panel hours + 1-2 agent-weeks; Stage C 2-4 agent-weeks + <$50; Stage D absorbed into the scale track's S0 band — resolved by the respective build ledgers; (c) at the 10k+ scale stages the sense multiplier vanishes as a cost category because synset/LU-keyed import delivers sense-split concepts natively (the scale track's existing premise), with the G1 merge census (WN sense distinctions that collapse to identical profile-1 explications) becoming a standing per-stage measured output — resolved by the scale-track S0 build. Rider: DESIGN-ONLY; no feasibility conclusion.
rationale: Separates planning bands from commitments; the authoring bottleneck (not judge dollars) is flagged as the binding resource, with the mitigation (R4 mint-for-need + exclusion lists) named.
resolution_path: resolved by the Stage-A V-A/V-B run

claim: Staging + go/no-go ruling (this designer's, confidence 0.8, offered for maintainer approval): GO on Stage A only — sense-split break/make/find/friend and run V-A + V-B — because it is maintainer-required direction regardless of outcome, costs <= $10 + 2-3 agent-days, is decisive in both directions on the adjudication's 5/12 prediction, unblocks the load-bearing sense-scoped binding-soundness measurement, and touches neither the encoder pin nor any frozen record. Stages B (24-word panel subset), C (full kernel-v1, ~110-140 concepts) and D (scale handoff) are GATED on Stage A's readout, not approved here. Sequencing constraints carried from the adjudication verbatim: no further prose iteration or judgment spend on unsplit polysemous concepts; the v2.2 pilot decision (RUN when pA quota returns) is untouched and its outcome only selects V-A/V-B's judgment channel. Honest risk ledger acknowledged in the design: within-frame clustering judgment, profile-1 expressivity merges (G1), inherited instrument risk, authoring as the scarce resource, per-sense typing may still prove unsound (which is the point of measuring), dangling-deprecation leakage. No feasibility conclusion on CORRECTNESS or EFFICIENCY is stated or implied.
rationale: The ruling row: smallest decisive increment first, everything larger gated on its result, all frozen decisions left standing.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md section 6; docs/next/analysis/g2-measurement-and-sense-splitting-adjudication.md section D (pilot decision + no-spend-on-unsplit rule)

claim: WHY TRAIN-TIME INSTRUMENTED WHERE INFERENCE-TIME COULD NOT (interpretive ruling): rules-2 removes the fragile host-cooperation interface BY CONSTRUCTION — the treatment is an offline weight update from a byte-pinned deterministically-materialised corpus, and the measurement channel is forced-choice string equality vs certified engine gold, the programme's deterministic-channel class (now six-for-six instrumenting cleanly), whereas all three rules-1-line failures died at runtime judgment interfaces (elicitation, task form, verifier engagement; that class remains zero-for-five). Contributing process causes, credited: the ASM-1830..1836 blocking-pilot protocol's first instance exercised every gate channel on real GPU at the operating point pre-freeze and caught B4's vacuity (IP-4), which was struck rather than run; and the fail-closed pin discipline descends from the rules-1-c post-mortem. Honest converse: rules-2 ROUTES AROUND the inference-time slot, does not vindicate it — verify-retry remains unmeasured in both directions, settled un-instrumentable at the 2-option operating point (ASM-1848). Standing residual on the content claim: the ASM-1806 DESIGN-OPEN (anti-correlated forced-flip control cannot fully separate closure content from the pick-the-non-bridge shortcut) survives the PASS and travels with it.
rationale: Records the structural explanation for the lane's first valid reading so successor designs inherit the principle (test every kept interface at the operating point; delete every untestable one), and keeps the two standing residuals (verify-retry unmeasured; ASM-1806 shortcut) attached to the affirmative sentence.
backing_ref: docs/next/analysis/rules2-interpretation.md section 4; docs/next/analysis/rules1c-instrument-invalid-interpretation.md sections 1-3; poc/rules-2/asm-go-1847-1859.json row ASM-1848; registry/experiments/rules-2.json pins.blocking_pilot

claim: FORWARD PATHS licensed as directions, never premises: (a) kernel-specific value, if it exists, must be sought where the two rules sources DERIVE DIVERGENT closures — inventories/domains rich enough that kernel structure (UFO priors, cross-domain composition, the large-scale kernel track's regime) yields entailments a plain dictionary does not; ASM-1851 pre-registers the mechanical re-activation condition (a fresh divergent leg-1 artifact); (b) the authorized-but-unrun R2 (360M) tier would supply a second sign point at ~$6.5 worst-case if the maintainer wants scale language above 'none'; (c) the s4' refusal destruction is plausibly a training-mix/curriculum question (refusal retention under closure fine-tuning, aligned with honesty-first scoring #18), not an instrument question — a cheap follow-on inside the existing harness; (d) replication under corpus regeneration or a second vertical would convert 'stability at three seeds of one corpus' into a robustness statement. None of these is evidence; each names the experiment that would make it so.
rationale: Separates what the PASS makes worth buying next from what it licenses now; every path carries its own record and gates.
resolution_path: (a) new inventory + fresh knull leg-1 showing divergence -> amended rules-2-knull GPU leg; (b) coordinator-authorized --rungs R2 launch under the existing frozen record's second-tier provision; (c) a rules-2b record varying the refusal mix with s4' promoted to a primary; (d) a replication record with corpus-regeneration seeds.

claim: CLAIM DISCIPLINE RESTATED FOR THIS REGISTRATION (no conclusion is drawn here): the registered readout is MECHANICAL — a rule evaluation over the pinned analysis output — not a feasibility conclusion; interpretation belongs to the downstream interpretation role and the audit. Binding caps that survive the PASS rule: (a) the PERMANENT claim cap (PROPOSED-ASM-1438 as resolved by PROPOSED-ASM-1852) — the knull-analog sibling proved the plain-dictionary rules source derives the byte-identical training corpus on this closed inventory, so ANY rules-2 outcome is 'engine-materialised entailments (derivable from EITHER rules source) are internalisable by a small host', NEVER 'kernel-specific value'; (b) the s4' side-effect cap (ASM-1947) on deployment-shaped language (the fine-tuned host loses trained refusal); (c) the frozen extrapolation envelope verbatim (gold-parse-only, this closed inventory and vertical, LoRA at pinned HPs, proof depth <= 4, entity form at disclosed chance 0.5, scale language 'none'); (d) G-6 — the emitted verdict stays PASS-PENDING-AUDIT until a CONFIRMED non-runner audit lands.
rationale: Statistical honesty discipline: registering a mechanical PASS must not smuggle in an interpretive conclusion; the caps are the frozen record's own text, restated so no downstream reader mistakes the verdict object for a claim.

claim: THE DIVERGENCE-SEAM PRINCIPLE, operationalised: per ASM-1851/ASM-1939(a), kernel-specific value is askable only where the kernel and a matched non-kernel source derive DIVERGENT closures. ENGINE-INF constructs that seam deliberately — kernel-v1 sense-split per-sense typing vs word-level/mechanical sources over the certified deterministic engine — and makes closure correctness vs third-party-derived gold on the divergent items the PRIMARY endpoint. It is simultaneously the programme's first direct test of whether the kernel's typing makes the engine draw correct conclusions.
rationale: Four attribution probes (f2b-transfer, DECONF-B, CASC-0', rules-2-knull) all answered content-not-structure because the compared sources derived identical (byte-identical, in the knull case) closures; the question must be asked where they cannot coincide, and sense-splitting is structurally inexpressible in a word-level source.

claim: The Stage-A substrate exists as committed bytes: data/kernel-v1/ holds 11 sense concepts over break/make/find/friend with per-sense identity-bearing explications whose clause content types the arguments per sense (break.violate: 'Y is words; these words say: people cannot do some things...'; break.shatter: many-small-pieces; 53 excluded break siblings listed in the sense index), plus annotation-layer typing sidecars (ck-ufo per-sense ontic categories; 30 soft-type records, binding:false).
rationale: The divergence seam is built from bytes that already exist under maintainer direction (ASM-1884, Stage-A GO ASM-1909); ENGINE-INF adds no new kernel construction requirement at E0.

claim: SOFT-TYPE FENCE: the per-sense soft-type sidecar (binding:false, forbidden_effects including derive-disjointness and assert-type) is NOT consumed by ENGINE-INF in any arm. The kernel arm's hard axiom module (data/axioms-engineinf-v0/, kot-axiom/1, experiment-scoped) is authored by the explicator role from the identity-bearing EXPLICATION clause content, with every axiom citing its source clause; the promotion of explication clauses to binding domain/range axioms is an experimental stipulation scoped to this record and does not alter the corpus's typing-authority discipline.
rationale: Consuming rank-only records as axioms would violate their own forbidden-effects contract and taint the arm; the explications are kernel-proper and carry the per-sense commitments in their identity payload.

claim: The third-party gold substrate is already pinned: WordNet 3.1 (data/lexical-wn31, source tarball sha 3f7d8be8ef6ecc7167d39b10d66954ec734280b5bdcd57f7d9eafe429d11c22a) with mechanically extracted structural axioms (hypernym/hyponym/entailment) sufficient to compute the physical_entity/abstraction top split, per-synset gloss example sentences, and SemCor sense-frequency counts (index.sense tag_cnt). Sentence-level SemCor text and VerbNet selectional restrictions are NOT pinned and are named E1 additions.
rationale: The E0 deterministic gold channel must be constructible from committed bytes with no network dependency; what is missing is named rather than assumed.
