claim: GOLD CONSTRUCTION RULES (mechanical over third-party bytes; no LLM judge anywhere in the instrument): G1 sense-by-attachment (a WN31 gloss example carries its synset as sense label; E1 adds real SemCor sentence tags); G2 argument typing by WN31 hypernym closure to the physical/abstraction top split; G3 anomaly labels by the pre-registered cross-pairing rule over G1+G2 (disclosed as constructed-rule gold; VerbNet upgrade + ~30-item human spot-audit at E1); G4 refusal gold = excludedSenses membership. The RULES are stipulated; the BYTES are third-party and predate kernel-v1.
rationale: The channel must be deterministic and third-party-anchored while being honest that G3's labeling rule is a construction; the deflationary readings (kernel wrong on attested items) do not depend on G3.

claim: ARMS, matched by construction (same engine bytes, same shared world-compiler, same scorer, same items; only the source module differs): K (kernel-v1 + axioms-engineinf-v0); D-word-dom (one relation per lemma, SemCor-dominant-sense signature — the pi:011 shape); D-word-union (one relation per lemma, union/LCS signature); B-wn (same sense URNs, typing mechanically from WN31 sentence frames only, zero kernel authoring); K-shuf (per-sense domain/range assignments deranged within lemma, byte bulk identical). Word-level arms consume sense-tagged input by projection to the lemma relation — the same comparator construction as the rules-2-knull leg.
rationale: Kernel-vs-non-kernel must vary the source and nothing else; the projection step is the definition of what a word-level dictionary is, not a handicap.

claim: B-wn IS THE KERNEL-SPECIFICITY DECIDER: the primary (headline) contrast is K vs B-wn on Div(K,B-wn). Beating only the D-word arms licenses at most 'sense-split typing beats word-level typing at this scope' — a sense-STRUCTURE claim, never 'kernel-specific value', because sense structure exists in WordNet without kernel authoring. A K win over B-wn that is trivial (frames too coarse) triggers the pre-named E1 extension arm B-vn (VerbNet-typed senses) before any strengthened claim.
rationale: Kernel-specific means beating the best matched source constructible WITHOUT kernel authoring; pre-registering the two-tier claim ladder prevents overselling either tier.

claim: GOLD/BASELINE PROVENANCE SEPARATION (anti-circularity, fail-closed): B-wn's typing may consume WN sentence frames ONLY; gold G2/G3 consume hypernym paths and example objects ONLY; no arm compiler may read any gold field. Enforced mechanically by the poisoned-gold canary: perturbing gold bytes must leave every arm's compiled TBox+worlds byte-identical, and a planted compiler gold-read must trip the canary (PC-4).
rationale: The one seam where the instrument could rig itself (baseline or kernel typing derived from the same bytes as the gold labels) is closed by a mechanical byte-level test, not by argument.

claim: ENGINE FROZEN AT E0: ENGINE-INF Stage E0 uses the certified RULES-1 closed inventory only (R-DOM, R-RNG, R-SUBP, CAX-DW, fail-closed refusal codes) — no new rule kind, no engine version change, no encoder touch. Class-hierarchy reasoning is precomputed mechanically from pinned WN31 hypernym axioms into flat stated cls facts by the arm-shared compiler (the engine has no cax-sco). The E1 grade-W increment (in-engine typed sense-elimination, R-DISAMB) IS an engine version change and requires twin+sparq-reason conformance rerun plus a CF-gated mini-certificate before any scored use.
rationale: E0 rides entirely on certified machinery so a divergence result cannot be an artifact of new engine code; the engine extension is staged behind its own gates.

claim: PRIMARY ENDPOINT AND DECISION BAND, fixed before any byte is scored: divergence certificate Div(K,b) computed mechanically per baseline BEFORE scoring (the ASM-1851 leg-1 analog); PASS-affirm iff K divergent-set correctness Wilson-LB95 >= 0.80 AND K minus B-wn >= +0.20 on Div(K,B-wn) with exact-binomial LB95 > 0 AND K beats both D-word arms on their divergent sets AND convergent-set no-net-harm (K not worse than any baseline by > 0.02). Deterministic engine, no seeds, double-run byte-identity mandatory; inference is over the finite WN31 gloss-example item frame (census numbers reported; CIs carry the pre-registered generalization to Stage-B lemmas only, tagged EXTRAPOLATION).
rationale: One primary endpoint, exact thresholds, and a non-vacuousness artifact fixed pre-freeze, per the programme's prereg discipline.

claim: KILL CRITERIA, verbatim: KILL-e1 (vacuity) fires iff |Div(K,D-word-dom)| < 25 at the operating point or divergent cells span < 3 closure ops or < 3 lemmas — the instrument cannot ask, no verdict either direction. KILL-e2 (convergence-deflation) fires iff |Div(K,B-wn)| < 10 — the kernel-vs-mechanical-sense-typing question is unaskable at this inventory and the outcome is recorded as a D3(b) input, never as an affirmative.
rationale: The knull lineage's lesson made mechanical: certify non-vacuous divergence before interpreting any contrast; name the convergent outcome's meaning in advance so it cannot be spun.

claim: BOTH READINGS PRE-REGISTERED: (affirmative) a PASS-affirm is the programme's FIRST kernel-specific-value datum, scoped to this inventory, this engine, downstream of third-party sense identification; (deflationary) KILL-e2 or FAIL-deflate (B-wn matches/beats K on the divergent set, or K's divergent-set correctness < D-word-dom's) is the honest trigger for the D3(b) thesis reframe — grounded content + certified engine, not kernel-specific structure — delivered to the maintainer as a pre-registered decision input. The verbatim deflationary sentence is fixed in the design §2.3 and may not be softened post hoc.
rationale: The experiment is decisive only if both outcomes have owners and meanings fixed before data; the reframe trigger is a feature, not a failure mode.

claim: HONESTY-FIRST SECONDARY SCORE: alongside the primary raw exact-match, every arm is scored with the issue-#18-aligned asymmetric penalty (wrong assertion 1, refusal 0.5, correct 0; form frozen at freeze). Descriptive, never in the PASS rule; it exists to separate K's fail-closed refusals from D-word-dom's confident wrong assertions on excluded-sense and category-error cells.
rationale: The programme's scoring direction penalises wrong answers above refusals; the word-level arm's characteristic failure mode is exactly confident wrongness.

claim: BLOCKING PILOT INSTANTIATION (mandatory, pre-freeze, per ASM-1830/1831): PC-1 no baseline floored/saturated on pilot divergent cells (K at 1.0 disclosed as certificate-normal); PC-2 pilot divergence |Div(K,D-word-dom)| >= 6 and |Div(K,B-wn)| >= 3 across >= 2 ops and >= 2 lemmas, divergence certificate committed; PC-3 K-shuf differs from K on >= half the pilot divergent cells with the predicted signature, D-union not row-identical to D-dom; PC-4 planted mistyped axiom fires the scorer/certificate on predicted cells only + poisoned-gold canary; PC-5 extractor well-formedness >= 0.95 + oracle arm 1.0 through the pinned scorer. The kernel-as-text control is structurally N/A (no LLM/host consumer exists — no prompt surface for the text to enter), recorded with reason; best achievable verdict is PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS.
rationale: Every interface the instrument keeps is exercised, not argued, before freeze; the N/A is explicit and downgrades, never silently passes.

claim: EXPECTED OPERATING POINT (direction only, never a premise): the WN31 gloss-example extractor over break/make/find/friend is expected to yield ~60-120 items (attested + cross-pair anomaly + excluded-sense refusal cells) with >= 25 items divergent between K and D-word-dom and >= 10 divergent between K and B-wn. Resolution path: the extractor's first run plus the PC-2 pilot check; if the expectation fails, KILL-e1/e2 semantics apply pre-freeze at zero cost.
rationale: Item counts are guessed from the measured sense inventory (59 break verb synsets, 53 excluded siblings, per-sense examples), not measured; the pilot resolves the guess before any freeze.
resolution_path: resolved by the engine-inference experiment run

claim: FORWARD PATH, direction only: a committed ENGINE-INF divergence certificate over this inventory is exactly the 'fresh leg-1 artifact showing divergence' that ASM-1851 pre-registers as the mechanical re-activation condition for the rules-2 knull GPU leg. If E0 certifies divergence, a rules-2-shaped train-time campaign on THIS inventory (E3, its own record, own pilot, maintainer authorization, ~$7-18 GPU at rules-2 anchors) would for the first time have non-byte-identical kernel vs dictionary training corpora — the train-time kernel-specificity contrast rules-2 could not buy. Never a premise of ENGINE-INF's own verdict.
rationale: Names the experiment the divergence artifact would enable, per the register's discipline of licensing directions rather than conclusions.
resolution_path: resolved by the engine-inference experiment run

claim: STAGING, COST, GO/NO-GO: E0 (axiom module + extractor + 5 arms + oracle + divergence certificate + blocking pilot + freeze + run) at $0 model spend, CPU-only, 2-4 agent-days — GO recommended (designer confidence 0.85, ASSESSMENT, binding on nothing); E1 (SemCor/VerbNet pins, ~30-item ~1h human spot-audit, grade-W R-DISAMB engine extension behind conformance + mini-certificate) $0 + 2-3 agent-days, gated on E0's verdict; E2 (Stage-B ~60-90 senses) 1-2 agent-weeks authoring shared with the sense-split track, gated on E0 PASS-affirm or maintainer direction. The maintainer's one pre-freeze decision: G3 anomaly gold at E0 as WN-rule-constructed (recommended; disclosed-weaker) vs waiting for the VerbNet pin.
rationale: Cheapest-decisive-first: the seam is created in the TBox where authoring costs days, not in a training corpus where the same divergence costs a GPU campaign.

claim: CLAIM CAPS, fixed now: any ENGINE-INF outcome is scoped to the Stage-A inventory (11 senses, 4 lemmas), the certified closed engine inventory, compiled worlds (no NL parsing — the l3a wall stands), grade-S input (downstream of third-party sense identification at E0), and gold whose G3 component is rule-constructed until the E1 upgrade. No outcome licenses: NL robustness, any host/train-time/inference-time integration claim, any scale claim, any deployment claim, or any CORRECTNESS/EFFICIENCY thesis verdict. An affirmative licenses exactly one sentence: 'where kernel-v1 sense-split typing derives engine closures that matched non-kernel sources do not, the kernel's closures are the correct ones by third-party-derived gold, at this scope.'
rationale: The result will be quotable in both directions; the licensed sentence is fixed before it exists.

claim: NORTH-STAR FRAMING: colibri (the colibri engine (open-source GLM-5.2 CPU runner), Apache-2.0 single-file C engine running GLM-5.2 from ~25GB RAM + ~370GB NVMe) is adopted as the reference integration substrate for thesis (B) (inference-compute reduction) because it exposes the internals no API exposes (expert routing, cache/pin, draft injection, KV); thesis (A) (intelligence/grounding) is host-agnostic and enters this substrate only for colibri's determinism/pinning properties. GLM52-NS is a roof the current tracks build toward; it blocks nothing and is blocked by almost nothing at probe stage.
rationale: Separating which thesis actually needs the local engine prevents spending colibri's brutal throughput (0.05-1 tok/s) on experiments an API serves better.

claim: COLIBRI FACT INVENTORY, fetch-grade: single C file c/glm.c (~2,400 lines) + headers, zero runtime deps, no plugin architecture (customisation = editing glm.c / the FP8->int4 converter under c/tools/ / c/openai_server.py); ~17B dense params resident (~8.7-9.9GB int4, sources disagree), routed experts streamed int4 from disk (~370GB, ~19MB/expert) with per-layer LRU cache + pinned hot set loadable from a stats file (PIN=) + AUTOPIN learning cache (.coli_usage) + OS page cache L2; sigmoid router + per-expert bias, greedy top-k, TOPK/TOPP env overrides; PILOT=1 router-lookahead prefetch (next-layer routing 71.6% predictable from current-layer post-attention state; measured NEUTRAL on saturated disk); MLA KV 576 floats/token/layer (57x); MTP head int8-mandatory, 39-59% acceptance, 2.2-2.8 tok/forward; grammar-forced GBNF drafts injected as pre-accepted spans (~1.0 acceptance, adaptive disable <50%); cold decode ~11GB expert reads/token, ~0.05-0.1 tok/s on ~1GB/s NVMe, community 0.28-1.83 tok/s; requires x86 AVX2 + OpenMP, >=16GB RAM (25GB rec.), ~370GB LOCAL NVMe. PROVENANCE CAVEAT: obtained 2026-07-12 via summarising WebFetch of the README and raw glm.c — paraphrase risk; two internal discrepancies flagged (21,504 routed experts vs 75 MoE layers x 256 = 19,200; top-k 6 vs 8). Every row is re-verified mechanically from the checkout + model config.json at probe P0.1 before anything load-bearing rests on it.
rationale: The design's arithmetic and seam map rest on these engine facts; house honesty requires recording that they are fetch-grade, not checkout-verified, and naming the mechanical upgrade path.
backing_ref: the colibri engine (open-source GLM-5.2 CPU runner) README + c/glm.c (fetched 2026-07-12); docs/next/design/glm52-kernel-integration-northstar.md §1.1, §5.2 P0.1

claim: GLM-5.2 ARCHITECTURE, fetch-grade: 744B-total-parameter DeepSeek-V3-style MoE by Z.ai (weights MIT); ~40B active/token; 78 transformer layers (first 3 dense FFN, remainder MoE); 256 routed experts/MoE-layer with ~8 active + 1 shared expert (colibri glm.c summary said topk~6, n_shared~2 — config.json at P0 resolves); embedding dim 6,144; vocab ~155k; MLA + DSA sparse attention; 1M-token context; native single MTP layer for EAGLE-style speculative decoding. Sources: Z.ai/community coverage via web search 2026-07-12 (orbital.net.in, lucaberton.com, NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel GLM-5 MoE-DSA page, SGLang GLM-5.2 cookbook, together.ai model page) — none verified beyond search-result level; treated as background, with colibri's config.json as the authoritative pin at P0.
rationale: Seam arithmetic (11.4GB/token, expert-count bands) needs the MoE geometry; the design deliberately keeps exact values non-load-bearing until read from the checkpoint config.

claim: SEAM TAXONOMY, fixed for this track: B1a concept-conditioned expert CACHING/PINNING/prefetch (quality-safe); B1b concept-guided expert-DROP = dimension collapse on the expert axis (function-changing); B2 deterministic engine as speculative DRAFTER through colibri's existing draft/grammar injection path; B3 concept-vector KV compression/augmentation; A1 engine as grounding/verification/constrained-decoding layer at the OpenAI-gateway seam. B1a/B1b/B2/B3 serve thesis (B); A1 serves thesis (A); B2 and A1 share the engine-output plumbing.
rationale: A fixed seam vocabulary keeps later preregs, gates, and verdicts addressable and prevents scope drift between quality-safe and function-changing interventions.

claim: DISK-BOUND ARITHMETIC (over ASM-1971/1972 premises): cold expert I/O ~= L_moe x k x 19MB ~= 75 x 8 x 19MB ~= 11.4GB/token (matches README ~11GB); disk-bound throughput tok/s <= bandwidth / (miss_rate x 11.4GB), hence INVERSE-LINEAR in expert bytes actually read — halve misses (better pinning) or halve loads (fewer experts) => ~2x tok/s until the CPU-matmul wall. PREFETCH DOES NOT REDUCE BYTES READ, only overlaps I/O with compute; with I/O >> compute the overlap ceiling is the compute fraction, which is why colibri measures PILOT neutral on saturated disks. Therefore on small boxes the levers are (i) WHAT SITS IN RAM (pin/cache selection), (ii) HOW MANY experts are loaded (top-k/top-p/drop), (iii) draft amortisation (union-of-experts per verified batch); prefetch recall matters only on balanced hardware. RAM bands: 32GB box => ~15-18GB cache ~= 800-950 experts ~= 4-5% of ~20k; 64GB => ~2,300-2,600 ~= 12%.
rationale: Corrects the task brief's raise-prefetch-recall emphasis honestly and identifies which interventions can move throughput on the probe hardware; all arithmetic, no new empirical claim.

claim: SEAM RANKING by tractability x impact x testability, and FIRST TARGET: (1) B1a — zero-to-tiny C changes (PIN= interface exists), attacks the measured bottleneck, quality-preserving, mechanical admission gate from trace replay; (2) B1b — larger win (footprint + linear I/O) but function-changing, enters only after B1a's admission gate; (3) A1 — buildable now at the gateway, thesis value gated on kernel coverage + blocking pilots; (4) B2 — injection path exists (grammar drafts) but impact ~0 at current engine coverage; (5) B3 — deferred (ASM-1981). FIRST TARGET = B1a entered through measurement-only probe P0, because its decisive question (does concept-conditioned routing structure exist in GLM-5.2?) is answerable for ~$20-50 with no C modification, no quality eval, and a mechanical yes/no whose negative also kills B1b cheaply.
rationale: Cheapest-decisive-question-first ordering; the P0 trace dataset is re-analysable indefinitely (delegate-and-reanalyse discipline) without re-spending on collection.

claim: ADMISSION GATE G-B1 (pre-registered shape; exact threshold frozen in the P1 prereg): from P0 routing traces, offline oracle replay compares held-out-prompt cache hit rates at MATCHED RAM budget for: global-hot pin (AUTOPIN-analog), recency-LRU (colibri default), embedding-topic-cluster pin (kernel-free deflator), kernel-concept pin, and oracle per-prompt pin (upper bound). The B1 seam family is ADMITTED iff kernel-concept pinning beats BOTH the global-hot AND topic-cluster deflators by >=10% relative miss reduction at matched budget on held-out prompts; else B1a AND B1b are recorded dead-at-this-model and the track's residual value is A1 plumbing + B2 acceptance measurement. Analogous in role to DDC's ddc0 Stage-0 admission gate.
rationale: DeepSeek-family load-balanced routers may be too flat to condition on; a ~$30 mechanical gate finds out before any prototype is paid for, and its FAIL branch is worded in advance.

claim: DEFLATOR DISCIPLINE for every B1 claim: colibri's own AUTOPIN learning cache (pin-what-was-hot, zero kernel) and an embedding-topic-cluster prior (cluster prompts with any off-the-shelf embedding; per-cluster expert histograms; zero kernel — the knull-analog for this seam) are MANDATORY comparison arms at matched RAM budget. A kernel-specific sentence is licensed only if the kernel arm beats BOTH; a tie is reported with equal prominence as 'concept/topic conditioning helps; the kernel is one conditioner among several' — the f2b/DECONF content-not-structure wording, pre-adopted. The one mechanism a topic model cannot replicate — conditioning on entailed-but-unstated concepts from engine closure — is gated on engine/kernel coverage and is the only route to a structural kernel claim at this seam.
rationale: Four prior kernel-specificity probes deflated to content-not-structure; this seam ships with its strongest deflator built into the reference engine, so the control structure must be fixed before any number exists.

claim: QUALITY-SAFETY CLASSIFICATION: B1a pinning/prefetch interventions are quality-preserving BY CONSTRUCTION (they change what is cached, never what is computed; verified in P1 by DRAFT=0 byte-identical outputs), so their endpoints are pure systems numbers needing no ability-retention gate. B1b expert-drop and forced-mode B2 CHANGE the computed function and require DDC-style matched-compression/matched-load-count retention evaluation with their own prereg + blocking pilot before any run. No function-changing intervention may be reported under a quality-safe label.
rationale: Keeps the cheap rung genuinely cheap (no eval burden) and makes silent quality degradation mechanically impossible to smuggle into a throughput claim.

claim: POTENTIAL-SAVING BANDS, direction-only, resolution = P0.3 replay + P1 live A/B: if global-hot pinning achieves hit H_g=0.3 and concept-conditioned pinning at matched budget achieves H_c in {0.5, 0.6, 0.7}, disk-bound throughput multiplies by ~{1.4x, 1.75x, 2.3x} (bytes/token scales with 1-hit); B1b loading 4-of-8 experts halves I/O (~2x disk-bound tok/s) by arithmetic with quality entirely open; a concept-bounded deployment shard at retained fraction f=0.25 cuts the disk estate 370GB -> ~93GB if retention holds. None of these numbers is a premise anywhere; G-B1/G-P1/P2a gates resolve them.
rationale: Registers the size of the prize honestly as projection, so planning can weigh the probe without any band leaking into a claim.
resolution_path: resolved by the P0 concept-routing probe

claim: B2 ENGINE-AS-DRAFTER design: engine-derived spans (entailed answers, typed-slot fills, canonical renderings) enter through colibri's EXISTING draft/grammar injection machinery in two pre-distinguished modes — (i) LOSSLESS: drafts verified by the model's own distribution via rejection sampling; output distribution unchanged; acceptance rate is the measurable; a pure (B) accelerator; (ii) FORCED: engine-licensed spans forced as in grammar mode; output changes; classified as an (A) mechanism (constrained correct decoding) and never reported as a pure speedup. Value scales with (coverage of engine-derivable output spans) x (surface-form acceptance); at current engine coverage the expected end-to-end impact is ~0, so B2 is a P1 side-quest (measure acceptance on kinship/sense items), not a lead bet.
rationale: The injection path is production-proven (grammar drafts ~1.0 acceptance), so the honest open variables are coverage and surface-form match, both named with their resolution paths.

claim: B3 (concept-vector KV) is DEFERRED INDEFINITELY as a seam: MLA KV (576 floats/token/layer, RAM-resident) is not the measured bottleneck at any context colibri users run; substituting foreign vectors training-free breaks a function the attention was trained to read, and any learned adapter is a training intervention outside the training-free tier; and injecting kernel vectors into activations violates the programme's standing Law-1 interface-locality discipline as currently stated. Recorded as a research note whose mandatory deflators (kernel-free KV merging/eviction) are pre-named; re-opening it requires an explicit maintainer decision revisiting Law-1 plus a long-context workload where KV is demonstrably binding.
rationale: Prevents the programme's one-concept-vector-carries-many-tokens thesis from being tested first at its least tractable, discipline-violating seam.

claim: A1 GATEWAY design: all (A) integration lives at the OpenAI-gateway seam (c/openai_server.py or a proxy), zero C changes — (1) pre-generation engine-closure grounding (SCALE-GROUND A3/A4 arm shapes with GLM-5.2 as host); (2) post-generation claim checking with engine verdicts {CONSISTENT, ANOMALOUS, REFUSE} driving verify-retry or flagging (honesty-first alignment); (3) engine-emitted GBNF grammars for constrained decoding (simultaneously (A)-correctness and (B)-throughput). A1's colibri-specific asset is INSTRUMENT-GRADE DETERMINISM: pinned weights + DRAFT=0 byte-reproducible greedy decoding remove API-model drift from the instrument threat model, and GLM-5.2-as-host is the natural scale-artifact test of the verify-retry hypothesis (prior hosts possibly too small to cooperate). Every A1 thesis experiment requires the ASM-1830..1836 blocking pilot and inherits the ENGINE-INF divergence-seam discipline; A1 experiments not needing determinism should use the API, not colibri.
rationale: Routes (A) around the programme's 0-for-5 runtime-interface failure zone by naming the pilot requirement up front, and captures the one genuinely new (A) asset (a deterministic frontier host) without overclaiming.

claim: GATING + PREREQUISITES (the dependency line): P0 needs nothing but the authorised EC2 box; P1 needs G-B1 plus concept-LABELLED corpora (current <100-concept kernel suffices — conditioning needs labels, not coverage); P2a needs a P1 win + DDC's arm/control discipline (reading ddc1's verdict for method transfer); P2b needs ENGINE-INF E0 + kernel coverage (SCALE-1 S1/S2) + a blocking pilot; the NS row (comprehensive-kernel-driven routing/drafting/grounding) needs SCALE-1 S2 (1M kernel), RULES-SCALE (engine at scale), the NET-NEW concept->expert alignment map (seeded at P0/P1), and the runtime NL->concept mapper (SCALE-1 §6.6's named open; the l3a parse wall). Current tracks (sense-split, DDC, ENGINE-INF, SCALE-1) are not blocked by and do not wait on GLM52-NS.
rationale: Draws the explicit line from today's tracks to the maintainer's north star, so no stage silently presumes an object that does not exist yet.

claim: INFRA SPEC: probe on one x86 AVX2 EC2 instance with INSTANCE-LOCAL NVMe (network volumes excluded per colibri's own warning; Graviton excluded by AVX2). Recommended probe box i4i.2xlarge (8 vCPU / 64GiB / 1,875GB Nitro NVMe — 64GB gives ~12% expert-cache headroom for conditioning experiments); cheapest viable floor i4i.xlarge (4 vCPU / 32GiB / 937GB); m6id/c6id.2xlarge rejected (474GB tight; c6id 16GB RAM at colibri's floor). Storage lifecycle: instance-store is ephemeral — convert once, sync the ~370GB int4 artifact to S3 (~$8.5/month), restore per session (~30-60 min parallel); the FP8->int4 converter streams the ~756GB checkpoint shard-by-shard (5GB peak staging) so no oversized download volume is needed. Spot pricing preferred for measurement stages.
rationale: Smallest plan that meets colibri's published floor with enough RAM headroom that the admission question is not artificially capped by cache size.

claim: COST/PERF PLANNING BANDS, resolution = P0 receipts: instance pricing i4i.xlarge ~$0.34/h, i4i.2xlarge ~$0.69/h on-demand us-east-1 (spot ~60-70% off) — from model knowledge, VERIFY AT SPIN-UP; P0 total ~$20-50 on-demand (~$8-20 spot) + $8.5/mo S3 + 2-4 agent-days; P1 ~$50-150 + 1-2 agent-weeks; P2a ~$100-300 + 2-3 agent-weeks; expected P0 baseline throughput ~0.05-0.1 tok/s cold on ~1GB/s-class NVMe (dev-machine analogue), measured by iobench + the baseline suite. No band is a premise or a commitment.
rationale: Prices the staged plan for the maintainer decision without letting any estimate masquerade as a measurement.
resolution_path: resolved by the P0 concept-routing probe

claim: STAGED PLAN + GATES: P0 (bring-up + fact verification + baseline perf + concept-stratified routing-trace collection + offline replay; measurement only; the one permitted C touch is a ~20-line trace-dump patch, diff kept in-repo) gated by G-B1 (ASM-1976); P1 (live concept-guided PIN A/B vs AUTOPIN vs topic-cluster vs random at matched budget; B2 acceptance side-quest) gated by G-P1: kernel arm beats both deflators live with DRAFT=0 byte-identical outputs — a win licenses at most 'concept-conditioned expert caching reduces expert I/O on concept-labelled workloads at this model/box', sign not slope; P2a (guided expert-drop, matched load-count, prefill/loglik-scored retention) and P2b (A1 host experiments) each require their own prereg + blocking pilot; NS is grant-class and gated per ASM-1983. Every stage stops cleanly; no stage pre-commits the next; within an approved stage ceiling the no-pre-surface rule applies.
rationale: Probe->prototype->integration with a kill available at every rung and both directions of every gate worded before any number exists.

claim: CLAIM CAPS, fixed now: nothing in GLM52-NS P0-P1 licenses any intelligence, efficiency, parity, cost, or thesis claim — P0/P1 are systems measurements on one model on one box under concept-labelled workloads; a G-P1 kernel-vs-topic tie is reported with equal prominence under the pre-adopted content-not-structure wording (ASM-1977); B1b/A1 results, when they exist, are scoped by their own preregs; no outcome anywhere in this track moves the CORRECTNESS or EFFICIENCY synthesis verdicts, which remain INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING per feasibility-synthesis-v6 until their own designated experiments land.
rationale: GLM-5.2 numbers will be quotable; the cap is registered before any exists.

claim: EVALUATION-THROUGHPUT MITIGATION: at colibri's 0.05-1 tok/s, generative benchmarks cost days per arm, so all P2a quality/retention endpoints ride PREFILL/LOGLIKELIHOOD scoring (batched prefill amortises expert loads across the whole sequence; the poc/pubeval scoring style), not generation; a small openai_server.py patch exposing echo+logprobs is a named build task. Generative evaluation is reserved for cells where loglik endpoints are undefined, at explicitly budgeted item counts.
rationale: Without this the function-changing rungs are unaffordable on the reference substrate; with it they cost 10-100x less by construction of the workload.

claim: MAINTAINER DECISION POINTS surfaced by this design: (1) approve/deny P0 + its ~$20-50 ceiling on the already-authorised extra EC2 (after approval, no re-surfacing within the ceiling); (2) fork-vs-upstream-PR etiquette for any glm.c change beyond the P0 trace-dump patch (single-author upstream, no plugin architecture, fork-maintenance liability; upstream-contribution-noise discipline applies); (3) whether B3/Law-1 is ever revisited (default NO per ASM-1981); (4) at P2b time, whether GLM-5.2-as-deterministic-host replaces or supplements API hosts in (A) experiment designs. Each is a threaded GitHub issue per the maintainer-decisions-as-issues practice when it becomes actionable; none is decided in this document.
rationale: Separates what the designer proposes from what only the maintainer may decide, and names each decision's trigger point.

claim: F1 PLAN FRAMING: the GLM-5.2 follow-up comprises (i) F1-K Kernel-as-Expert as the PRIMARY experiment, unconditional on the probe's routing outcome, and (ii) the SECONDARY routing levers pre-registered as a three-branch decision tree (A: concept structure -> F1-A live pinning A/B; B: predictable-but-not-conceptual -> F1-B salvage-or-kill; C: no exploitable per-prompt structure -> F1-C pivot), with per-experiment cost ceilings, signed off ONCE by the maintainer; after sign-off F1-K runs on P0's landing (subject to MAINTAINER GATE 0), the branch classifier fires mechanically, and each experiment runs without re-surfacing inside its ceiling (no-pre-surface discipline). Sequencing on the one box: F1-K first, then the classified routing branch. Lever definitions carried from ASM-1973: B1a = concept-conditioned expert PINNING (quality-safe, speed); B1b = concept-guided expert DROP (function-changing, size+I/O), gated behind B1a's outcome and its own future prereg.
rationale: Pre-writing the primary experiment and all routing branches before the probe's outcome is known removes outcome-contingent design freedom, and a single sign-off keeps the maintainer loop off the critical path.

claim: P0.1 BASELINE PARTIALS read from the running probe's probe-main.log (S3 artifact bucket, glm52-probe prefix) on 2026-07-12: expert-disk I/O = 80% of decode wall-time on the probe i4i.2xlarge; effective NVMe read bandwidth 3.33 GB/s; cold decode 0.09 tok/s. These are the ONLY probe numbers read before the F1 design, its branch classifier, and the KaE ladder were fixed; no routing-structure output (traces, replay, LOOKA, fingerprints, sense pairs, TOPK sweep) was read. All three values are re-verified against the final stats.tgz when the probe lands; a material revision (>10% on any value) triggers re-derivation of ASM-2012 before any branch runs.
rationale: Pins exactly what was known at design-freeze so the pre-registration's blindness to the classified quantity is auditable.

claim: F1 PHYSICS ENVELOPE (arithmetic over ASM-2011 + ASM-1974): with disk share d=0.8, removing fraction r of expert miss-bytes multiplies decode throughput by 1/(0.2+0.8(1-r)) — r=0.3 -> 1.32x, r=0.5 -> 1.67x, r=1 -> 5.0x HARD CEILING on this box; any larger claim on this box is arithmetic error. Bandwidth cross-check: 3.33 GB/s / ~11.4 GB/token bounds cold decode at ~0.29 tok/s vs 0.09 measured, implying ~31% effective read efficiency; therefore endpoints use engine miss-byte counters as primary and iostat as cross-check, never bandwidth arithmetic as measurement. For F1-K the same physics constrains EVALUATION cost only (prefill/loglik mandatory per ASM-1988), not the quality claim.
rationale: Fixes the interpretation envelope before results exist and demotes bandwidth arithmetic to a sanity band.

claim: BRANCH CLASSIFIER (routing levers only; F1-K exempt by construction), frozen blind (no routing-structure output read at emission): from P0.3 offline replay at the probe box's matched RAM budget on held-out prompts, compute M_oracle (relative miss-byte reduction of oracle per-prompt pin vs global-hot pin), M_kernel (kernel-concept pin vs the STRONGER of global-hot and embedding-cluster deflators; the G-B1 quantity with its margin now frozen at exactly 10% as ASM-1976 required of this prereg), p_perm (one-sided permutation p, >=10,000 shuffles, within- vs across-concept-cluster fingerprint similarity), SensePairSep (fraction of registered sense-minimal-pair comparisons where cross-sense fingerprint distance exceeds within-sense). Branch A iff M_kernel>=10% AND p_perm<0.05 (sub-case A1 iff SensePairSep>=0.75, else A2: F1-A proceeds but sense-level granularity and sense-split B1b shards are recorded unsupported at this scale). Branch B iff not-A AND M_oracle>=15%. Branch C iff M_oracle<15% (ceiling speedup <~1.14x by ASM-2012 — beneath measurement dignity; both routing levers dead). Corrupt/irreproducible probe outputs -> classifier does not fire; probe re-runs within its own ceiling first.
rationale: A mechanical, exhaustive, pre-frozen classifier is what makes the single-sign-off tree legitimate; thresholds are stated with their arithmetic justification rather than tuned to data.

claim: F1-A DESIGN (Branch A, secondary-primary): six arms, fully paired on N=48 fresh held-out prompts (>=2 per probe concept cluster, incl. fresh sense-minimal pairs; 96 decode tokens; surface-disjoint from map-building prompts, no shared content 5-gram; KaE patch disabled in all routing arms): a0 LRU-only, a1 AUTOPIN (deflator 1), a2 embedding-cluster pin (deflator 2, knull-analog; pinned off-the-shelf sentence embedding frozen by content hash at bring-up; K = concept-cluster count), a3 kernel-concept pin (treatment; map from P0 trace train-split only), a4 random pin (floor), a5 oracle per-prompt pin (ceiling, never to-beat). Primary endpoint: expert miss-bytes per generated token (engine counter, iostat cross-check). Mandatory quality invariant: DRAFT=0 greedy outputs byte-identical across arms per prompt (SHA-256 over token ids); any divergence voids the run. Optional zero-marginal-cost rider: B2 lossless draft-acceptance measurement on kinship/sense items, skipped without penalty near ceiling.
rationale: Instantiates the parent P1 with exact arms and the byte-identity check that makes B1a's quality-safety mechanically verified rather than asserted.

claim: F1 ROUTING-LEVER DEFLATOR MANDATE (ASM-1977 instantiated): no kernel-specific sentence is licensed in any routing branch unless the kernel arm beats BOTH colibri's AUTOPIN usage-history pinning AND the concept-agnostic embedding-cluster baseline at matched PIN_GB budget on held-out prompts; a tie with either deflator is reported with equal prominence under the pre-adopted content-not-structure wording (the f2b/DECONF-B/RULES-2 form). In Branch B the mandate takes the additive form: concept labels must add >=5% median relative miss-byte reduction OVER the kernel-free conditioner or the kernel claim is dead at this model/box. The KaE deflator ladder is registered separately (ASM-2029).
rationale: The programme's four content-not-structure deflations make the kernel-free baselines the entire evidential question at these seams; fixing the licensing rule before data exists prevents post-hoc softening.

claim: F1 MEASUREMENT PROTOCOL on the colibri knobs: PIN=<per-arm file>; PIN_GB identical across pinned arms within a branch (fixed at bring-up from measured free RAM, recorded in the run manifest) — the comparison is VOID without matched budget; TOPK/TOPP untouched at config defaults (routing changes belong to B1b's own future prereg); STATS on (fingerprints + maps); DIRECT on as the PRIMARY configuration for routing arms (O_DIRECT so every miss pays NVMe) with page cache dropped at arm boundaries, DIRECT off as a separately-reported secondary realistic configuration, never pooled, and DIRECT off for F1-K (quality endpoints); PILOT off; DRAFT=0; KAE unset in all routing arms and set per the F1-K prereg in F1-K arms only; AUTOPIN/.coli_usage enabled for arm a1 only and fresh elsewhere (leakage voids affected arms). Prompt order randomised and seeded; arm order rotated; every run emits a committed manifest (binary/config/pin-file/KaE-table hashes, knob values, per-prompt counters). Knob semantics are fetch-grade until re-verified from the checkout at bring-up (ASM-1971 carried); any semantic surprise halts for a protocol amendment BEFORE data collection.
rationale: Attribution discipline: the page cache is a policy-free L2 that blurs arm differences, and AUTOPIN state leakage would contaminate the very deflator the design exists to respect.

claim: F1 ROUTING-ARM STATISTICS: statistical unit = prompt; fully paired design; primary analysis = paired one-sided permutation test on per-prompt miss-bytes/token (10,000 resamples), alpha=0.05, PLUS a pre-registered effect threshold of >=10% median relative miss-byte reduction (F1-A, each of a3-vs-a1 and a3-vs-a2 individually; PASS requires the conjunction — conservative, no multiplicity correction) or >=5% additive (F1-B.2, a2+K vs a2); N=48 fixed in advance, confidence intervals reported, no optional stopping, no post-hoc subgroups beyond pre-registered per-cluster descriptives; instrument check: engine-counter vs iostat disagreement >10% on any arm triggers investigation before analysis. F1-K statistics are registered separately (ASM-2030).
rationale: Small-N paired systems measurements need the effect-size floor and the fixed-N rule to keep a noisy pass from being manufactured by re-running.

claim: F1-B DESIGN (Branch B salvage-or-kill), strictly ordered: F1-B.1 paraphrase diagnostic (6 same-concept surface-disjoint pairs vs 6 surface-overlapping concept-distinct pairs; if paraphrase pairs do NOT share routing while surface-matched pairs do, the structure is surface-form and the kernel routing lever is killed with no further spend); else F1-B.2 additive ablation (a2 kernel-free embedding-cluster pin vs a2+K adding kernel concept labels at the same budget vs a1 AUTOPIN; kill if additive gain <5% median or p>=0.05; salvage -> scoped F1-A rerun inside the remaining ceiling). A kernel-free prefix-fingerprint pinning policy (16-token prefix, STATS harvest, pin, continue) that survives a kill is explicitly a NON-kernel engineering result, reported as such, and not pursued under this programme's ceilings without a separate maintainer decision.
rationale: Branch B is where wishful reading is most likely; the ordered diagnostic separates surface-form structure from power failure before any live spend, and the non-kernel salvage is firewalled from kernel credit.

claim: F1-C DISPOSITION (Branch C): register G-B1 FAIL; B1a AND B1b recorded dead-at-this-model-and-box; the TOPK sweep result is recorded as a colibri-native concept-free observation with no kernel sentence attached; committed traces are retained for re-analysis; F1-K's admissibility is UNTOUCHED (its gate never depended on native routing structure). The 1M-kernel scale gate does NOT rescue absent routing structure (more concepts add labels, not router signal), so deferral-to-scale is disallowed as a default; defensible re-entries (different MoE host with documented expert specialisation; materially finer conditioning signal) each require a fresh probe-grade design. Pivot menu — the choice is a MAINTAINER decision: C-i A1 gateway plumbing demonstration (zero C changes; thesis experiments remain gated per ASM-1982; <=$40), C-ii park the routing substrate after F1-K completes (terminate instance, keep S3 artifact, ~$0), C-iii B2 acceptance measurement then park (<=$20). No option moves any thesis verdict.
rationale: Pre-writing the kill branch with its non-rescue clause prevents the commonest failure mode of dead levers: indefinite deferral disguised as patience.

claim: F1 SCALE GATE (routing levers): F1's minimal kernel/map is the probe's ~24 concept-stratified clusters labelled by the existing <100-concept kernel, with WordNet sense glosses/synonym sets used ONLY to author fresh surface-disjoint renderings inside those clusters (surface variation, not new concepts; word-level/sense-level caution inherited from the sense-split track and handled by the A1/A2 sub-casing). The FULL B1a/B1b claim additionally requires a >=1M-concept sense-split kernel (SCALE-1 S2), the runtime NL->concept mapper (the l3a parse wall), and — for the one mechanism a topic model cannot replicate, conditioning on entailed-but-unstated concepts — engine closure at coverage (RULES-SCALE). Every F1 routing result is scoped verbatim to concept-labelled workloads at this model and box. KaE's scale gate is registered at ASM-2033.
rationale: Separates what is runnable now (labels, not coverage) from what the north-star claim needs, so no F1 sentence can be quoted beyond its scope.

claim: F1 ROUTING-ARM COST BANDS at i4i.2xlarge ~$0.69/h on-demand: F1-A ~27,650 decode tokens across six arms at a warm 0.09-0.25 tok/s -> 31-85 instance-hours ~= $21-59, ceiling $150; F1-B.1 ~$3-5 and F1-B.2 ~16-43h ~= $11-30, ceiling $60; F1-C <=$40 (pivot-dependent); S3 retention $8.5/mo continues throughout. Warm-throughput band is a projection until stats.tgz is read. No band is a premise or a commitment; ceilings, not estimates, bound spend. F1-K costs are registered at ASM-2032.
rationale: Prices the maintainer decision without letting an estimate masquerade as a measurement.
resolution_path: Instance receipts + the warm-throughput rows of the probe's final stats.tgz; bands replaced by measured values in the branch run report.

claim: F1 ROUTING-LEVER CLAIM CAPS AND PRE-ADOPTED WORDING (ASM-1987 carried): a Branch-A PASS licenses at most 'concept-conditioned expert pinning reduced expert I/O on concept-labelled workloads at this model and box, beyond both usage-history and topic-cluster pinning at matched RAM budget, with byte-identical outputs' — sign not slope; a Branch-B salvage licenses at most the additive-value analogue; every null takes the content-not-structure wording at equal prominence. Nothing in any F1 routing branch licenses an intelligence, efficiency-thesis, parity, or cost claim, and no F1 routing outcome moves the CORRECTNESS or EFFICIENCY synthesis verdicts, which remain INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING under their own designated experiments. KaE claim caps are registered at ASM-2033.
rationale: GLM-5.2 numbers will be quotable; the cap is registered before any exists.

claim: F1 MAINTAINER GATES: (0) the scoped Law-1 amendment + the ~150-200-line glm.c KaE patch approval, blocking F1-K only (ASM-2025/2031); (1) the single plan sign-off — F1-K primary + routing decision tree + per-experiment ceilings (K $250 / A $150 / B $60 / C $40); after approval F1-K runs on P0's landing (gate 0 permitting), the classifier fires mechanically, and each experiment runs without re-surfacing inside its ceiling; (2) any Branch-B expanded-N re-test (one maximum) is a separate spend decision; (3) the Branch-C pivot choice C-i/C-ii/C-iii; (4) carried from ASM-1989, any further glm.c C change beyond the P0 trace-dump patch and the gate-0-approved KaE patch. Each is surfaced as a threaded issue per the maintainer-decisions-as-issues practice when actionable; none is decided in this document.
rationale: Separates what the designer proposes from what only the maintainer may decide, and makes the no-pre-surface boundary explicit on both sides.

claim: LEVER C DEFINITION AND PRIMACY (maintainer scope expansion, this tick): Kernel-as-Expert (KaE) — for tokens whose context maps to a kernel-grounded concept via a training-free external gate, splice a small per-concept grounded content-carrier into GLM-5.2's MoE output, ADD (alongside native experts; pure quality) or REPLACE (in place of the lowest-weight routed expert; quality + per-token I/O saving). KaE is the PRIMARY F1 experiment, is a CORRECTNESS-thesis test at 744B scale, is the architectural descendant of f2b-transfer (+0.25 content lift through a thin interface) and DECONF-B (content-not-structure attribution), and is UNCONDITIONAL on the probe's routing outcome because its gate is external, not router-based. 'Kernel' is used loosely and the design tests grounded CONTENT, not kernel structure; the ladder of ASM-2029 keeps every claim at exactly the rung it earned. Hypothesis (stated, not asserted): on concept-covered QA, grounding corrects items GLM-5.2 misremembers; the win, if any, is correction-where-wrong, not new capability.
rationale: The maintainer's stated goal (A) needs a quality seam; anchoring it to the programme's one audited affirmative and pre-declaring the content-not-structure framing keeps the strongest lever inside the honesty discipline.

claim: KaE LAW-1 INTERACTION: house Law-1 (interface-locality — no kernel vector enters a model's activations) as stated PROHIBITS KaE's carrier, which writes into the residual stream. F1-K therefore requires an explicit, scoped, registered Law-1 amendment BEFORE any run: kernel-derived content vectors may enter activations ONLY within the KaE track, ONLY via the registered moe() splice, with the ASM-2029 deflator ladder mandatory. The maintainer's scope-expansion instruction signals intent but is not the amendment; the amendment is MAINTAINER GATE 0 and must be a registry event. Without it F1-K does not run and the remainder of F1 is unaffected.
rationale: A standing house law must be amended explicitly, not overridden by inference from a task brief; surfacing the conflict as a named gate is the only honest resolution available to a design pass.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-followup-experiment.md §2.2; docs/next/design/DDC.md §1.2 (Law-1 statement); registry ASM-1981 (prior Law-1-motivated deferral)

claim: KaE GATING + SPLICE (crux 1), all training-free, native router untouched: PRIMARY gate G-lex = harness-side lexical phrase->concept matcher (the ~24 probe concepts, trigger surface expanded by WordNet lemma/derivational sets) emitting per-position concept spans via a KAE= sidecar file; REGISTERED VARIANT G-emb = per-token hidden-state cosine against per-concept anchor vectors (means from plain forward passes) at the splice layer, threshold calibrated on dev to match G-lex firing rate, run only budget-permitting; NOTED-NOT-SCHEDULED G-route (fires on native router weight mass over concept-associated experts) admissible only after a Branch-A classification. SPLICE: inside colibri moe() (reported at glm.c:1270; fetch-grade, re-verified at bring-up) after the routed top-8 weighted sum + shared expert, before residual writeback: ADD y += g*K[c][l]; REPLACE skips the lowest-sigmoid-weight top-8 expert and adds w_dropped*K[c][l]. LAYER SET: pre-registered dev pilot over L1={one mid-stack MoE layer ~40} / L2={4 evenly spaced mid-late} / L3={all MoE layers, reduced g}, crossed with g in {0.5,1.0,2.0}x mean native expert weight; argmax dev configuration FROZEN before the main run. Known honest weakness: word-level triggers conflate senses (sense-split track defect); sense-minimal-pair items are a tagged subgroup.
rationale: The gate must not touch the router (no retraining, per programme ethos) and must be auditable; a lexical sidecar gate is deterministic and byte-replayable, with the embedding gate as the registered generalisation.

claim: KaE CARRIER (crux 2): ADOPTED = per-concept per-layer definition offsets v_{c,l}, the mean hidden-state difference at gated positions between contexts WITH the kernel explication of c prepended and matched contexts WITHOUT (m=16 construction contexts per concept, disjoint from all evaluation items; forward passes + arithmetic only, no gradients; storage ~0.6-44 MB). Deflator carriers are built by the IDENTICAL procedure with substituted prepend-content; d0 is a norm-matched random vector. REJECTED for F1-K: any trained LM/MLP expert or learned projection (training intervention outside the programme's tier). DEFERRED: decoded kernel concept vectors projected into activations (the ASM-1981/B3 objection — foreign vectors the stack was never trained to read). BRIDGE CONTROL d3-text: kernel explication prepended as prompt text, no splice — measures whether the in-routing seam adds anything over f2b-transfer's prompt seam. Steering-vector/task-arithmetic literature backs the construction shape at abstract level only (not source-verified this tick).
rationale: The offset construction is the only carrier that is simultaneously training-free, dimension-native, capacity-matchable across deflator arms, and a faithful in-activation transport of the exact content mechanism f2b-transfer already validated at the prompt boundary.

claim: KaE ADD vs REPLACE (crux 3): ADD (y += g*K[c][l], native experts intact) is the pure-quality mode and runs FIRST; REPLACE (drop lowest-weight routed expert on gated tokens, K takes its weight; quality + one fewer expert load per gated token-layer) is ADMITTED only after ADD passes ladder rung K-1. REPLACE endpoints: paired accuracy non-inferiority vs ADD (>= ADD - 2 pts) AND measured expert-byte saving > 0 on gated tokens; failing either records 'removal costs more than it saves'. OFF-CONCEPT GUARD (both modes): the gate never fires off-concept, so a 60-item off-concept subset must be byte-identical to baseline (SHA-256 over token ids); any mismatch VOIDS the F1-K run.
rationale: Removal is only worth its quality risk once the addition is shown to carry value, and the byte-identity guard makes 'off-concept harmless' a mechanical fact rather than an assumption.

claim: KaE DEFLATOR LADDER (the knull discipline, mandatory; all arms same gate/splice/layers/g, differing ONLY in carrier content, all built by the ASM-2027 construction): b0 unmodified baseline; d0 norm-matched random vector (PLACEBO — if d0 beats b0 the run is VOIDED as noise-sensitive instrumentation); d1 equal-capacity CONCEPT-AGNOSTIC content offsets (generic encyclopedic paragraphs unrelated to the gated concept) — the 'any extra content helps' arm that capped f2b/DECONF-B/RULES-2; d2 plain-dictionary definitions of the SAME concepts (the knull proper); d3-text prompt-injection bridge control; K kernel explication offsets. LICENSING LADDER, each rung at the ASM-2030 test and margin, failing rung n caps the claim at rung n-1: K-1 K>b0 ('content injection at the MoE seam lifts accuracy on concept-covered QA at this model/box'); K-2 K>d1 ('concept-ALIGNED content beats equal-capacity generic content at this seam'); K-3 K>d2 ('kernel-authored explications beat plain-dictionary definitions at this seam' — the first kernel-specific content-seam sentence the programme could earn; a K~d2 tie is the modal expectation on the four-deflation record and is reported at equal prominence). K vs d3-text is a descriptive mechanism finding, both directions pre-worded. No rung licenses an intelligence, parity, or general-capability claim.
rationale: Without the ladder a KaE lift would be unattributable among perturbation, content-in-general, concept alignment, and kernel authorship; the ladder is the entire evidential content of the experiment.

claim: KaE QA PROTOCOL: known-concept subset = MMLU items whose question+options lexically match >=1 trigger lemma of the phrase->concept map, supplemented mechanically (same filter) from a pre-registered pool (ARC-Easy/Challenge, OpenBookQA, CommonsenseQA) if MMLU alone is insufficient; HARD GATE n_min=240 pooled items or F1-K does not run and returns to the maintainer with the measured coverage shortfall; subset composition reported verbatim. Splits: dev=24 (pilot) + 60 off-concept guard; test >=216, untouched until arms freeze; construction contexts disjoint from all. Scoring: per-option loglikelihood via prefill/echo+logprobs (ASM-1988 mitigation; generative scoring unaffordable at 0.09 tok/s), argmax=answer. Statistics: unit=item, fully paired; paired one-sided permutation on per-item correctness, 10,000 resamples, alpha=0.05, effect floor >= +3 accuracy points PER LADDER RUNG; flip matrix (corrections vs regressions) = mechanism secondary; baseline-wrong subgroup DESCRIPTIVE ONLY (selection on errors regresses to the mean under any perturbation — d0 is the control); honesty-first penalised score a named secondary descriptive; fixed n, no optional stopping. CEILING-BOUND WORDING pre-adopted: if b0 subset accuracy >95%, headroom < the effect floor and the result is reported 'ceiling-bound at this subset', not a lever null.
rationale: The subset filter, minimum-n gate, paired flip analysis, and ceiling-bound wording are each required to keep a 24-concept trigger map from producing an underpowered, skewed, or headroom-artifact result that reads as a verdict.

claim: KaE COLIBRI FEASIBILITY ENVELOPE: the patch is a single-file glm.c edit kept as an in-repo diff — sidecar+offset-table loader (~60 lines), moe() splice ADD/REPLACE (~40 lines), hidden-state dump mode for carrier construction (~50 lines), optional G-emb gate (~50 lines); ~150-200 lines total in one function plus a loader, estimated one agent-day on the instance. This EXCEEDS the P0 trace-dump-only C envelope (ASM-1986/1989) and therefore rides MAINTAINER GATE 0 with the Law-1 amendment; fork-vs-upstream etiquette carried from ASM-1989 (nothing offered upstream without a separate decision). The moe() location (glm.c:1270) and router semantics (logits -> sigmoid+bias -> top-8 -> weighted sum + shared expert) are coordinator-supplied fetch-grade facts, mechanically re-verified from the checkout at bring-up before the patch is written.
rationale: Bounds the engineering honestly (single function, single file, single author upstream) and keeps the one C-surface expansion inside an explicit maintainer gate rather than scope creep.

claim: F1-K COST BANDS at i4i.2xlarge ~$0.69/h: prefill-dominated — construction 768 prefills (24 concepts x 16 contexts x 2 variants), pilot 9 configs x 24 dev items, main >=5 arms x 240-300 items x per-option prefills; prefill throughput on this box is UNKNOWN until measured at bring-up (union-of-experts loading dominates; page cache assists repeated related items); band ~$40-140; CEILING $250. Pre-registered fallback if the projected main run exceeds the ceiling: cut items to n_min=240 and defer REPLACE — never a budget overrun. No band is a premise or a commitment.
rationale: The single largest cost unknown in F1 is colibri prefill throughput; pricing it as a measured-at-bring-up quantity with a mechanical fallback keeps the ceiling honest.
resolution_path: Bring-up prefill-throughput measurement on the probe box (recorded in the F1-K run manifest) + instance receipts; bands replaced by measured values in the F1-K run report.

claim: KaE SCALE GATE + CLAIM CAPS: F1-K's minimal map = the ~24 probe concepts with WordNet-expanded trigger lemmas and the existing kernel explications as carrier content; sufficient for gating and carrier construction but possibly insufficient for benchmark coverage (the n_min=240 gate is where this bites). The FULL KaE claim ('kernel grounding corrects a frontier model where it is wrong, on open workloads') requires a >=1M-concept sense-split kernel with per-concept grounded content (SCALE-1 S2), the runtime NL->concept mapper, and sense-correct gating. CAPS: every F1-K result is scoped verbatim to 'concept-covered QA items at this model and box'; the ladder rung earned (ASM-2029) is the entire licensed sentence; F1-K feeds the CORRECTNESS ledger ONLY through verdict-gen's registered pathway, and no F1-K outcome moves the CORRECTNESS or EFFICIENCY synthesis verdicts, which remain INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING under their own designated experiments.
rationale: KaE is the lever most likely to produce a quotable frontier-scale number; its scope and thesis pathway are therefore fixed before any number exists.

claim: BUILD SCOPE AND QUARANTINE: poc/engine-inference/ + data/axioms-engineinf-v0/ + analysis/engine_inference_stdin.py + registry/experiments/engine-inference.json (DRAFT) are built to the frozen-shape of the ENGINE-INF design; the builder does not freeze, run the registered campaign, or conclude. A pre-freeze FULL mechanical execution of the deterministic harness occurred as harness validation (rules-1 certificate precedent: deterministic + pin-gated, a registered re-run reproduces the bytes for $0); its numbers are EXPLORATORY, quarantined, and may not be quoted for or against any registered hypothesis.
rationale: The instrument is a $0 CPU pure function; building it without executing it is impossible to validate. The quarantine keeps the freeze-then-run discipline meaningful: the verdict is produced only by verdict-gen over the FROZEN record's registered run.

claim: ITEM EXTRACTOR RULES (mechanical, deterministic, over pinned WN31 bytes only): (a) examples = quoted strings in synset glosses of the 13 minted + 114 excluded Stage-A synsets; (b) predicate match = longest inflected form of any synset lemma (verb.exc + minimal suffix morphology; noun-lemma concepts match the noun); (c) undergoer = head noun of the post-predicate NP (strip determiners; stop at prepositions/subordinators/pronouns; head = last WN-noun token), else the pre-predicate subject NP (intransitive/passive fallback) when it contains no pronoun; (d) pronoun-owned argument slots EXCLUDE the example (the undergoer is the referent, not a later noun); (e) every exclusion logged with a named reason; PC-5 candidate denominator = examples with a detected predicate and a non-pronoun nominal argument.
rationale: The design pre-registers the item shape but not the parse; these rules are simple enough to audit by eye, fail closed on everything else, and yielded 0.978 well-formed-unique-gold on 181 candidates (>= the 0.95 PC-5 floor).

claim: NOUN SENSE + G3 DEFINEDNESS: an argument noun is typed via its most-SemCor-tagged WN31 sense (index.sense tag_cnt; ties and zero tags fall back to sense 1), then hypernym-closed to the physical_entity/abstraction top split (G2); nouns reaching neither or both sides are excluded (logged). The G3 cross-pair anomaly rule is applied only to minted synsets whose OWN attested example-object sides are unanimous; mixed-side synsets are logged and generate no cross-pairs.
rationale: Both choices are third-party-frequency-anchored and mechanical; the unanimity restriction keeps constructed-rule gold (the design's disclosed weakest plank) away from senses where its premise is already violated.

claim: D-WORD-DOM SIGNATURE, measured: the SemCor-dominant sense is derived from the pinned index.sense (break v-00364717 'interrupt' tag 15; make v-02566500 'engage in' tag 508; find v-02253273 'come upon' tag 159; friend n-10132360 tag 169), and its word-level range = the majority G2 side of the dominant synset's own gloss-example objects (break->C_abst, make->C_abst, find->C_abst, friend->C_abst). NOTE: the design's §1.5 illustration assumed shatter-dominant break (range C_material); the measured dominant is interrupt, so the pi:011 over-derivation runs in the OPPOSITE direction (false conflicts on PHYSICAL break objects). The divergence is measured, not assumed; the seam construction is unaffected.
rationale: The strongest committed word-level dictionary must be constructed mechanically from third-party frequency, not from the design's worked example.
backing_ref: engineinf_lib.py build_dword_arms; arms/arm-manifest.json dword_dom block; design §1.3 D-word-dom row sha256:f50f557c2e53552c3587942ed81214ac58f05dbecc515ab51c9803390524fe1f

claim: DIVERGENCE SIGNATURE: Div(K,b) 'full' compares (verdict, refusal code, G2-side projection of derived undergoer classes) — decision-relevant divergence at gold granularity, not URN vocabulary trivia (K deriving C_words while B-wn derives C_wn-something is an O1 divergence only when their SIDE assertions differ). Decision-level Div (verdict+refusal only) scopes the correctness contrasts. Side projection: material/person/wn-somebody->phys; words/info/happening->abst; wn-something->none.
rationale: Arms speak different class vocabularies by construction; comparing raw derived URNs would make Div vacuously large and un-killable (KILL-e2 could never fire), destroying the anti-knull gate's meaning.
backing_ref: design §2.3 divergence certificate; engineinf_lib.py row_signature/divergence; analysis/engine_inference_stdin.py

claim: B-WN FRAME TYPING: a synset's object slot = the token immediately after the verb slot in its WN sentence-frame templates (verb.Framestext), counted only when literally 'somebody'/'something'; somebody-only -> range C_wn-somebody, something-only -> C_wn-something, mixed/none -> untyped; C_wn-somebody disjointWith C_wn-something is declared as the frame vocabulary's own two-way slot split. Nouns are wn-kind-typed by whether their hypernym closure reaches person (n-00007846).
rationale: This is the strongest typing constructible from WN bytes with zero kernel authoring (the ASM-1956 anti-strawman requirement); the disjointness stipulation gives B-wn real discriminative teeth (it false-flags and true-flags — measured non-degenerate).
backing_ref: design §1.3 B-wn row + risk §6.2(2); engineinf_lib.py load_frame_object_slots/build_bwn_arm

claim: K-SHUF DERANGEMENT: domain/range constraint sets rotate by 1 over URN-sorted senses within each lemma (deterministic; subPropertyOf/classDeclaration bulk untouched). DISCLOSED LIMIT: 3 of break's 5 senses share range C_material, so part of the typing assignment is permutation-invariant within lemma — the control destroys all typing content that IS destroyable by within-lemma permutation.
rationale: The design mandates derangement 'across senses within lemma' (§1.3/§4.1); invariant material->material fixed points are a property of the kernel's own typing distribution, not of the shuffle.

claim: G4 SCORING RULE: on excluded-sense (refusal-gold) cells, REFUSE is correct AND vacuous CONSISTENT (no conflict asserted) is correct; ANOMALOUS — a confident category-error assertion against attested third-party usage — is incorrect. Honesty-weighted secondary: correct 0; refusal where gold != REFUSE 0.5; any other wrong 1.0 (penalty form fixed at freeze per design §2.2).
rationale: Scoring bare REFUSE as the only correct answer would gift K wins purely for coverage gaps (rigged-for-K); scoring CONSISTENT as wrong would punish broader-coverage arms for covering more. This rule punishes exactly what the design's W3 example punishes: the confident wrong assertion.

claim: WORLD ENCODING: each item compiles to rel(anchor, R_arm, undergoer) + stated cls(undergoer, side-class) + cls(undergoer, wn-kind-class); intransitive/passive occurrences put the subject NP in the undergoer slot; the anchor entity carries no class facts (no domain-side scoring at E0). The world compiler is shared byte-identically by all arms; only the relation URN is substituted per arm.
rationale: The certified engine types via domain/range over rel facts; a uniform undergoer slot keeps all arms' exposure identical and keeps the compiler trivially auditable.
backing_ref: design §1.1 (precomputed cls facts) + §1.4 item shape; engineinf_lib.py neutral_world

claim: PRIMARY FRAME: the registered contrasts are computed on decision-level Div(K,b) RESTRICTED to G1/G3 cells (attested + constructed-anomaly gold); G4 refusal cells feed KILL-e1's full-divergence count and the honesty-weighted secondary ONLY. KILL-e2 threshold applies to the restricted frame |Div_dec(K,B-wn) ∩ (G1∪G3)| < 10.
rationale: G4 cells are verdict-divergent for every non-kernel arm by construction (K refuses 156/212 cells where baselines answer); leaving them in the primary frame would dilute the delta denominator with cells where both arms score correct under ASM-1997 — the contrast would be INCONCLUSIVE by construction in either direction. The restriction keeps the primary question exactly the design's: whose CLOSURE is correct where closures diverge and gold is decisive.

claim: DELTA LOWER BOUND: the 'exact-binomial LB95 > 0' clause is implemented as delta_lb = (2*CP_LB(wins, m; one-sided alpha=0.05) - 1) * m / n_pri over the discordant pairs m = wins + losses on the primary frame (0 when m = 0) — a conservative bound conditional on the discordant count. Wilson-LB95 uses z = 1.959963984540054 (certificate precedent).
rationale: Deterministic outcomes make the per-item contrast a paired 0/1 census; the CP-on-discordant-pairs form is the exact-binomial reading of the design's clause with no distributional assumptions.

claim: ITEM CENSUS AT BUILD (resolves ASM-1964's estimate): 212 items over 4 lemmas — 21 attested (G1+G2), 35 constructed cross-pair anomalies (G3), 156 excluded-sense refusal cells (G4); extractor yield 0.978 on 181 candidate occurrences (35 logged exclusions incl. pronoun-argument and side-undetermined); oracle arm 212/212 through the pinned scorer; full 5-arm + oracle double run in ~2 s CPU at $0.
rationale: The design's 60-120 estimate is superseded by the extractor's first run, as pre-registered ('resolved by the extractor's first run').

claim: BLOCKING PILOT RESULT (instrument-validity only, never campaign evidence): PILOT-PASS-WITH-FLAGS on the 17-item deterministic slice (2 per lemma x kind), mode REAL, $0 — PC-1 pass (all baselines strictly between 0 and 1; refusal caps met); PC-2 pass: |Div(K,D-word-dom)|=16 >= 6 and |Div(K,B-wn)|=14 >= 3 spanning 3 closure ops and >= 2 lemmas (the non-vacuous-divergence existence proof, the anti-knull gate); PC-3 pass: K-shuf differs from K on 5/9 typing-active divergent cells with the predicted signature; D-union not row-identical to D-dom; PC-4 pass: planted range flip changed exactly the predicted cells and nothing else, poisoned-gold canary silent on the real compiler and TRIPPED by the planted gold-reading compiler, kernel-module provenance G2-independent; PC-5 pass: yield 0.978 >= 0.95, oracle 1.0, double-run sha match, B-wn frames available. FLAG: kernel-as-text structurally N/A (no prompt surface exists).
rationale: The design's §5 pilot instantiation, executed pre-freeze per protocol ASM-1830/1831; the flag downgrade to PASS-WITH-FLAGS is the protocol-mandated best verdict for this instrument.

claim: PC-1 OPERATIONALISATION: the degenerate-arm gate is the WHOLE-SLICE floor (0 < baseline score < 1 per baseline; refusal rate <= 0.5 on non-G4 cells for every arm), with per-baseline divergent-cell scores additionally REPORTED as evidence. D-word-union scoring 0 on its own divergent cells is the structural vacuity of the vacuous comparator (divergence with an untyped arm exists only where K acts), not instrument degeneracy.
rationale: A literal divergent-cell-only floor would fail every experiment containing a deliberately vacuous control arm; the design's PC-1 intent is contrast headroom, which the whole-slice floor tests.
backing_ref: design §5 PC-1 row; instr_pilot.py PC-1 block

claim: PC-3 DENOMINATOR: the K-shuf-differs fraction is measured over TYPING-ACTIVE divergent cells (K non-refusing). On refusal cells K-shuf refuses identically to K by construction (the shuffle deranges typing content, not sense coverage), so such cells are shuffle-invariant and cannot discriminate any control.
rationale: Without this restriction the pilot measured 5/16 = 0.31 purely because 7 slice divergent cells were refusal cells — a denominator artifact, not a weak control; on typing-active cells the measured fraction is 5/9 = 0.56.
backing_ref: instr_pilot.py PC-3 block; first pilot iteration (PILOT-FAIL) vs second (PASS-WITH-FLAGS)

claim: PC-4 PLANT SELECTION: the planted mistyped axiom targets the FIRST slice-present K-typed sense in item-id order (measured: break.malfunction, range C_material -> C_words), guaranteeing the plant is exercised by the slice; the predicted-changed set is exactly the slice items mapping to that sense.
rationale: A fixed plant target (the design's illustrative break.violate) can fall outside a deterministic slice, making the teeth untestable — the first pilot iteration failed exactly this way; slice-aware selection keeps the CF-2-style predicate exact.
backing_ref: instr_pilot.py PC-4 block; design §5 PC-4 row

claim: CLASSIFIER RECONCILIATION WITH ROUTING-ANALYSIS-V2 (evidence registered at ASM-2042): (1) the v2 10k-random-shuffle computation IS the registered p_perm quantity of ASM-2013 executed correctly; the v1 cyclic-shift run is VOID (non-exchangeable null, instrument defect), superseded not averaged; p_perm leg of Branch A is measured SATISFIED (p=0.0001 raw AND mean-centered; branch outcome invariant to metric choice, so no threshold amendment is needed or made). (2) The classifier does NOT fire: M_kernel (kernel-concept pin vs the STRONGER of global-hot and embedding-cluster deflators, P0.3 replay miss-bytes — the kernel-SPECIFICITY leg) and M_oracle remain OPEN and unread; strong concept structure in routing does not establish that kernel labelling exploits it beyond a generic embedding cluster; branch selection waits on the replay unchanged. (3) SensePairSep is computed under its frozen >=0.75 rule when the classifier fires; v2 sense-pair numbers (break p=0.0029; bank p=0.1026) are descriptive direction only. (4) F1-K is unmoved (unconditional by construction); G-route stays noted-not-scheduled. (5) Blindness consequence declared: REVISION-1 is written AFTER reading fingerprint-level structure; it therefore changes NO branch threshold, branch condition, endpoint direction, or ladder margin — only review-demanded methodology; new fingerprint-similarity metrics whose data does not yet exist (F1-B.1 overlap) use mean-centered cosine primary / raw secondary on instrument grounds (93.9% universal mass compresses raw cosine).
rationale: The corrected evidence resolves one conjunct of Branch A while the deflator conjunct stays open; registering exactly this split — plus the broken-blindness declaration and the rule that the revision moves no thresholds — is what keeps the pre-registration's legitimacy auditable after the coordinator's verified re-analysis was read.
backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-followup-experiment.md §R0; poc/glm52-probe/results/routing-analysis-v2.json (ASM-2042); registry ASM-2013 (frozen classifier); poc/glm52-probe/interpretation-fable.md (v1 defect provenance)

claim: F1-K FROZEN CANDIDATE-INDEPENDENT SCORING (supersedes the per-option loglikelihood clause of ASM-2030): one fixed template per item (instruction header + question + options as labelled lines in PUBLISHED order + fixed answer cue), identical bytes across every arm and pass, hashed in the freeze manifest, containing no concept triggers (mechanically verified at freeze); ONE prefill per item per arm; answer = argmax over next-token logprobs of the k single-token answer-LABEL tokens at the answer position (single-token property verified at freeze); deterministic tie-break lowest label index, logged. Gate spans are computed ONCE per item on the frozen full template, frozen in the sidecar, identical across arms/passes by construction; spans inside option text apply identically in all arms; label tokens are never triggers. Predicted-label vs gold-label distribution reported per arm as a descriptive position-bias check. This removes distractor/length/option-dependent-gating bias structurally and cuts scoring cost from k prefills to 1 per item-arm.
rationale: Prefill-likelihood scoring is valid only when the scored continuation set is candidate-independent and the gate cannot interact with candidate text differentially; a frozen label-token readout achieves both by construction rather than by correction.

claim: F1-K DOSE-EXACT DEFLATOR d1-drng (supersedes the generic-encyclopedic d1 clause of ASM-2029): the generic-paragraph d1 arm is REMOVED as confounded (matches dimensionality, not dose — content length, norm profile, covariance, construction provenance uncontrolled). Replacement: R=5 independent seeded DERANGEMENTS of the concept->carrier assignment over the IDENTICAL K carrier set (no concept keeps its own carrier; seeds enumerated in the freeze manifest, disjoint from the 2 pilot-reserved derangement seeds), with layerwise norm matching: deranged carriers take direction from the donor concept and norm from the recipient — rescaled per (concept, layer) to ||v^K_{c,l}||. Norm rule extended to ALL arms: d0 and d2 rescaled per (c,l) to the same reference; g applied post-rescale; raw and rescaled norms logged. Rung K-2 becomes K vs d1-drng: primary = cluster-level paired test (ASM-2038) of K vs per-item mean correctness over the R passes, alpha=0.05, +3-pt floor; secondary descriptive = K's exact rank among the R passes (best-of-6 nominal 0.167, stated so the rank cannot be over-read). Licensed K-2 wording: 'the TRUE concept->carrier alignment beats label-deranged assignments of the identical carrier set at identical dose at this seam.' d0/d2/d3-text retained unchanged in role.
rationale: Derangements of the same carrier set are the only control in which literally everything about the intervention is held fixed except whether the label->carrier mapping is the true kernel one — the knull 'kernel content, not any content' bar made dose-exact, as the review demanded.

claim: REPLACE CONFIDENCE-BOUND NON-INFERIORITY (supersedes the point-estimate 'accuracy >= ADD - 2 pts' clause of ASM-2028): REPLACE (still admitted only after ADD passes rung K-1) PASSES non-inferiority iff the lower bound of the one-sided 95% CI on the paired accuracy difference REPLACE minus ADD — cluster-level BCa bootstrap over concept clusters, 10,000 resamples, consistent with the ASM-2038 primary analysis — exceeds -2 accuracy points (Delta_NI = 2 pts, pre-registered), AND measured expert-byte saving on gated tokens > 0. A point estimate above -2 with a CI crossing -2 is a FAIL, pre-worded 'not shown non-inferior'. REPLACE NI is powered by the ASM-2038 formula with its own dev-measured discordance; if unaffordable at the power-required n it is DEFERRED per the ASM-2041 degradation order, never run underpowered.
rationale: A point estimate cannot license a non-inferiority sentence; only a confidence bound at a pre-registered margin can, and an underpowered NI test would pass vacuously — hence the defer-not-degrade rule.

claim: CLUSTER-AWARE POWER AND SAMPLE-SIZE DERIVATION (supersedes the n_min=240/item-level-statistics clauses of ASM-2030, the N=48 clauses of ASM-2014/ASM-2017, and the 6+6 clause of ASM-2018): (1) PRIMARY ANALYSIS everywhere = per-concept-cluster mean of paired per-item (or per-prompt) differences, one-sided cluster-level sign-flip permutation, 10,000 resamples, alpha=0.05, cluster-level BCa 95% CI reported for every primary contrast; effect floors unchanged (+3 pts F1-K rungs; >=10% median relative F1-A; >=5% additive F1-B.2). (2) F1-K: SE(mean paired lift) ~ sqrt((rho*delta + delta/m)/C); design target SE <= 1.2 points == >=80% power at alpha=0.05 one-sided for a true +3-pt effect (and ~80% joint power for the dual criterion at true +4); required C >= (rho*delta + delta/m)/0.012^2; planning point (delta=0.10, rho=0.03, m=15) C>=67, n~1005; band over stated assumptions (delta in [0.05,0.15], rho in [0,0.05]) n~360-1440 (the high corner exceeds the kernel's concept count and resolves ~+3.4 pts at C=96, reported as such), HARD CAP n_max=1440; >=216 items confirmed underpowered for +3 pts. Trigger map EXPANDED to all kernel concepts with registered explications (target C in [48,96]; at C=48 detectable effect ~+3.5 pts, reported as such). POWER GATE: if the mechanical filter cannot reach C>=48 with m>=8 within n_max, F1-K does not run and returns to the maintainer (subsumes the old n_min=240 gate). Dev split expanded to 96 items; inputs frozen before the test run as delta-hat at its one-sided 80% upper bound and rho-hat floored at 0.02; n_required and inputs are freeze-manifest entries; no fallback ever cuts n below n_required. (3) F1-A/F1-B.2: N_required = ceil(DEFF*(1.645+0.842)^2*(sigma_d/Delta)^2) from MEASURED P0.3 replay variance, DEFF = 1+(m-bar - 1)*rho-hat over concept clusters; N in [48,96] (48 = stratification floor, 96 = affordability cap); N_required>96 -> power-gate return to the maintainer instead of running. (4) F1-B.1: 16+16 pairs, one-sided two-sample permutation on per-pair routing overlap (mean-centered fingerprint cosine primary per ASM-2034(5)), alpha=0.05, powered >=80% for d>=1.0 (n_per_group ~ 2*(1.645+0.842)^2/d^2 ~ 13 < 16); the diagnostic detects only the GROSS surface-form signature — smaller effects fall through to F1-B.2 by design, pre-worded so a pass-through is not evidence of absence.
rationale: Items and prompts cluster by concept (shared carriers correlate treatment effects), so item-level tests overstate information and 216 items cannot resolve +3 points; with cluster count bounded by the kernel's concept count, the only honest levers are more clusters, cluster-level inference, dev-measured variance inputs, and explicit power gates that return rather than run underpowered.

claim: F1-K PRE-RUN FREEZE-GATE: maintainer sign-off (GATE 1) approves the RULES; before any F1-K spend the coordinator commits a freeze manifest instantiating them mechanically: (1) scoring template bytes + sha256, label token ids with single-token verification, tie-break and option-order rules; (2) trigger-map version hash, gate precedence rules, per-item frozen span sidecar hash; (3) carrier tables — construction-context list hash, raw + rescaled norms per arm, reference-norm rule; (4) enumerated derangement seeds (2 pilot + 5 main); (5) carrier-blind tuning record — panel composition, S(L,g) table, frozen (L,g); (6) power record — delta-hat_U, rho-hat_floor, achieved C/m, n_required, SE target, cost projection vs ceiling, REPLACE NI margin + CI method; (7) bring-up measurements — measured s/prefill, affordability check, any invoked degradation steps in the pre-registered order; (8) off-concept guard set and dev/test item id list hashes. Items 1-4 freeze before the pilot, 5 at pilot completion, 6 before the test run; the test set stays untouched until all eight entries are committed; any post-freeze deviation is a logged protocol amendment BEFORE further data collection, never silent. F1-K may not start without the manifest.
rationale: The review's fifth blocker is that sign-off without a mechanical freeze leaves every remediated degree of freedom re-openable at run time; a committed manifest converts the rules into checkable bytes and makes deviation auditable.

claim: CARRIER-BLIND TUNING (supersedes the argmax-on-K layer/g selection clause of ASM-2026): the pilot grid (3 layer sets x 3 g values, 48-item stratified dev subset) is evaluated over an UNLABELED CARRIER PANEL of three tables — the true-K assignment plus 2 pilot-reserved seeded derangements of it (seeds disjoint from the main-run derangements); selection statistic S(L,g) = unweighted mean dev accuracy across the three panel members with the panel unlabeled in the selection code, so S is invariant to which member is the true mapping and cannot favour the true carrier; tie-break = fewer spliced layers then lower g; the frozen (L,g) is applied IDENTICALLY to every arm (K, all R derangements, d0, d2; d3-text has no splice). Panel composition, seeds, S table, and per-member accuracies (unblinded only after the freeze) are manifest entries.
